review_full: Totally in love with the Auro of the place, really beautiful and quite fancy at the same time. The ambience is very pure and gives a sense of positivity throughout. Outdoor and indoor interior are quite quaint and cute. Love the open kitchen idea and there whole marketplace ideology. Due to coronovirus they specifically use disposable cutlery to keep the pandemic in mind taking all the precautionary measures from the beginning of the place with the mask on their staff and using good sanitisation. The food is really amazing specially the pizza straight from the oven and the hummus and pita bread are quite delicious too. If you're looking for a classy yet soothing Italian place in Delhi,Fatjar is a go to for you!
I went this bar 8 days regularly with my husband.. we are fully satisfied by the services. Staff is better (vitoni and amen) they both served us daily . Will surely visit again. Highly recommended ❣️
We were few friends and was a birthday celebration. The food was so good in taste and it was really fresh. We all loved it and highly recomment.

Fatjar Cafe and Market is the perfect place for a casual lunch with your loved ones. The Ambience is delightful and the food is totally the best thing about the place! YOu can purchase your choosing of Olives and a lot more directly from the place. They are taking all the prevention measures for the spread of COVID-19 and I can say this without hesitation that it is by far the most safest place felt after looking at their actions ✨

Hey Guys, if you are craving for pizza and searching for it then you should visit this cafe. Yes, I highly recommend you to visit this FatJar Cafe because the cafe offers scrumptious pizza. I have ordered Veggie Pizza which was really awesome. Apart from that, I have also ordered Cappuccino and one soft drink. If I talk about the ambiance then no doubt, because it is absolutely pretty. From my side I'll give 5/5 for this cafe. It is completely amazing. I had a great experience with this cafe. Here are some pictures have a look

We were looking for a special meal and we found it at the FatJar . Well presented and tasty food , whilst it is more expensive than we usually pay , it was well worth the experience. I had Chicken with roast vegetables, the chicken was tender and tasty and the vegetables tasted like vegetables, my husband ate a pasta dish , which by Indian standards was very good.  If you are looking for a holiday from Indian food and don’t mind the small extra expense then treat yourselves to this restaurant. They also have a Range of wine and beer.

Kailash colony is brimming with small cafes now. I happened to visit this beauty for lunch and was amazed with the 1st impression it made.  We started with Falafel & Pita and Margherita Pizza. Safe to say, Margherita pizza was the best choice of the day. Its made freshly in front of you in the Wood Fired oven. The taste was exquisite and I would recommend this to anybody who plans to visit this outlet. I could come here just to have that Pizza. Hummus, Pita and Falafel were a good side dish while we ordered the next couple of dishes. We moved on to order Fussili Alfredo. This white sauce fussili pasta was good but could not be compared to the pizza we had earlier.  In the end, we went for Frozen Yoghurt with Honey. It is recommended to have it with honey, which will do wonders to the frozen yoghurt!  This place turned out to be quite peaceful and the plants enhanced its beauty.

If you are looking for a varied selection of delicious, high quality dishes, this is your place. We spent three weeks traveling around India, and our best meals of the trip were unquestionably found here. Great place if you are tiring of the typical Indian dishes and are lusting for some ethnic variety. Oh, and do yourself a favor: You MUST get the Sticky Date Pudding with Vanilla Bean Ice Cream. It may be the best dessert we have ever had.

Kailash Colony is the place I always head to when I’m not in the mood for GK & Nehru Place’s hustle bustle. This time around we planned to visit Fatjar Café and Market which is right on the road and was very easy to find.   Fatjar’s ambience is very pleasing to the eyes and the light colours used makes it look very airy. The use of plants further makes this place look more beautiful. Most of the cooking is done in front of you with a semi open kitchen and their food fire oven looks amazing too. The products on display establishes the word “market” in the name perfectly. The varied seating options are very comfortable and a small outdoor seating is great for sunny winter lunches. The parking though is limited in front of the café.   We started our meal with some “Falafel & Pita” which had some moist falafels which were nicely fried and not at all oily. The pita breads were very fresh and so was the hummus. You could literally tell that that just after your first bite. The consistency of the hummus was noteworthy too. Next we ordered some humble “Truffle Fries” which were very crispy and a pinch of truffle to top them made them taste even better.   For the main course, we ordered a “Fussili Alfredo” which was very creamy and the pasta was cooked to perfection. The only thing they need to look at is that usually the pasta is supposed to be served along with a bread which they don’t. The highlight of our meal was simply their “Margherita Pizza” which had a brilliant base made out of the freshest dough you could think of. The cheese used to top the pizza looked very premium and the balance between the sauces and the cheese was tremendous too. The presentation deserves a special mention as it wasn’t the usual pizza stand they serve their pizza on but is more a raised steel stand which again was a nice touch.   To accompany our meal, we ordered a “Berries Shakes” which was very thick and was prepared using real berries instead of the preservatives filled syrups. Though the sweetness was a bit too low for our liking and a little more sweetness would have made that shake perfect.   We ended our meal with a “Frozen Yoghurt with Honey” which had two scoops of frozen yoghurt which had some ice crystals too and once you top it up with honey, the combination really does wonders to the taste.   The service was top notch and the stewards were very considerate about what we were ordering and didn’t shy away from recommending their favourites and also explaining the dishes on the menu. Overall, Fatjar simply amazed us with its varied food options on the menu and its serene ambience. The place looked fantastic and is perfect for a romantic date or co working or if you are looking forward to a quiet and a hearty meal.

Perch calls itself a ‘wine and coffee bar’ but it’s also a very good place for food. This restaurant stretches across two levels: on the first floor (only stairs, no lift, as is the norm in Khan Market), and on the floor above. My husband and I came here for lunch, and found that all the ‘regular tables’ (read: with normal chairs) were occupied, so all that was left were the high tables with bar stools, on which you perch. We settled in at the table in the centre of the restaurant, which seems to function partly as a sort of waiting area—you can sit here with your glass of wine or whatever until they find another table for you.  However, we were quite comfortable at this table (and we had it to ourselves), so even hen we were offered a table upstairs, we opted to keep sitting here. Almost all the tables around us were occupied by people at work: several business meetings were going on, and a couple of people were working in laptops as well.  The menu at Perch is not a huge one. The wine and coffee lists (which also include some soft drinks, cocktails, etc) are part of the menu, plus there are small plates, large plates, and desserts. The food is international.  We ordered the following dishes: Mushroom croquettes: Five to a portion, these were pretty large, the outside a crisp shell, the inside almost meltingly soft. Delicious enough on their own, without needing the dip that accompanied them.  Pork Scotch egg: A huge Scotch egg, wrapped around with minced pork, crumbed and fried. I was very impressed with this—the pork (which was delicious) was perfectly cooked, the outside crumb was crunchy and golden, and yet the egg yolk was oozy. This came with a little heap of spiralized carrots, and an emulsion of grapes and mustard, of which I’d have liked a bit more, it was so yummy. A great dish. Cherry whisky cheesecake with plum pudding ice cream: The dish’s name on the menu says it all. The plum pudding ice cream could have done with some dried fruit (I got bits of crumbled pudding, and the flavour of the spice and alcohol was excellent, though), and I do wish there had been more cherries inside the cheesecake, not just as a garnish. This was a good dessert, but its description gave the impression of more fruit than there was in the dish—and me being a lover of fruity desserts, I was a bit disappointed.  We finished with a cup of flat white each, with came with its own little accompaniments: a tiny slice of what seemed like banana bread, and a tiny biscuit. The coffee was excellent.  In all, we paid Rs 2,529 for our meal, taxed and service charges included.

The Chinese I had in vegetarian items was so perfect for me as was recommended by my friend. Also, we were served by Sagar and he had given good service and hospitality. I would definitely prefer to visit the place again.

This is a very popular restaurant for enjoying a drink or coffee with your friends. You can relax and enjoy in this place with tasty and delicious snacks. Service is as per your convenience.Governor Amolak Rattan Kohli
The service was OK and the food seemed good. Except that my wife ate scallops and got a gastroenteritis.

The restaurant is located in the posh Khan Market and offers ample seating space. The natural light streaming into the restaurant gives the place a lively feel. For drinks, based on my friend's recommendation, we had the coffee sangria (highly recommended). It is served chilled and you may want it to reach room temperature for more flavor to kick in! Unfortunately, the food was disappointing. I had the mushroom duxelle pappardelle (not recommended), which was overcooked and super high on black pepper. The dessert, however, was the highlight - Cherry and Whiskey cheesecake (highly recommended). The ambiance is relaxed, however, it may get noisy when full as the table tend to be close to each other. Service can be slow (ours picked up towards the end).

My best friend had come from USA and wanted to visit place that serves finest coffee:) so we came to the perch. And I tried flat white for the first time being told to how it’s different from cappuccino:) the food is good and yes it certainly is a luxury heavy place. Not pocket friendly but totally worth the money spend.
Nicely fitted out Cafe in Khan Market, with good coffee and nice selection of food options. Great place to chill away from the bustling streets of Delhi!

A friend from Delhi introduced me to this place, which I understand is fairly new and definitely new to me despite several visits to Delhi in the last few years. It’s very stylish inside, has a great wine and coffee menu, and is a good social café as well as a good place to ‘perch’ by the window and either watch people coming and going in Khan Market (it’s upstairs) or work away on a laptop with a great view. Would definitely visit again.

We had a reservation and they didn’t even have that noted. We got a small two people table for 3. The service was slow and bit chaotic - that too for a Monday evening.  Not client friendly. The menu seemed rather average. Wine choice was ok. The food not very interesting.

Spend an evening with a dear friend and had a great time. Not only did they serve excellent wine, but their main course was awesome too. This was my 3rd visit to this place and I really look forward to dining here during my visit to delhi.
Good coffee, nibbles and bites. Amazing selection of wines. Very few places in Delhi have this open bright feel. The crowd not unruly and loud like in other cafes. It's been my favorite hangout now.

There are a lot of places that have come and gone in the bustle of Khan market, but this little spot has been a constant fav of mine. The food, the people and the drinks are to die for. If you are looking for hard liquor, you may not find what you want here, but trust me, you want to try their drinks.  If you are a coffee fan try the Vietnamese coffee - I prefer iced. It is the best version outside Vietnam! If you like your coffee dark and strong then this is not for you.  Their gnocchi is one of the best available in Delhi. The Kasava chips are also nice.  The best time to visit is in the afternoon, you can have coffee and read a book or work watching the sunset from the upper floors. That would be a sign that it is time to drink so you can also unwind all the while with great food. It does get very busy so don't hate on the crowds.
Consistently good. Fantastic atmosphere, pretty terrace and sangria to die for. Perch is a little treasure in Khan market.
The food was super and nicely served! They have many wine options, even a Spanish Tempranillo (my favourite) and good coffee!! The atmosphere is so nice, I would definitely recommend this place!
This place has the true European ambience , lively place with great food, boiled bean salad and artichoke is fabulous, staff is professional. Oh not to forget the Pinot Girogio !

A killer selection of cocktails, nice wines (including both new/old world) and some great tapas. Went with a girlfriend and we shared four delicious small plates between us. Lots of interesting ingredients that I haven't seen often at other restaurants here: harissa, houloumi, etc. A very cool spot and a great option if you're travelling and are craving something a little different that the more common flavours you're exposed to in India. The staff is polite and professional, the washrooms are clean, and the decor is simple but cool.

One of the best new places I've been to in Delhi. I tried the chicken and garlic mousse as a starter, and then the Belgian pork belly. Both were amazing. The double chocolate mousse I picked for dessert was good too. I went for a working lunch, so didn't try any of their cocktails and drinks.

Definitely this restaurant has been perfectly able to perch itself above the ordinary and into the elite.the place is not only perched on the first floor with a good view of the happening street down but the menu is also perched two notches above the overcrowded restobar khan market.i am sure none offer traditional cassava/tapioca fries in delhi though selected places give tapioca chips.good place to sip your favourite wine with amazing choice of salads and titbits and conduct your business meeting as well. Great service,pleasantly non imposing interiors.ask for a place by the bay window to see the world go by while you perch in heaven.

I walked into this place after having snacks at Big Chill. The big window facing the street seemed inviting! Once you walk in, you see an artsy place with classy people having conversations over wine or coffee! Even though the place is small, the service is great and the finger foods and salads are very interesting. The owner stopped by to say hi and checked on us to make sure we were having a good experience The collection of wines is amazing!

Delicious take on my favorite Moscow Mule.Very nice and simple deco (love the plants in pots univers situation)Had bread with tomato chutney and cheddar followed by ca;Jamari and prawns salad.Really had a great time here.
Spend an evening with a dear friend and had a great time. Not only did they serve excellent wine, but their main course was awesome too
the ambience was great and the service was also great .  their coffee and gnochi was also really good.

Fantastic place to have good time friends. The menu offers excellent choice of drinks and food. A great place to celebrate gatherings. I would highly recommend the place because of the wonderful team and the choice of selection on the menu.

We came across Perch by accident and had dinner there on two separate occasions, having enjoyed it so much the first time round. There is no doubt that it has a strong European emphasis, although results in fantastic, modern food and attentive service that was amongst the best that we've experienced here.  The Indian Sauvignon Blanc was very acceptable, rather than the more expensive European alternatives, and there was a good range of cocktails, mocktails and beers. Decor was stylish but simple, ambience was good with lively, tasteful music to suit the meal. All food we ordered was good, and we've taken a menu home to replicate some of our favourite dishes. Have two appetisers or one main each, a couple of puddings are good to share on a small table.  Half carafe of wine, half of Sangria, two beers, five appetisers and two puddings including taxes and service about £75.

If you have not been to Perch already, you are missing out. Seriously. It is the only wine and coffee bar in the city and it lives up to both coffee and wine standards. Coupled with a relaxed ambience with herbs planted on one side and wine bottles on the other, it is the perfect place to Perch yourself on a Delhi afternoon. Must try their Vietnamese coffee and Coffee Sangria.
Rahul is doing awsome service thanks.. he has helped us with food selection and drinks... awesomeplace..

We walked in a little early for dinner so decided to have a few drinks before dinner - thankfully it was happy hour! So we ordered a few cocktails - and they do not skimp on the amount of alcohol in these drinks - yowza! Now usually I like to feel like there isn't too much alcohol in a mai tai but holey moley mine was packed with rum!! I'm not sure I really enjoyed it. Regardless - the food was delicious! The lamb chops are spicy and delicious and the thai green curry is incredibly flavorful. Service is timely and friendly which is great and not easy to come by in India. While this isn't the cheapest restaurant - the experience is well worth the money you pay.

Literally perched atop the bustling alleyway of the Khsn Market this gem of a restaurant is well worth a visit. By 7 it was full and buzzing. The menu is an wonderful alternative to the (albeit excellent) butter chicken, with a really imaginative selection of vegetarian and non-veggie starters, small plates and main dishes. The green apple salad and the ceviche were superb. A cool place in a busy part of town.

In Khan Market which is frequented by foreign tourists and locals, this bar is very popular and sevens drinks and lovely snacks . You can have good time with your friends .Governor Amolak Rattan Kohli
Casual lounge with a bohemian interior. Charming little cafe and wine bar, love the quirkiness. One of the better places in Delhi to get a good coffee. Service is fast.

Just love the food and coffee there. I have tried vietnamese coffee - warm and iced, and prefer iced. Their gnocchi is one of the best available in delhi. The kasava chips ate also nice. But the eggplant parmigiana was average. And well their house wine is also good quality. Over all a place i like visiting because the ambience, food and hospitality is nice, just that it has become a little too loud for a peaceful conversation, but thats because the place is b coming more popular by the day.
They maintained everything according to the market. Coffee is awesome. Staff is very friendly. Will rate 4.9 out of 5.
Great food, lovely ambiance. Visited Perch last week with a couple of business colleagues the food was brilliant all enjoy our dishes. Recommend to all.

With a lovely ambience in the midst of Khan Market, Perch is a perfect spot for a glass of wine or coffee. They have a great selection of cocktails, particularly the Elder Tree, that is a personal favourite. Perch also offers great food. Their cold cuts platter is delicious when paired with a glass of red. The potato and garlic gnocchi and the jerk chicken salad is also very good. Do try the pan tossed shrimp and calamari. There is a great selection of salads as well. All in all, Perch is a lovely place, and I would highly recommend it.

Expensive but great coffees. My friend and I ordered two coffees, pasta and a sandwich. The bill came down to about Rs 2000. Food was okay but really good coffees especially the Vietnamese one. Would love to go again but just for coffee.

Thanks to years of NZ/Australian coffee our coffee standards are pretty high but wow Perch certainly hit the spot. Fantastic coffee! Food was a bit on the pricier side but would come back in a heartbeat for another latte. Lovely space too very relaxing.
at perch the service is great and the ambience is also really nice. they have great food and coffee .

Perch is the kind of place in the busy Khan Market where you can actually have a quiet conversation, even though it is one of the more popular spots. It's sophisticated, pleasing music and usually frequented by regulars. The wine list, needless to say, is extensive, and they have a number of interesting wine-based cocktails as well. Of course it's not cheap, but for the vibes, service and food and drinks that it offers, I would say it is money well-spent once in a while.
Perch has a beautiful ambience, offers tasty food and great service. Highly recommend the double chocolate parfait dessert.
The ambiance of the hotel was fantastic. Mr. Abhishek Kumar provided us with a excellent and great service.

I truly reckon the perfectness in terms of ambience, staff n food. Worth mentioning that Grappa has been the best place to dine out and is still maintaining its charm for years together. Vivek and Topesh are the assets they have. Looks like a great teamwork.
Amazing place for quality time with friends.Nice drinks.. gr8 ambience..Had so much fun.Staff is fully professional.
ambience was great and Fantastic service by bartenders. You should especially try grappa negroni here

I went with friends....the amazing part of my night is the cocktails of grappa ...it's out of the world...the manager mr.topesh is amazing...the way he is approaching his guest like we are GodThankyou so much for  great hospitalityLove this place

Visited Grappa with friends based on a recommendation and we could not be more pleased ,apart from the obvious amazing cocktails We loved the decor and the upbeat friendly vibe of this place, would recommend anyone who is looking for a cool place yo hang out with friends/ couple, if you get a chance do see Topesh who manages the place and is a connoisseur of good cocktails, thanks to the team and Topesh for showing is a good time, we will be back for sure!!!!

The place has class to it. We were here for evening drinks & early dinner, and loved everything about the place - the ambience, the service, the F&B! We drank Cold Coffee, Skinny Boy Colada, Elder Tree, The Soprano, & Ice Tea. For food, we'd Southern Style Fried Chicken, Confit Duck Salad, Stuffed Chicken Breast & ended with a Double Chocolate Parfait. All the dishes were great. Will surely return for more.

Best place to Hangout in Khan Market, if wine is your thing. Lots of options available by the bottle and by the glass.The small plates and desserts are also too good. The have a decent collection of other liquors too on the menu.

Charming place, indo-european fusion style, perfect break for a lunch break after your Kahn market shopping. The deco is airy, you will enjoy the light and peacefull coffee shop spread on two floors. Menu goes with a limited but good selection, from small salads to larger plates. Price are medium/high for Delhi.

Hands down, best cafe/restaurant in New Delhi. There are places with better dishes, better drinks and better prices, but nowhere does the full package quite as well as Perch. The whole menu is delicious, all their coffees and alcoholic drinks are exquisite, ambiance and decor is 10/10 and the prices are also reasonable for the quality of the produce used in all their menu items.  Totally biased review since this is mine and my wife's absolute favourite place to go, any time, any day of the week, but that's enough of a review in itself. If you have a chance to stop by either Perch location do not hesitate!

Nice elegant and very minimalist decor however very pleasing. We had lunch at another restaurant and came to this place just for coffee. Nice pleasant staff. The furniture is all solid chunky wood. There seems to be a good collection of wines in there and the menu looked tempting with a lot of stuff that you don’t get everywhere, a lot of European dishes. One can go for a snack or have something more substantial and wash it down with coffee or wine or beer. We had a cappuccino and a latte which were good. I would think they should stop putting the cookie and the piece of cake freebies because both tasted bad. They weren’t fresh. The cookie was soft and the cake was hard and seemed old. The coffee however was good and well presented. Will go again and spend more time eating some of the stuff that has been appreciated by others.

The interiors are simple and cheerful with plain wood and lots of natural light .  The menu while limited has some interesting option. I had the tomato gazpacho and the grilled sea bass . The gazpacho was excellent the sea bass was ok . They have a decent wine list as well .

Amazing vibe and atmosphere having great coffee and food.It has amazing wine bar too but coffee specially flat white  , single origin Indian, vietnamese iced  is outstanding.Salad and food are equally matching Go to place for relaxed sunday cafe experience in khan market
The ambience was pretty chilled, however music was at all time low volume. Loved the coffee sangria, but Risotto was a disaster with loads of cheese. Worth a try!

Perch, situated on the first and second floors in Khan Market, is worth the steep climb. Food is delicious. We had the tiger prawns with soba noodles and the scotch egg starter. Both were just right, the prawns huge, plentiful and succulent and the scotch egg nice and crisp. The lemon tart to complete the meal was also outstanding. Meal for two with non alcoholic beverage, starter, mains and dessert would be about 2000 per head with taxes. The place is small with about 5 tables seating 2-3 at a stretch. The rest are bar stools which probably give the place its name. There is a huge picture window which overlooks the trees. Service is good, helpful and courteous and regulR water is provided. Perch is worth a try if shopping in Khan market
The menu is refreshing and interesting. The service and ambience are great. A tad expensive but worth the experience.
Had a beer, amazing atmosphere, everyone seemed relaxed - this was a weekdaygood view from the large windows, prompt serviceExcellent place to have a drink in the late afternoon/early evening

Even a basic dish like Sole fish tastes good here I tried the Pan Grilled Sole and loved it My son tried the Confit Duck, but was disappointed since it turned out to be a salad (cold) would have appreciated if the guy taking the order has said so
Best service.. ask for rahul kumar.. the guy has been helping with out food and with drinks. Awesome view

Great place to visit went their last week and a special mention to Mr.Abhishek. Great gentleman and a master when it comes to drinks. Charley's rum punch, my favorite. A must visit place, very soothing and amazing ambience. Will recommend it to everyone, their service is their UPS. Keep it up abhishek.
The food was delicious and The drinks at the bar were amazing, the bartender named Abhishek was polite and made good drinks.

I visited with my friends and was looked after by Abhishek Kumar.  He made sure that we have a great time by offering us their specialities both in the food and the bar menu.  He was courteous enough to ask us how the food was.  In short a brilliant experience.
The place serves amazing cocktails and ambience is top notch. Staff is quite hospitable. They took care of our needs and special requests on high priority. Thank you I would love to visit again😊

Worst experience we had in a bar so far. Cocktails with almost no alcohol inside. Clearly let them notice about it and staff didn’t end up with any reasonable solution, instead they avoided the person who complained. Waited 15mins for an orange juice. Got overcharged for a pack of cigarettes without prior notice of the price. Finally they tried to charge underhand tip to the bill even though we clearly expressed our dissatisfaction. Never ever again.

food, bar, music and ambiance. if you would like to experience all the three visit this restaurant. this is part of Holiday Inn hotel in Mayur Vihar and I have visited it twice during my recent stay. the food taste excellent and service was amazing.

I had visited Kylin on 17th Aug. I was staying at Holiday Inn and hence wanted to give Kylin a try. The food is good, ambience is great and service is also good. But has very limited menu specially for veggies. Overall an good place to visit.

For those of who know the location of Mayur Vihar it will be difficult to comprehend how Kylin Experience an in house restaurant of Holiday Inn Express MV& Noida have Japanese & Asian Cuisine...  Anyways, that is besides the point. Went with my family for a dinner. The restaurant is spacious with tables well spread out though the over covers would not be more than 60 (my estimate and I could be wrong). We ordered sushis and rolls and liked them. I am not a connoisseur of Japanese food but we loved the food. We tried out the lettuce roll a starter as recommended, and it was awesome as mushroom starter that we ordered was not available. We then moved on to the main course ordering fried rice and another dish which was very nice. Ended the dinner with a Chocolate Mah-jong and relished it. Price range was acceptable for the ambience and value. Would certainly visit a second time, when the opportunity arises.

During my vacations to India we hardly go out to eat, but a recommendation from my friend led us to Kylin for dinner. The ambiance was just perfect. On the manager's recommendation we had the Wasabi prawns and dragon rolls and Chicken Teriyaki which is now my husband's favorite dish. The staff was courteous and polite.  Visiting Kylin again is definitely in my agenda and is highly recommended.
One of the most decent places to go to with friends, family or for work related meetings. Fabulous food and a very courteous staff. Enjoyed the food and the Ambience. Great experience overall.

One of the finest restaurants I have been to in a long time. I had to take time out to write this review for the wonderful experience I had at this wonderful place.  The teriyaki chicken and the wasabi prawns blew me away. Fantastic food. The staff was better than most of the fine dining restaurants of Delhi and Mumbai.  Great experience. Its a must go for all.

Lovely atmosphere and excellent service. Food is refreshingly different and novel. Really enjoyed my visit!!!Be careful of the bill though...unnecessary taxes on charges also applied, thus inflating the bill in a wrongful manner. Not appreciated.

We went there on birthday special time. A nice place for evening drinks and food. Spend your time with friends in this place over conversation. The interior is friendly and not intimidating like any other pubs.The place has very soft lighting and the second floor, especially, has a cozy feel. No issues on the decor/service. we ordered a Pulled Duck Salad. The Mulled Wine was more or less as expected - star anise/cinnamon loaded, sweet, with a floating orange slice. The Holiday Coffee was infused with a heavy dose of whiskey, topped with whipped cream and grated candied orange. Both of these items were incredibly comforting on the cold evening. The pulled duck was prepared and seasoned well but the meat had been marinated with too much balsamic vinegar and tasted that way. Can't call this place very cost-effective either - the season specials were surely priced over the value-for-money mark.
Great air conditioning, bright natural light, high ceilings make this place a Himalayan person elated. Good coffee collection -  try the Columbian!

Perch is a very likable place. The cafe is dotted with plants that complement it's wooden and quite interiors. There is a terrace also. The cafe music was peppy but not overwhleming.  We ordered the Duck confit with mango. The subtle flavours hit the spot. The portion was enough for the three of us and this was just one of the dishes we ordered. The Pork Scotch Eggs was okay. It was horribly overpriced for a serving of one egg! We ordered a pitcher of Sangria and Cold Coffee. Thankfully their Cold Coffee is not too sweet. The Melba Toast was decent. The Cold Cut platter was a winner for us with its presentation, portions and taste! Service was a little slow but courteous.
Perch has excellent coffee as well as good food. the place has a nice vibe to it and food is also good

Had a quick business lunch with a colleague this time. I tried the sea-bass while she had the grilled chicken. The portion sizes were generous and filling and quite delicious. The single origin cappucino was also quite nice.
A fantastic place in the middle of the city. Good range of liquor and fantastic service. A place to be if you are in Delhi NCR area.

One of the best places in delhi to hangout or catch up with friends. Topesh comes up with amazing suggestions of the cocktails which certainly are a must try. Keeping it sassy this place knows how to lighten the mood yet keeping it very courteous. Cheers to the team for doing such a great job. Keep it going.

Grappa bar at Shangri La, Eros, is a cocktail bar with class. The selection of spirits on offer is better than most in-hotel bars, but the selling point has got to be the team behind the bar! Fantastic service, drinks mixed and matched with flair and, should you require something off the menu, chances are the cocktail will be produced before your very eyes. Some individuals of note, should you be lucky enough to be served by them, Debjani and Amit will produce produce your favour tipple with a smile!!
LOVE this place. great one for people who love wine. Maybe the only good place in delhi for wine connoisseurs.  a little expensive tho
Situated in the heart of Delhi at khan market this place offers great food and European style coffee at reasonable prices.

went for a date with my partner and it was just the perfect place for us! what i most liked about it was the great vibe and the cozy atmosphere, i just loved it from the moment i entered! take a window seat if possible, you'll enjoy it even more. will definitely come back again
The food and salad was exceptional. My wife and I came away uniquely satisfied with an excellent afternoon meal and drink.
Perch is one of the hidden gems in delhi thy cater to European tastebuds. Love spending time here during winters.banana cake is really yum
Pleasant atmosphere, staff if helpful and courteous, but not very communicative (note if you travel alone).

Nothing special about this bar; it is like any other hotel bar. High end prices, simple food, 13 euros for G&T... well, just try and find out. But no reason to go specially for a drink from somewhere else.

We were a group of 8. Very nice ambience and good weather. First couple of starters were decent but post that we got below average food. Ultimately had to call the chef to get things right. Chef was really helpful and got us very nice food post that.  Main issue was the service, it was very slow. Seemed everyone was very busy. And the staff serving us kept changing.  Further problem came with the billing. Multiple items wrongly billed. Staff was arguing they were right which they were not. Plus it took us half an hour , together, to first get the wrong bill and secondly to get the correct bill.  Overall expected better things from Shangrila. Not my first time here. Service has always been above average, this time a little disappointed.

After a heavy curry at late lunch and a full day doing the sights, we decided to have a boozy evening in the bar at the hotel. Live cricket match on meant enough atmosphere. We thought about being outside on the deck but weather and haze put us off. Had a few rounds of excellent gin and brandy punch, and some simple snacks of pizza, calamari. Playing a few card games and general unwinding, service and quality of drinks a definite highlight.

Located on the ground floor of the Holiday Inn, Mayur Vihar, this restaurant has great ambiance but service was realllly slow, that too when most of the restaurant was empty. Food quality and taste was good, but bigger portions would have been better. Overall a good experience but still a long way to go.

As I'm an Asian, I thought it would be fun to try Asian food restaurant. So I went in and ordered some sushi... AND IT WAS GREAT!!! Taste of Sushi is slightly different from the ones in Japan, but very very delicious, and I've been coming to this place again and again! Not only the foods but also the people here are all doing great services, in friendly atmosphere, with very professional manner. I want to give them 100 stars in ratings.

Hosted dinner for 100 people in Kylin restuarant. Occassion was Post Marriage Dinner with very closed relatives.  Food was amazing and each one of our guest appreciated. DimSums were the main attraction for all.  Variety and taste of each dish starting from Starters, Mocktails, Main Course and in last Sweet Dish, every item was mouth watering and liked by each of our guest's and this was topped up by smiling and very cooperative staff. Service by each and every staff was more than perfect . Thanks a lot Team Kylin for making the sp event of the family one of the memorable one. Sp thanks to Ashish Saxena, Udai and Somendra!

Great food, excellent drinks, supreme bar staff.  Aniket cheeky smile is always a pleasure to see.  Realty friendly great atmosphere, just what you need when you are travelling away from home!!!  Can not recommend enough.
Excellent service and praise worthy knowledge on cocktails. Would recommend a visit to Kylin solely on Aniket’s service and humility

Had an superb evening with my friend,always feel been something unique about this place, ambience is really nice the best part of this place that you get authentic dishes with the choice of mouthwatering item that makes you appetite through oriental to thai & Japanese food. Service is really beyond expectation, would like to appreciate ms. Usha for her warm hospitality, everything was FantasticI do hardly recommend people must do visit once for dining option. Keep it up guys, kudo's to all.

Very good. Nice place. Tepanyaki wow. Chef knows how to cook. If staying in Crown Plaza or Holiday,  very recommend for those having difficult to eat here nearby. 아주 좋아요. 장소도 멋있어요. 철판구이 꼭 드셔보세요. 주방장님이 인도분인데 아주 맛있게 하네요. 인도 오셔서 입맛이 까다로워 식사하시기 어려운분들 여기 강력 추천합니다.

Holiday Inn is situated in a great location in East Delhi and the second most reason to visit this hotel is to have an incredible experience at its restaurant, Kylin . It has an extensive menu and very palatable Asian food to offer .  I visited with my hubby to celebrate our anniversary. Without wasting any time , we asked the chef to present only signature dishes. Every dish was tempting and tasted very well.  Everything was taken care of in a great manner.  Full marks to their service, food , ambiance and music. Thanks team Kylin Experience for a memorable day and making us feel really special.

What a Place - What a brilliant idea - Mexican starters specially fish tacos - salads like of rocket vth goat cheese & french lentils - with a main course from the coast - malabar prathas Vth aapams made so fresh vth chicken or prawn curry or obagine kokam curry !! The deserts carrot cake , tiramisu , Browne really special  Donot miss the masala capachino & the great smelling rose & earl grey tea !! Very pocket friendly too !! We stayed on fr ages post the dinner as loved the ambience !!

I visited this Fine Dine Restaurant a Couple of times and I enjoyed the food everytime. Its a perfect place to have chinese or Japanese food.  In Food I love their Sushi and In drinks I like Sunriser.  Both are Must Try.

We were staying in the hotel and were just too tired to think about going out to eat so we popped into NYC. You could either have the buffet - very extensive - or order off a menu catering for all tastes. As we'd been eating buffet food for the last few days we went for the menu. Good food, big portions and Kingfisher by the pitcher, yes we were very happy.
I was totally happy with food and beverage service, and great thanks to sudhir and mukesh in nyc.  Chef danish was amazing. We will come soon. Thanks.

Really had a great experience at Kylin as always.It was my office farewell dinner and I suggested the place to my friends. We had a great evening. We were served by Sagar who recommended few items on thenu to my non vegetarian friends since I had no idea abt non veg items. They loved the recommendations. My favourite was Baby potatoes and pan fried noodles. Looking forward to more visits like these. Thanks for the wonderful experience yet again.
Great place to have an authentic Thai food. Excellent service and delicious food. The ambiance is nice to enjoy with family
Went their with the group of friend to experience the food.. the ambience is great and the food service also..The server named girish is very good in his work and very good in nature
Went there recently with a group of friends .Lovely place , Great ambience and lovely food. Enjoyed a lot and especially great service by Sagar .Will love to visit again
Been to Kylin recently with family. Impressed by the overall food quality and service. In particular, Sagar from the service team was helpful and ensured a good experience.
We visited for a family dinner, food was great and the service by attendant Sagar was also good. We recommend going there for tasty Chinese food.

Service is decent, atmosphere is decent with a good vibe with and DJ. The food I had was really poor. The tom kha soup was literally thick like cream and didn't taste like coconut. I was literally almost sickened by the texture. I put some water in it to thin it out, had a few bites and had to stop eating it. Almost flavorless as well. I wonder if it was the bottom of their soup container or something? Or what happened? OMG that was yucky....I'd eat somewhere else.
The food was very tasty. The menu was diverse and easy to comprehend. The staff was cooperative. Fast service.

Chinese and Japanese food are one of the most popular cuisines in the family and I mean real Chinese and Japanese, not those Indianised Chinese restaurants. The Kylin Experience in the Holiday Inn in Mayur Vihar perfectly suites our tastes and pocket. If you are looking for a fine dining experience in Chinese cuisine I would suggest you should definitely go for this. A good experience is guaranteed. Location: The restaurant is one of the famous chain of Kylin restaurants and is located in the ground floor of the hotel Holiday Inn. It is easily accessible from all parts of Delhi and nearby Noida / Ghaziabad. The metro station is nearby which is a plus point. Parking: There is free valet parking available at the hotel so hassles of self parking. Ambiance: After entering the lobby, turn right which takes you to the restaurant. It is quite spacious with huge ceilings giving a airy feel. Decoration is little on the darker side and typical dash or red colour is present as expected in a Chinese restaurant though it is quite soothing and never goes over the top. Seating: The restaurant has a small bar section and then the regular seating section. Seating is regular chair and sofa combination and is spacious so that there is enough space for movement. Chairs are comfortable and cutlery is of good quality. Service: Since we went on a lunch on a weekday so there were hardly any guests, thus the service was spot on. We were helped in choosing the correct food order and suggestions were provided. Overall quite happy with the service. Food: We ordered Salmon Sushi and it came as a Tuna one (although the Chef came immediately to apologies and change but we insisted to continue with what was served - and it was damn good in taste). Also in order was Clap Pot Lamb with Pad Thai Noodles and few starters. The quantity was more than enough for two adults with good appetite so order carefully. Taste was really perfect with each ingredient in perfect quantity. Price: Being a fine dining restaurant it is pricey but not that much to pinch your pocket considering the wonderful food being served. A 3 course meal can go from 3K to 4K which is ok considering the brand name of the restaurant. Overall a very satisfying experience of Chinese and Japanese cuisine and definitely wish to come back more to savor some more items. Do visit and you will not be dissapointed.

Services were awesome. Rooms and very nice. Pool side is serene and food is delicious. I would specially praise the staff, every department is outstanding starting from the front office team to the restaurant and even house keeping...way to go.Will definitely visit again!!!

Food: In starters, I tried their Baby potato Platter, Broccoli & Almond Dimsums and in Main Course, I had Veg Fried Rice with Clay Pot Vegetable. The Baby potato Platter tasted delicious, it was little spicy from outside and sweet inside. The Broccoli & Almond Dimsums was the best dimsum i ever had in my life, the filling tasted so yumm.  In the main course, veg fried rice with clay pod oven was a perfect choice, the aroma of rice was so tempting and the clay pod was full of vegetables and taste was also very good. In Desserts I tried their Fried Ice cream rolls and I am a fan now. It was one the best desserts I ever had. We didn't have to wait long for our Food.
Food was great and economical .You may ask for discount if more people. complimentary jasmine tea served at   the end  is very refreshing

We went in a group of 4 adults , for a lunch,this amazing place which served Thai Chinese and Japanese -> The Kylin Experience -Holiday Inn.The super cushiony sofas and well spaced seating arrangements add to the class. The space is huge with both inside and outside seatings.Bar counter is huge and the beautiful chandeliers add to the beauty of the whole decor.  Famous for their Sushis, their handcrafted Sushi suits Indian taste palate utmost.  I ordered the following items:The service was bang on quick and staff was very courteous.  We started with traditional Thai soup Tom Yam preparation involving coconut milk, the flavour of ginger and lemongrass added a distinct touch and felt very soothing in this weather. Traditional Maki Asparagus Roll- Classic Asparagus Roll, cooked to perfection and tasted wonderful. Crunchy Yasai Roll- A delight for vegetarians, consisting of avocado, cucumber and carrot along with Teriyaki.Prawn California Roll- A makizushi roll with the crunchiness of delicious prawns, the fresh and crunchy prawns made it taste really delectable. Chicken Satay- Grilled chicken cooked in peanut sauce with a sweet and pungent marinade giving a delightful touch.Lo Han Vegetables with Spicy Garlic Sauce- Also known as "Buddha's Delight", it is basically assorted vegetables cooked in garlic sauce. The spicy taste was too hot to handle but tasted very yummy. Chicken Bullet Chilli- Very spicy as evident by the name. Chicken pieces were cooked to perfection and the taste was definitely different from what we often eat elsewhere. Pan Fried Veg Noodles- Noodles cooked along with plethora of veggies, played a perfect companion to mains dishes.Thai gravy along with broccoli, baby corns and other veggies carrying a tantalizing flavour and an amazing texture making us drool all over.The curry again was a delight with the dominant flavour of lemon grass remaining on the palate for long. With multiple veggies in this dish, they had absorbed the essence of the gravy to the core making the dish a must have when here.  Most of their dishes are extremely well prepared and delicious. An authentic Japanese and Pan Asian cuisine restaurant. Finally, the best came in the form of a dessert. 'Chocolate Mah-Jong Ice Cream' was an wonderfull affair, a warm soft brownie, dipped with hot chocolate and served with Icey cold vanilla icecream. You got to love this. Great vibe and lively ambience. The chef especially came to address us and took a feedback which was a nice gesture. However, despite being in a five star hotel, Kylin Experience comes way under your budget when you compare it with the many other restaurants in a five star.

It was our first visit to The Kylin Experience in Holiday Inn located in the heart of east delhi, Mayur Vihar First comes first, we dont have much options for Chinese, Thai in east delhi, I must say Kylin comes to the saviour when it comes to fine dine option with wonderful service, taste and ambience. Its located at ground floor of the hotel. Here is my recommendations Guava Sparkle - Very refreshing and different in taste. Crispy Mushroom , chilli honey - quite sizable mushroom topped with honey chilli sauce Udon Noodles - My all time favorite and filling Sushi with avacado - just lived it with prominent filling of Avacado Trio homemade ice-cream - three different flavors -Basil, Jasmine and Star Anise with apt quantity of sugar is worth a try. In the last, i would highly recommend Kylin for family get together and celebration with impeccable service and authentic taste

The braised lamb was a slight disappointment -- a bit on the tough side, though flavorful -- but overall, the meal was excellent. The service was very friendly and efficient -- even a bit overenthusiastic, as when they cleared the small bowl of mustard that I'd requested, not realizing that I was going to need it for a second sandwich!  I can't vouch for the Indian food, but this is a good place to enjoy foreign cuisine, and a good atmosphere for a dinner meeting.

Kylin is a name that's synonymous to good Asian cuisine in Delhi NCR. But then, it's an open secret, that it takes more than good food to make it a fine dining experience. Something that's served hot and fresh at Kylin Experience, Holiday Inn, Mayur Vihar. We were at this place for lunch recently. And to say, we were impressed much, will be pretty much an understatement. On a pleasant Saturday afternoon, we headed to this pretty place for a late lunch. Going ahead with Chef Harish's suggestions, we didn't bother to flip through the exhaustive menu. So, this was our order for the day. Seafood Tom Yum Soup Seafood & Chicken Dimsums Lamb Bulgogi Braised Pork Belly Fried Rice Noodles Fried Ice Cream The weather was just right for a hot bowl of Tom Yum soup. The zing of the spiciness added a delectable flavour to the preparation. And also, since it was a clear soup with the flavours and freshness of the ingredients intact, it actually revved up our appetite for the dishes that were to follow. If you're planning a visit to this place in this weather, ask for a bowl of this soup. The dimsums, both the chicken and the seafood ones, had a generous filling and had a melt in mouth encasing. To satiate our curiosity, we were explained by Chef Siddharth, that the presence of rice starch made the encasing much softer without leaving those irritating bits sticking to our teeth. Splendid, we say. The star of the afternoon was the teppanyaki preparation, Lamb Bulgogi. Also, the fact that we had the chance to witness the dish being made, right in front of us, made it all the more special and delicious. Subtle flavours, just the right amount of juiciness and the perfect texture of chewiness made the preparation just right. The Pork Belly, wasn't far behind. Being partial towards pork, my favourite meat, maybe was a reason for me to go for extra helpings of the preparation. But that no where goes on to imply that it wasn't a superb preparation. The fact that the belly fat wasn't left too stubborn and was easy to cut with a fork and chewed, made the preparation all the more delicious. If you're a pork lover, chances are you will like this one. And when we had the above preparations with the fried rice and noodles, we were happy souls from inside. Go for it. The fried ice cream brought a sweet ending to our meal. Here, maybe we got too busy clicking the pictures of the ice cream and so, we had a bit of watery cream instead of the 'ice' cream. Nonetheless, it was good. Though I won't list it amongst the best ones I had, it was not bad either. Now, to this good food, add a couple of more things. The impeccable service of the entire staff, with a smile on their faces and utter promptness. Thanks to the supervision of ever-warm, witty and thorough professional, Mr. Ashish Saxena, it's difficult to find one grey area when it came to the service at the Kylin. Here, another person calls for a special mention. Executive Chef Siddharth Sarma. Affable and easy to strike a conversation, Chef Siddharth will delight you every now and then, with one trivia or the other. A reflection of his talent, the sharing of trivia, goes on to make your meal taste all the more delicious. It's noteworthy that Chef Siddharth and Chef Harish have a team in place that ensures that you have some of the best Asian meals in town. And these two aspects come together, along with the good food, to offer you an absolute fine-dining experience. Go ahead, relish the experience, with some 'delish' preparations.

With so many of kylins in Delhi NCR now, they got 2 outlets of kylin express, 3 outlets of kylin experience, a kylin skybar, kylin premier and wanchai by kylin. I was here at the kylin experience in holiday in hotel in mayur vihar, though I have been to the other outlets too, not all of them but a few, other than this. As my experience at the other outlets was exceptionally good, so there wasn't supposed to be any sort of doubt for trying out this one. Kylin experience serves chinese, Japanese, Thai and Asian cuisine food. They also got a full bar at the outlet, though I just had one beer, so not to mention of any cocktail. But yeah, food was succulent so I will mention a few dishes to not to miss here. Dishes be; kafir lime spring rolls, honey chilli lotus stem, veg tempura, stir fried vegetables (asparagus, snow peas, lotus stem etc), thai green curry and hakka noodles. Other things that I tried here were veg dimsums, Tom yam soup (veg) and the ice cream platter of 3 different ice creams. Rest, service and staff was good, very prompt servicing and friendly staff. Ambience too was very soothing.

The Kylin experience will definitely give you the lifetime experience especially if you're a sushi fan. This outlet is located in the premises of The Holiday Inn hotel. The space is huge with both inside and outside seatings. The super cushiony sofas and well spaced seating arrangements add to the class. Bar counter is huge and the beautiful chandeliers add to the beauty of the whole decor. I ordered the following items: Garlic chilli fish - Cooked to percection the aroma of dish along with its taste was very impressive. Wok tossed chicken with bullet chillies - I ain't a chilli person but I really loved how the overall dish was prepared. The chicken was crunchy on outside and was really scrumptious and gooey on inside. Chicken and coriander seiw mai - I'm a big time dumpling fan. The covering was well cooked and enough sticky with delectable chicken filling. Chicken and coriander crystal - These dumplings were served in proper wooden boxes and it's essence was so enticing. My mouth is already watering with a thought of it. SUSHIS: Dragon roll - It was super creamy and delicious. I felt I was on cloud 9 after gulping it. It contained salmon, tuna, avocado. Prawn California roll - It was no less with crunchy prawn filling inside the rice roll. And tasted amazing with the dips Philadelphia roll - To the vegetarians surprise, this one contains avocado and cheese. This is no less when it comes to appetising you. DESSERT: Fried ice cream spring roll - This was one very unique item with fried spring rolls stuffed with in house vanilla ice cream. My dessert couldn't have tasted better with this yummy hot and cold combo The service was bang on quick and staff was very courteous. The chef especially came to address us and took a feedback which was a nice gesture. It was totally a heart throbbing experience. I'm definitely coming back here again.

On my visit here on Sunday for lunch, we tried the following- 1. Tom Kha Veg- A traditional Thai soup preparation involving coconut milk, the flavour of ginger and lemongrass added a distinct touch and felt very soothing in this weather. 2. Traditional Maki Asparagus Roll- Classic Asparagus Roll, cooked to perfection and tasted wonderful. 3. Crunchy Yasai Roll- A delight for vegetarians, consisting of avocado, cucumber and carrot along with Teriyaki. 4. Prawn California Roll- A makizushi roll with the crunchiness of delicious prawns, the fresh and crunchy prawns made it taste really delectable. 5. Chicken Satay- Grilled chicken cooked in peanut sauce with a sweet and pungent marinade giving a delightful touch. 6. Lo Han Vegetables in Spicy Garlic Sauce- Also known as "Buddha's Delight", it is basically assorted vegetables cooked in garlic sauce. The spicy taste was too hot to handle but tasted very yummy. 7. Chicken Bullet Chilli- Very spicy as evident by the name. Chicken pieces were cooked to perfection and the taste was definitely different from what we often eat elsewhere. 8. Stir Fried Veg Noodles- Noodles cooked along with plethora of veggies, played a perfect companion to mains dishes. # Trio Homemade Ice Cream- Homemade Ice creams with perfect amount of sweetness just as per my liking. # Chocolate Mah Jong with Ice Cream- A combination of white and dark chocolate which will excite chocolate and non-chocolate lovers alike, served along with Ice cream making it a great combo. Both times our experienced were totally ruined, we don't expect this type of service from a hotel Like Radisson.

Our extended family of 8 chose NYC for brunch last Sunday from a plethora of choices available in south Delhi.. This hotel has some fine restaurants- the iconic Great Kabab Factory and the new, highly rated Thai restaurant Neung Roi, both of which we visit frequently. It was the first time that we had a full meal at NYC, and I must say that we were not disappointed. The spread was sumptuous- Indian, Italian/continental, Asian, Mediterranean and even a 'chaat' (spicy, uniquely Indian snacks) counter. I went for the Asian - the shredded chicken, stir fried veggies, spicy lemon fish were delightful. Others thought the sea food platter and the sushi was great, Mediterranean was good, but the north Indian was nothing exceptional. Beer was limited to Kingfisher which was fine by me but the youngsters wanted other brands. The Spanish white wine was just acceptable. Dessert choices were plenty and we indulged ourselves! Service was excellent. Of 'course', nothing comes cheap in Delhi five star hotels - the original bill was around INR 2300 per head ( fortunately we got a substantial discount being members)

We received a complimentary breakfast with our room stay which was the buffet. The breakfast had the following to offer; eggs in any way, pancakes and waffles, bacon, lots of curry options, pastries, toast, yoghurts different fruits and juices. For lunch I had the enchiladas which where huge and only £7! We also then had the dinner buffet which was about £25 including tax but was totally worth it as we spent hours there. Things they had to offer included chicken, vegetables, curries, noodles/Chinese cuisine, lots of starters and sides, cheeses and a large range of desserts. I would definitely recommend, we also had the option to charge to the room and we found this a much more enjoyable experience than having to split the bill between us all at the table and settled it later instead. We thoroughly enjoyed this place, one let down I would say though is that the alcohol is very expensive when all the different taxes are added on top. I know that alcohol in India is expensive so this wasn't too much of a problem.

Food - 4/5 . The food was delicious and well prepared. Great variety especially the chaat counter. Desserts were average though. The drinks were below par , one beer that too carling , no cocktails , couple of mocktails and no fresh juices. Service - 3/5. We came in , sat down and for a long time no one came and attended. I had to ask one of the waiters about the menu and what drinks were on offer. They did not have a drinks card which would have been better rather than the waiter explaining the drinks. They were more interested in giving out the menu prices rather than how it was laid , what all was on offer etc. No one cared to ask if we needed something , we had to ask them about everything we needed. Could have been a better experience in this regards. Ambience- 3/5. Nothing to write about just like any other five start hotel. overall the experience was good but I may not go again as it was not special . Worth a visit in case you want to have a family gettogether on a sunday.

My second visit to this mostly empty (weekday lunch) restaurant that serves up chinese, japanese and thai medley of dishes. The service is attentive, the food comes quickly and is reasonably tasty even if not outstanding. Good for meetings and quiet lunches for sure in a pleasant setting. Recomended
For three days of stay we took sumptuous morning breakfast here. Quality of food is really excellent. Right from fresh orange/watermelon juice we enjoyed other vegetarian food.

So I went on lunch today to this amazing place which served thai Chinese and Japanese. Hospitality I don't even have to say about it- it was superb. Coming to the food part- I tried their fried ice cream with spring roll, it was soooooo delicious can't even express it in words it was one of the best thing I ever had in desserts. Coming to there sushis- I'm not a big fan of sushi but all I know is they were good and presented in a beautiful way. There chicken domains were heavenly. All in a perfect place to spend some quality time with your loved ones.

Bringing the best of Oriental, Kylin Experience is one of the finest dine-in with an assortment of Chinese, Japanese and Thai retreat. With their specialized menu and especially the extra-ordinary Sushis, our weekend got overwhelmed with an astonishing visit at the outlet at Mayur Vihar. Once entered, the casual, vibrant and colorful ambiance comes into notice. The saturated and warm lighting themed with grey tone of chairs and tables are greatly synced. The food is a wonderful mélange of fresh, healthy, seasonal dishes bursting with flavors. Profounded with their menu we started off with, In FOOD we had,  ➊ GARLIC CHILI FISH.  As fresh and authentic it could be. Perfect crisp of the batter and utterly supple texture of the fish. The blend of garlic and chili was well balanced. The overall taste was appetizing.  ➋ WOK TOSSED CHICKEN WITH BULLET CHILIES.  The most favorable dish in terms of the amalgamation of chilis and finely soy sauce tossed chicken. The taste was absolutely tasteful and with added peanuts, it was alluring. The presentation, however, could have been taken more care.  ➌ CHICKEN AND CORIANDER SIEW MAI. The greatly stuffed and authentic chicken and coriander filling were astonishing. The Siew Mai batter was smooth and drooling. Finely tasted and aptly quantified.  ➍ CHICKEN AND CORIANDER CRYSTAL.  The taste was equivalent to the Siew Mai but the texture of the batter and the blend of taste was comparatively less appealing than Siew Mai.  SUSHIS. ➎ PHILADELPHIA ROLL. Salmon & Philadelphia Cheese Uramaki with Cucumber.  The color texture of the Sushi looked alluring. The Salmon tasted amazing with the sushi filling of mild-tasting fresh and simple cheese and cucumber. *HIGHLY RECOMMENDED* ➏ PRAWN CALIFORNIA ROLL.  Tempura prawn roll with lettuce, chili mayo, and Schichimi.  Prawn is always been the most the lovable ingredient and it did taste splendid in the sushi format. The prawn tempura has a fine blend of chili mayo and shichimi spice which gave a fine spicy yet tasteful quotient.  ➐ DRAGON ROLL.  Crab Stick, Salmon, Tuna, Avocado, and Cucumber with Mayonnaise and Teriyaki sauce. One of the most fantasizing variants of the sushi they offer.  The Salmon felt absolutely fresh and exquisite as an add-on in this sushi. The foremost likable quotient is that it was overwhelmed with lots of mayo and teriyaki sauce on the top which was quite drooling. *HIGHLY RECOMMENDED* ➑ FRIED ICE CREAM SPRING ROLLS.  Quite a distinctive preparation of even the simplest of recipes. The roll batter was fried and filled with cold yet drenching vanilla cream, which was astonishing overall. The taste was eventually delightful.  Moreover, the experience was quite a happiness worthy with the eccentric food menu especially their Sushis. The staffing was quite prompt. The aura and the experience altogether worked like a charm.

It was quite a discovery to find this wonderful cafe in the hullabaloo of Hauz Khas village.. No pushy salesman at the front and a wondful air conditioned cafe a few floors up. I had a prawn curry and thoroughly enjoyed it and was made to feel very welcome. I was guided through the Appam menus and kept well stocked with different types of ways to eat my curry!  Certainly the perfect place amongst the bars, clubs and tacky looking restaurants for a decent cafe meal with style. I think the beautiful people of delhi come here .. It was great for people watching!
While staying at Holiday Inn, we dined at Kylin--total of 9 guests. The food was wonderful! Great dim sums, noodles and a variety of chicken dishes to please both American and Indian taste buds.

I was looking for a place with a view yesterday and decided to go here basis all the good reviews. We could sit in the balcony because it was too cold so chose a table next to the window. It is a very cute place.  We ordered mulled wine, veg tacos, chicken curry and appams. This was the first time I had mulled wine and it was delicious... warm and smelt like cinnamon. The veg tacos were good but the quantity was quite less. Only 3 pcs in a plate. The chicken curry was not spicy at all. I like South Indian food because it is spicy. But it tasted nice. Good food but the menu options are less. There is only 1 chicken South Indian main course. Also, considering the quantity it is quite pricey.

Was over in Delhi with work and went to the restaurant at this hotel for a meal with colleagues. food was super - especially for a hotel which can sometimes be very bland - & service was very nice. Highly recommend. this was the restaurant on the first floor, I believe there is another one up on the roof but didn't have the time to go to that one

Gone on Sunday for Brunch with family. Much more variety than expected. Starting with Indian,continental,Mediterian,Chinese,Japanese... We stated with pani puri & bhelpuri. Bhelpuri was very good. Garlic bread was superb. Humus , Hakka noodles were ok. Sweet dishes are the best specially marsh mellows dip in chocolate fountain & pumpkin nut pie. Ice creams are home made & value for money. Staff very helpful.

Sunday Brunches always bring in the pleasure of waking up late on a lazy day and enjoying the vast variety of scrumptious food in an cheerful ambience. New York Café Sunday Brunch is one such inviting place to dig into some delicious food and savour on cuisines from all over world. We reached by around 1 pm, and apart from the 3 bloggers tables, almost 80 % tables were full. People were already chit-chatting over beer and mocktails and had made themselves comfortable in the lively atmosphere. The live music playing Phil Collins and Bryan Adams added to the whole mood and it just seemed like a place to be on a Beautiful Sunday Noon. The open chic restaurant has a theme based cuisine every day along with Indian cuisine which is a hit amongst Delhiites. Sunday Brunches are a mix cuisine with soup, a huge variety of salads/starters, Mezze station, Main course ( Asian/ Indian ), The Live counter ( Pizza, Pasta, Shawarma ) and of course, the wide array of western and Indian desserts! The menu seemed huge, so we decided to start right away with the salads. First on our table was served a chilled sparkling wine and white wine ! The staff courteousness and the efficient service is must mention. Our dirty plates were always replaced with the new ones every time we came to the table. The drink/ mocktail/ cocktail recommendations kept flowing and the staff seemed to enjoy their work throwing a cheerful smile throughout. And I would give a lot of the credit to the Chef Sreenivasan G who was on toes and seen everywhere around – At the live station, addressing the sous-chefs and chitchatting with guests. Humble enough to take suggestions, and thoroughly having a passion for food, he enjoys going out and trying different cuisines too! Considerate enough to make sure we tried everything on the menu, he said he makes sure his guest should never go hungry! And catering to that customer satisfaction, they have introduced a on-the-go-breakfast counter in the lobby where they keep food items like croissant, puffs, tea, coffee just outside the lift early in the morning around 5 am. We thought this idea to be brilliant, and never heard before and give a 5/5 for this thought! The salad section really looked appetizing ! There were individual portions of Cherry Tomato Gazpacho with Roasted Almonds, Asparagus and Mozarella Salad with Chilli Citrus Salsa and the “we-loved-it” Raw Mango Salad with Chilli and Honey brought in the perfect summer flavours. There were also On the Platter salads like Watermelon and feta with balsamic Caviar, Fried Eggplant with spicy Peanut and Palm Sugar Dressing but the combination of Pomelo Salad with coconut and tamarind dressing was worth a try! It was a little too tangy for our taste, yet tasted refreshing! In Non Vegetarian Salad, Maneesh loved the Prawn cocktail with Cilantro and Young onions and also the Chicken Liver Gateaux with Red Wine glaze. It was soft, flavourful and perfectly dressed ! The individual portions presentations were a treat to the eyes. From the elaborate Chaat section offering Chole kulche, Aloo tikki, Papdi Chaat, Dahi bhalla, Dhokla and even Khandvi, we tasted the Gol Gappa. The water was a little extra salty to our taste. But the ladies seem to gather around the corner and enjoying aloo tikki and Dhokla. The Mezze Section had an eclectic menu of Babganoush, Hummus Beiruty ( mixed with tomato, onions and parsley ), Mastvakhyar ( Cream Yogurt, cucumber, raisin, sesame seed and olive oil )and Lubia Bi Zayt ( Tender beans in onion, garlic, tomato and olive oil ). All of it tasted divine with the fresh and soft whole wheat pita bread. Then came on the table were crispy noodle and tofu vegetable rolls and paneer pakoras with green chutney. It was delight to see the pakoras had no sign of oil, and yet tasted great. And the pizza from the live station served all sorts of thin crust pizzas topped with chicken, pepperoni, tomato, capsicum, onion, mozzarella, Olives, Jalapenos…. There was also a wide range of choice in Pasta with sauces, but we didn’t try that. A fresh watermelon and sweet lime juice gave a good break to our eating before we went to the Main course section.  The main course variety is less as compared to the salads and others, but guess you are so full eating rest of the things that you don’t have an empty stomach to try the main course. Nevertheless, Maneesh tried the Dum Gosht Biryani and Manglorean Fish Curry and he had his fingers ( fork and spoons!!! ) licking. I tried the Tomato Pudhiney ka Pulao which I found average but the Hyderabadi Mirch ka Salan is must try! The rest of the main course menu was good too. Worth a Mention from the Live Kitchen is also The Grilled Prawn with Mango Chilli Sauce and the Chettinad Spiced Chicken Satay with tomato chutney. And then we turned to the much awaited section and we were not disappointed ! There was abundance of it. I tried all the western desserts and Maneesh tried the Indian ones and we both were drooling ! The Tiramisu, the Newyork style based cheesecake, the Mango Passion Fruit Pyramid, The Orange Crème Brulee, Walnut Tart, Pumpkin Pie everything was tasted and every single bite tasted heavenly. But recommended is the White Chocolate Strawberry Bavaroise. Not only did it look terrific, it tasted out of the world. Maneesh loved the soft gulab jamun which had a dry fruit filling inside, and also the kalakand which was fresh and sugarfree ! And yes, The Chocolate fountain with condiments and the marshmallows fondue made me a kid again. And for those calorie-watchers, there was even a wide variety of fresh fruits. We were so full that we had to skip the ice cream and the gelatos !  We definitely recommend this Buffet for the variety of cuisine, the presentation , the food preparations and the staff service. We give it a 4/5. Meal for two – INR 3500 ( Plus taxes ) Address : Radisson Blu Plaza, National Highway 8, Near IGI Airport, Mahipalpur, Delhi Phone number : 011-33036298

I am a big fan of Sunday Brunch. I like the idea of having an extended meal course after course until one is almost ready for evening tea. There are many places I have been to for brunch and I was always left with the feeling that buffets and bunches are the place where good quality ingredients turn into compost. NYC is an exception.  It is impossible to get everything right at a buffet but when a Chef is really talented, he would know how to manage most of the things on the menu to the best of his ability to create something of a spectacle for his patrons. Creating a menu with 200 odd dishes is quite a herculean task and Chef Sreenivasan G pulled it off like a charm.  The NYC restaurant in Radisson Blu Plaza was launched as New York Café with an aim to provide a relaxed New York type atmosphere. The delightful city’s ambience was invoked with large photographs of the city's skyline and neon signs. Colorful aprons were worn by the servers and everything was perfectly designed.  The restaurant’s name was later shortened to NYC and it fondly came to be referred as such. While a few black and white pictures of New York City are still see brightening up the walls, all the other signs have receded to the background to give the restaurant a chic and contemporary feel.  The Sunday bunch menu had individual portions of “Cherry Tomato Gazpacho with Roasted Almonds”, “Asparagus and mozzarella salad with chill citrus salsa” and “Raw Mango Salad with Chilli and Honey”. In Platters they had “Water melon and feta with balsamic Caviar”, “Pomelo Salad with coconut and tamarind dressing”, “Fried Eggplant with Spicy Peanut and Palm Sugar Dressing, and “German Potato and Red Cabbage Salad with Fried Onions”. The vegetarians at my table loved everything. I personally loved the Cherry Tomato Gazpacho the most. Gazpacho is a simple tomato based recipe that relies on a healthy amount of good quality cherry tomatoes and fragrant herbs like basil and coriander to infuse the gazpacho with oodles of flavour. This is a great summer recipe and I really loved its texture and taste at NYC.  The Non- Veg Salads interested me more than anything else. The “Chicken Liver Gateaux with Red Wine Glaze and Brioche” in particular was beautifully presented and tasted brilliant. They also has “Kafta Spiced Chicken on Rice Pancakes” and my favourite fresh “Prawn Cocktail with Cilantro and young onions”. The prawn was very flavourful and fresh, just the way it should have been. In the Platters they had “Honey Glazed Soya and Sesame Chicken”, “Cumin Crusted Red Wine Poached Tenderloin with Shallots”, “Pancetta” and my favourite “Smoked Salmon with capers and dill”. The salmon was divine.  The restaurant also had a very elaborate Chaat Station for people who love chaat. I am not a big fan of chaat but even I was tempted to look at and try the elaborate spread of “Chole Kulche”, “Aloo Tikki”, ”Papadi Chat”, “Dahi Bhalla”, “Golgappa”, “Bhel Puri”, “Dhokla”and ”Khandvi. I loved the Bhel Puri and had to go for seconds.  The Mezze Station consisted of “Babaganoush”, ”Tabbouleh”, “Humus Beiruty” (Hummus mixed with tomato, onion and parsley), “Mastvakhyar” (Creamy Yoghurt, cucumber, raisin, sesame seed and olive oil), “Lubia bi Zayt” (Tender green bean sautéed in onion garlic, tomato and Olive oil). I poured extra olive oil on to my hummus and enjoyed it with “Whole-Wheat Pita”. It couldn't get any better than that.  I wanted to walk around the place a little before I could eat another bite, so, I got some “Potato and Leek with parmesan soup” in a cup and sat in the restaurant listening to their live band. It was a very plesant experiece and a beautiful Sunday afternoon. My friends enjoyed the liveliness that was NYC.  It was now time for the elaborate main course. Even though I wss still quite full from the initial courses, I knew if I missed the “Leg of Lamb with haricot bean and kafirlime lime Sauce” I would regret it. The restaurant also served “Grilled Tenderloin, mash with Gravy”, “Pan Seared Salmon”, “Wilted Spinach with Saffron Sauce”, “Roast Vegetable Cannelloni” and ”Herb Roasted Pepper and Turned Potatoes”.  As I walked around I saw some delicious looking Turkey Roulade, Ginger Maple Carrots / Sweet Potato Fries / Pumpkin Puree and Brussels sprouts. I couldn't try it but everyone else at my table enjoyed it.  I am a big fan of Asian food so I just could not miss that section. I got a fresh plate and served myself some “Vegetable Hakka Noodles”, “Wok Fried Chicken with Sichuan Pepper, Dry Chilli and Young Ginger”, “Crispy Lamb with spring onion ginger Sauce Braised Pokchoi”and “Carrot and Snow Peas in Hot Garlic Sauce”. YES! All of that! They had “Tofu with Sautéed Onions, Red Chilli & Coriander” but I am not a big fan of Tofu so I skipped that. The Hakka Noodles went beautifully with the Wok Fried Chicken – rcommended.  If all that they had served was already not enough, they had an elaborate Indian section too. The Indian Section consisted of “Gosht Dum Biriyani”, “Tomato Pudineh ka Pulao”, “Mangalorean Fish Curry”, “Methi Murgh”, “Dhaniye Wale Aloo Ki Kathli”, “Teekhi Tali Mackerel”, “Subz Panchratani”, “Hyderabadi Saalan” (that they had run out of), “Lehsuni Palak Paneer Pasanda”, “Dum Ka Dal” and “Boondhi Ka Raita”. They had a lot of different kinds of Papad and Achar too. Have the Gosht Dum Biriyani with the Methi Murgh and Boondhi Ka Raita. Naan can be enjoyed with Lehsuni Palak Paneer Pasanda - a great combo for vegetarians. The servers were very prompt in taking away our dirty dishes and replacing them with clean ones. They also made sure that our glasses were always full of beverages. I enjoyed my mango passion fruit drink very much. It was a refreshing break from a very elaborate meal.  NYC has a Live Kitchen Counter from where you can order “Thyme and lemon grass scented seared fish”, “Grilled prawn with mango chilli sauce,”, “Chettinad spiced chicken satay with tomato chutney”, “Chutney bhara Paneer Pakora“ and “Glass noodles and tofu spring rolls with sweet chilli jam”. We had ordered almost all the dishes at our table other than the tofu dish. A common favourite was the Grilled prawn dish with mango chilli sauce.
