The prawns had been cooked perfectly and tasted special - an absolute must try. The Chutney bhara Paneer Pakora disappeared almost as soon as it was brought to our table.  At our table we were served several slices of all kinds of Pizzas with toppings like Tomato, mozzarella, onion, pepper, olives, jalapeños, chicken and pepperoni. We were also served Chicken Shawarma with Pita, Garlic aioli, Tahini, Pickled vegetables and Sumac spiced salad. We had a choice of picking the Pasta of our choice with all kinds of Sauces, everything made to order.  I am researching for my book on 'Desserts in Delhi' and when I saw the dessert spread at NYC, my jaw dropped. I loved the variety. I had to try everything. They had “Mocha Brownie Mousse”, “Orange Crème Brulee”, “New York Style Baked Cheesecake” (my favourite), “Mango Passion Fruit Pyramid”, “Tiramisu”, “White Chocolate Strawberry Bavaroise”, “Walnut Tart”, “Chocolate Éclair”, “Pumpkin Nut Pie” and “Vanilla Panacotta with Fruits” which was a Sugar Free concoction but didn’t really seem like it was sugar free because it was that delicious. The Pumpkin Nut Pie must not be missed. I am not the biggest fan of orange but the Orange Crème Brulee was absolutely delectable.  I was so engrossed in the above desserts that I almost missed the Indian desserts. The kind server came up to me and reminded me that I had more stuff to try. “Gulab Jamun”, “Kalakand” which was Sugar Free, “Malai Chop”, “RajBhog” and “Kesari Phirni”. If you happen to be at NYC do not come back without trying the big yellow RajBhog, it was delicous and the best dessert on the menu – everyone at my table loved it.  We also tried blue cheese, edam, etc. (with cra
It was a lovely meal we had on our last day. We selected Japanese dishes and what a taste. Very delicious not to be missed.

What an amazing experience overall !!! It was my mother's birthday and really wanted the entire evening to be super-special. I am so glad I chose Kylin. I started with the Salmon Sushi and then ordered Fiery Crispy Lamb and Tepanyaki Prawn with Sticky Rice. Must appreciate Pascal (the chef) here. Each dish was cooked to perfection and lamb was 'oh so sinfully crispy'. What made the experience special, was Navdeep. Mr. Sanjay Keswani, please make a special note of my comment here and would REALLY appreciate, if you could personally meet and convey my supreme pleasure at the way he handled our table. Always alert and always available (even without having to call him). He really ensured that we were well attended to and any sort of appreciation that could be OFFICIALLY presented to him, IS WORTH IT. Mr. Keswani, do share my gratitude to Navdeep. I had ordered a cake for the occasion, but had forgotten to carry it to the restaurant. Navdeep came and asked us(even without me having to say anything), if it was a special occasion we were celebrating and promptly organised a Cheese Cake(on the house) for us. Time and money well spent and would definitely recommend the place to others who would like to go there. WOULD SURELY VISIT AGAIN.
Food and service were both excellent.  The menu is quite varied, but very tasty Indian.  It was a bit noisy, but perhaps it was the time of day.
Excellent work,enjoyed my time their.Buffet is good choice, best value with exceptional service. Highly recommended.

NYC seems very proud of the variety they are able to provide, including Indian, western, chinese, and special guest themes (the night we dined there, it was Lebanese). I think one is best off staying with the Indian stuff, which was excellent (though they did a really nice felafel, kibbeh, etc.), as some of the western offerings were rather plain. They have a station at the back where some things are made fresh and delivered to your table (at breakfast, omelets, dosai, etc.; at dinner, grilled king prawns, bbq mutton or chicken). Dessert variety was also excellent. Service is hit or miss. One day at breakfast, I actually got a rather hostile reaction to a request for cutlery, which was not on the table where we were seated.

If I get to the Delhi international airport early for a flight, I like to head over to the NYC, Radisson Blu for dinner. It a RS 100 ride on Uber, about 300 in a taxi. I like the upscale, but casual vibe and the dinner buffet has a wonderful, but not overwhelming selection of food for Rs2000 including tax. I am a vegetarian and they have a fantastic selection of vegetarian dishes. The vegetables dishes contains lots of fresh vegetables, nice seasoned and not overwhelming spicy or oily for the western palate. If this restaurant was in Los Angeles, it would be the best "Indian" cuisine restaurant by far in Los Angeles. Check it out, I think you will like it.
I really enjoyed the food and timely service.  I any of the items are not available in the buffet, just contact the waiter or waitress and they will help you in getting those items to your table.
Great Food. Nice ambience. Lovely service. Restaurant is located near the airport in a five star hotel. They offer variety of good food.

Kyline Experience is beautiful and classy, the ambience has stunning interior with classy designs are added to the decor. I appreciate the designer and the architecture group for creating a classy lounge with dim lighting and comfortable seating, Kyline Experience is indeed amazing and beautiful. The ambience has amazing bar which look mindblowing. The menu of Kyline is full of delicious Japanese, Chinese and Thai cuisines. I ordered some of the signature cuisines on the recommendation of chef.  I started with Tom yum seafood soup, the amazing soup cooked with delicious ingredients and fresh seafood.  I like the presentation and flavors of Shushi plater which includes Prawn, salmon, nigiri and tuna. I appreciate the taste. In vegetarian i had Kylin lettuce Wraps, the taste was perfect. Carb singalpore dimsum was perfectly added with delicious fillings and beautifully presented. I appreciate the flavourful taste of Chicken coriander dimsum. Freshly prepared Ginger prawn was perfect in taste. Chilli garlic fish was delicious, perfectly marinated and fabulously added with spicy coating. I really appreciate the taste of Wok tossed chicken bullet chilli, soft and tangy chicken pieces added with flavourful sauce. In dessert segment i had delicious Fried ice cream spring roll, i appreciate the flavourful taste and beautiful presentation. Chocolate mahjong was extra delicious in taste with dark chocolate. I appreciate the presentation and taste of each cuisine with first class hospitality

Kylin the experience that was not just Amazing but A Laziz affair. Being baby of the most reputed brand of the hospitality sector, Holiday Inn, Kylin has truly lived up to the expectations of the Mother brand. It is an exquisite fine dining In-house Restro that serves you an Amazing variety of Japanese and Pan Asian food like Sushis, Bao, Dimsums, Teppanyaki, Robatayaki Menu straight from the Wok tossed and turned with Flavors and bites.  We went in a group of 4 adults and 2 kids, and it was a pleasurable dining experience. Our Luncheon started with Mocktails: Recommended ones:  Sunrise Lemon Iced Tea: This is a beautiful sunny day know with refreshing lemon flavours on a subtle tea base.  Another drink, which you must try out here is Guava Sparkle. A sublime refreshing drink that not only looks good but enhanced the guavas taste to a new Level. I got a customized Kaafir lime Chilli mojito for myself, that was gorgeous and yummm to have. The chilli and Kaafir are my favorite combination and the bartender made them to the perfection. We ordered Assortment and miniature tasting platters of Sushis and Dimsums as that's the best heard about for Kylin. In Dimsums we tried their Vegetarian versions comprising of Tofu and veggies, Almond and Broccoli; Snow peas, Mushroom and water chestnuts, Mix veg and lotus stem sui mui. In Non- Vegetarian, we tried their chicken coriander and ginger prawns. Each one of the variant had a peculiar taste and absolutely delicious. One of the best Dimsums we had ever relished.  Famous for their Sushis, their handcrafted Sushi suits Indian taste palate utmost. We had their: Crunchy Yasai Roll which had pickled radish, green apple, cucumber, chilli mayo and tempura flakes. Simply loved it. Next we tried their Vegetable Philadelphia Roll, comprising of Lettuce, cucumber, carrot and Philadelphia cheese with crunchy toppings. I will HIGHLY RECOMMEND their Asparagus Tempura Roll, which I loved the most. It had Asparagus tempura with chilli mayo and topped with black sesame. Another interesting Sushi was their Quinoa Sushi Roll comprising Avocado, green apple, carrot, quinoa with Wasabi topping.  In their, Non-vegetarian variant, we tried their Classic Prawns California Roll made up from Tempura Prawn roll with chilli mayo, lettuce, shichmi and Philadelphia Roll comprising Salmon and Philadelphia cheese uramaki with cucumber.  After tasting their specials, we also ordered few starters. Baby potatoes in crushed pepper were simply lip smacking and tossed in Asian greens.  We also tried their Tossed chicken with bullet chillies, which was an Oriental chilli chicken, but in shredded style.  In Main course, we ordered Chicken stir fried Noodles, which were lightly tossed noodles in Soya, greens, shredded egg and chicken. Also, we tried their Chicken Pan fried Noodles, A hot pot meal of noodles, veggies and chicken in a glazed garlic Soya base. We paired our noodles with all time favorite Kung Pao Chicken in peanut, Thai chilli's and greens. A beautiful velvety textured chicken that complimented the noodles very well. We love sea food, and tried their two best Recommended fish dishes, Steamed River Soul Fish in chilli garlic sauce and veggies. Another beautiful Flaky Fish was their Basa fish in Teppanyaki Thai sauce. Simply gorgeous, light and mouth melt-in. To add in little more texture, we ordered their Lightly Stir fried Asparagus, snow peas, lotus stem with ginger and scallion, which were really light and delicious.  The Lazziz affair didn't ended just here. We  couldn't stop ourselves to relish an amazing variety of Pan Asian deserts. This place is a paradise for sweet tooth lovers. Sinfully overloaded. The sweet tooth fantasy began with  Fried Ice cream spring rolls, which was unexpectedly the most surprising and delicious treat. Beautiful thin pastry fillet wrapping ice cold chilled vanilla ice cream. They were Delish.  Chocholate Mahjong with ice cream was a decadent moist chocolate slice thats sinful.  Green Tea Creme brulee is One of the most sinful desert on this Earth, which is pure love.️ Not too sweet nor too bitter, it's a beautiful light mousse, which you don't wish to leave till it goes off. The Caramel Crisp on the top and the crackle once you knock and dig in is a beautiful surprise. Loved it till last lick. The Date Roll with vanilla ice cream were sheer Bliss. The date cinnamon Flavors made it simply DIVINE.  The Kylin experience combined with Holiday Inn hospitality makes them stand tall and apart from the league and you can expect nothing but world class meals, yet at pocket friendly pricing. So, Why to miss the Japanese street food when you have Kylin next door.

Our love for Pan Asian delicacies is well known to our near and dear ones. We keep on trying every Pan Asian eatery that is suggested to us. And this time it was Kylin Experience, Holiday Inn, Mayur Vihar who served us some exotic and authentic oriental delicacies. Located on the ground floor, Kylin Experience is a fine dining Pan Asian restaurant with authentic Japanese and Thai being their speciality.  Kylin Experience serves mouth-watering sushi, dimsums and a unique mix of oriental favourites and is already a hit with food enthusiasts all over Delhi. The décor at Kylin Experience is both dramatic and spacious. The moment we entered the restaurant, we were overwhelmed by the pleasing Oriental interiors.    At Kylin Experience the oriental food gets a new definition. Soups, Appetisers, veggies and various kinds of seafood and meat tossed up with excellent curries are some of the highlights of the elaborate menu. The best thing about the dishes at Kylin Experience is that it serves dishes which have very traditional and authentic taste and are not like the oriental food that we are normally accustomed to, I mean the Desi Asian flavours.  We started with a few drinks at the bar and loved the Pineapple Mojito and Cucumber Mint Tumbler which were quite refreshing, but I guess I should have given a miss to Wasabi Bloody Mary.  The food menu, a perfect blend of comfort and innovation is packed with delightful and exciting Asian flavours to give an extraordinary experience of the cuisine that is commonly limited to just red or green curry in India. We started with Tom Kha Vegetable soup. It’s a Thai soup, and also called as Tom kha kai. We loved the spicy and sour taste of this coconut milk soup. I finished up my soup in a hurry as I was eager to feast on the Sushi and dimsums.    And very soon, the exotic presentation of dimsums and sushi arrived on our tables. We were served Asparagus Maki sushi, Vegetable curry dumpling, Tofu and vegetable dimsums. All of these were exotic pieces of culinary art, and tasted equally divine. It was one of the most authentic tastes that you normally don’t find at any other oriental outlets in Delhi NCR. In fact when the platters arrived on my table, the fragrance took me to the environs of restaurants in Singapore.  We also had the Oshitashi Roll, and the Asparagus Tempura roll. I simply couldn’t say anything, just kept eating. This was in fact one of those rare occasions when I forgot to click pictures before disturbing the presentations. Exotic, divine, tasty, I could only utter these words after eating the delicious rolls. They were flavoursome with crunchy shredded veggies inside rice outer crusts.   We also tried Crispy mushroom honey chili pepper, and Lotus stem honey chili in the starter section.  It was getting heavy on our tummy now, and we hurriedly wanted the mains to be served. The ever attentive Nitesh Ranasensed this and quickly served us Mapo Tofu vegetables, Lohan veg spicy, Garlic Veg fried rice and Noodles. Tofu as we all know does not have any flavour of its own and I prefer to avoid them. But the excellent work of the chefs here brought out the magic in the Tofu dishes too with perfect blend of spices. And now it was Desserts time. So when Chef Dhondhup asked me what to order, I simply said, bring all that you think are good. And soon our table was flooded with Chocolate Mah-Jong Ice Cream, Star Anise Ice cream and Fried Ice Cream Spring Rolls.  A perfectly hot n melting Choco Mahajong ice cream is something you don’t get everywhere. It is a warm soft brownie, dipped with hot chocolate and served with ice cream. At Kylin I was super delighted when my colleagues left the plate after just a bite. And I happily finished the entire serving.  Fried Ice cream Spring Rolls, was simply vanilla ice cream filled in crunchy and hot spring rolls, sprinkled with honey and nuts. It didn’t click though with me as the ice cream made the spring rolls soggy and sticky.  Star Anise ice cream was the show stealer of the evening. Again I was very happy to have had a chance to finish the entire serving.  Well, friends, we have tried to be as experimental as possible and enjoyed the authentic flavours that we were presented at Kylin Experience. This certainly is one of the most authentic pan-asian restaurants in Delhi and we hope that you also will have a gala time when you visit.
I really like to make my colleagues discover this restaurant : good food and a great show made by cocker when preparing the food

Was staying at the Holiday Inn so tried the restaurant for lunch after a very early start for an Agra trip. Very good extensive menu of Thai, Japanese fusion food. Tempura prawns with Wasabi mayo and chilli chicken wings very good starters and double cooked duck with rice very good and huge portion.!staff very attentive and friendly.

If you really wanted to have the best authentic food please visit the kylin experience to taste the best noodles which was prepared in perfectly blended spices and sauces. The pad thai noodles was amazing as it was full of chicken and nuts along with veggies in that.  The staff and service of this restaurant along with the ambience was great. Hope to dine with them again soon and try another best dishes. Just one thing i felt the prices are some where over hope they reduced it to some extent.

If you are looking for a fine dine experience in Mayur Vihar then The Kylin Experience located in Holiday Inn Mayur Vihar is just the place for you. An authentic Japanese and Asian cuisine restaurant, the Kylin Experience has all you look forward to in a lavish fine dine restaurant. We went to celebrate a special occasion here and were happy with the interiors at a glance. The interiors of this place are classy with aptly lit lighting and comfortable seating. For starters we tried the chicken satay, seafood dimsums and wok tossed chicken with bullet chillies. We liked the chicken satay and the dimsums but the star was the wok tossed chicken as it had an amazing taste with crunchy chicken pieces. For main course we tried the fried rice and noodles which were sufficient for two and were satiating in taste. The best part was that the noodles didn't need any additional gravy to complement it. For desserts we had the chocolate mah jong with ice cream and enjoyed it totally. Overall it was a very good experience with good food and good service. We look forward to visit the Kylin Experience again.

The first thing I noticed & liked was the new spacious lift :) so unlike a lot of restaurants in Hauz Khas Village, there is no need to climb narrow stairways. The best part is its location, its right above the Ogaan showroom and the first restaurant you cross when entering this Urban Village. The cafe is spread across two floors and even has a small open sitting section/balcony/smoking area. The decor is chic and they even have small plants on each table. The place is well light, clean, spacious and the art work gives the cafe a very soothing feel. The menu is Cafe Style with a heavy Coastal influence.  Ordered two non alcoholic drinks i.e. Kaffir Lime Tonic and J's Highway Lemonade. Started off with Mustard Tossed Home Fries which were ok, nothing great.  Eat With Your Hands Kerala Grilled Chicken was next and i will stick my neck out and admit that it was the best grilled chicken i have ever had. Very juicy, just the right amount of spices and cooked to perfection. Mutton Sukka was a delight. In mains we were served superb Cost Cafe Prawn Moilee and also a fabulous Chicken Curry. The chicken curry was packed with flavours and there was a perfect balance of spices. The malabar parantha was ok, but the appam's served here rate amongst the best served in Delhi (and yes i have been to Dakshin).  This was the first time i have had an Egg White Appam and i must say that i loved it. Perhaps my only disappointment that night was the lack of options in desserts. Forget dining etiquettes and use your fingers and I guarantee you that you will end up licking them off. The management here has got everything right, from presentation to taste of food to the beautiful ambience and of course the amicable service. The rates are also pretty reasonable. This Cafe is a must visit.
Loved everything in the place:the wonderful déco, the chill out ambiance, the excellent music! The food was also delicious (had the amazing goat cheese and aspargus crêpe! ). A must-go!!
This is at Hauz Khas village as you enter the village, on the third floor, there is lift to go up, food is very good we had very south Indian food here and couple of glasses of Beer! it is fully A/C.

Ordered tacos, which were the typical supermarket hard tortillas, stuffed with a few deep fried veggie pieces (zero healthy, id recommend they do grilled vegetables) and topped with plain shredded cabbage... Over-priced and nothing special...

Had heard a bit about how coast in hkv had become the toast of the village which has a mixed bunch of restaurants which locals and tourists flock. Went there for a business lunch and enjoyed each order immensely : the quinoa salad a recipe i now use at home:) the coastal fish with malabari paranthas delightful. A sure winner this place if it keeps the service and food quality intact

One of the best coastal food restaurants in Delhi. Particularly in Hauz Khas village, which is not cluttered with bars and restaurants of all kinds, it is difficult to find a place which specializes in FOOD. If you are looking for veg and non-veg food with great ambiance, this is certainly the place to be at.  If you are on a romantic date, this place might not really be the best since its quite small and you can easily get distracted by conversations on other tables, servers / waiters moving around etc. However, if some nice Kerala food + red wine is your priority, do drop by..  On a separate note, a glass of wine and food and heading to a club for some dance is a better option, than just spending time at this cafe

We went to Kylin since we had had a good experience there earlier. Large place with tables not jostling each other so gives space to all diners. Even though it was a Saturday evening it wasn't crowded. We opted for Chinese as that was what most wanted. The chicken and prawn dishes were good. The noodles and rice too were up to the mark. The only small minus was that though all the dishes were served together the rice and noodles weren't served hot. We were looked after by Nitish Rana who was excellent at his job. He suggested dishes which were to our liking and took care of our dinner very well. Nice experience.
Great restaurant enjoyed the dinner with Japanese / Indian food. Although arrived late like 22.30 we got a delicious meal.

Went to Kylin at the Holiday Inn Mayur Vihar on the occasion of my Parents 37th Wedding Anniversary past week . We generally buy the cake from the Hotel itself when we go on special occassions , Holiday Inn didn't have a Pastry Shop still the Manager Mr Faisal arranged a special Cake for Us from the Kitchen . It was super sweet of Him to do that   We were around 12 of Us . The Team really took good care of Us. The food was good Variety & Taste both and the Service was Exceptional . I mostly tend to forget the names of the staff, but because of their Efficiency and courteousness, I still remember. Nitish and Manish took care of Us in a really good way . Thanks a lot Team for making the occassion super special . Much Appreciate .  Shall visit again and soon to Try out the rest Dishes from the Menu .

They make you feel as the customer is the King !! They have live teppanyaki counter which definitely is a must try , and serves you at a great price (without a hole in your pocket) We started our meal with soups Hot and Sour being the Favourite then we jumped to the mains “Lo Han ” Spicy with Garlic sauce and noodles along with eggplant & bamboo shoot in soya chilli sauce that’s was fantastic above my expectations with Vegetable fried Rice !! Then surprisingly we had Fried Ice Cream Spring Rolls they were absolutely divine so I wished to meet the Chef and I appreciated him for our amazing dinner tonight , I will surely recommend this to all !! Special thanks to Mr. Nitish for giving us a great service , its a Very good option for family dinners or to have great time with friends !! Truely a fantastic experience will be coming again and again !

Yesterday I was there with a friend for the first time and for the last time . Below average food quality and taste even road side Chinese dhabas are better than this restra , then comes to service which was worst, even for water you have to remind them for three times , NOT AGAIN

Located above Ogaan, this lovely restaurant is spread over 2 floors with a small smokers balcony. Service was good and the food even better. The menu is limited but everything we ordered was good (except the cocktails - way too much sugar in those!). Must eat the spicy prawn moilee and egg-white appams!
Ideal place to dine after exploring the shops around the Hauz Khas Village. I had the set menu with the Malaber Paratha which was excellent. The desserts were good too.

Highly recommend this restaurant with the best appams and malabar paranthas. Delicious Goan fish curry, vegetable side dishes, and tandoori meats. Very good service and nice atmosphere. Definitely worth a visit.

I have been to Coast four times now and every time have had a great culinary experience. Since they started serving harder beverages, you can also now combine great food with a relativeky good and inexpensive cocktail. Having been with my Indian and foreign friends alike, I can report that very few come away from eating here without exclaiming the words "amazing, delicious, yum" at some point! If Keralan, especially fish, is your thing, this has got to be one of the best restaurants in Delhi to satisfy that craving.

I had a fantastic lunch here during my recent stay in Delhi. 3 of us ate here and we shared the Lamb Garam Curry, Masala Fish Curry and Vegetable Stew, all were delicious. There was a great selection of desserts on offer as well, especially the churros with some added cinnamon! To top it off the service and decor were excellent as well. I'd highly recommend this restaurant.

Really loved the food, the prawn curry, dry meat curry and aapams were amazing! It has also just got its liquor license.. so you can now have a drink too..! The building facade looks really good, loved the art, the food, a fantastic collection of deserts.. super chocolate cake and nutella pancakes! Highly recommend it for its super food and atmosphere! Excellent value for money. Lovely verandah.. for outdoor dining when the weather's better.

We came to HKV for another restaurant which turned out to be closed and were about to leave when the words "fresh juices" on the Coast Cafe sign caught our attention. Up we went to the light and immaculately clean Coast Cafe where the menu was varied, the servers knowledgeable, and the food very tasty indeed.  We ordered the fish tacos, the rocket, goat cheese and beetroot salad, the prawn curry and Malabar paratha. Oh, and juice! Huge glasses of beet, ginger, apple juice each -- cold and delicious -- really hit the spot.  The atmosphere in the cafe was sophisticated and the food very tasty indeed. Each dish was fresh and colourful, nicely presented, letting the natural flavours shine. The prawn curry in particular had a nice curry leaf note, not overwhelmed by the coconut milk and with just the right amount of mustard zing. Malabar paratha was flaky and not overly oily. Fish tacos had a good smoky chipotle taste -- would love to see this in crispy shells or even corn tortillas. The salad was also tasty and not overdressed.  A great little spot with a view of treetops, nice music, and clearly a well-thought out concept. We shall return!

What an amazing experience overall !!! It was my mother's birthday and really wanted the entire evening to be super-special. I am so glad I chose Kylin. I started with the Salmon Sushi and then ordered Fiery Crispy Lamb and Tepanyaki Prawn with Sticky Rice. Must appreciate Pascal (the chef) here. Each dish was cooked to perfection and lamb was 'oh so sinfully crispy'. What made the experience special, was Navdeep. Mr. Sanjay Keswani, please make a special note of my comment here and would REALLY appreciate, if you could personally meet and convey my supreme pleasure at the way he handled our table. Always alert and always available (even without having to call him). He really ensured that we were well attended to and any sort of appreciation that could be OFFICIALLY presented to him, IS WORTH IT. Mr. Keswani, do share my gratitude to Navdeep. I had ordered a cake for the occasion, but had forgotten to carry it to the restaurant. Navdeep came and asked us(even without me having to say anything), if it was a special occasion we were celebrating and promptly organised a Cheese Cake(on the house) for us. Time and money well spent and would definitely recommend the place to others who would like to go there. WOULD SURELY VISIT AGAIN.

This delightful oriental restaurant brings distinctive flavors from Thailand, Japan and China to the dining table. There's a teppanyaki grill, sushi bar and even dimsun is available along with a selection of cocktails and sake. The food is excellent and the service outstanding.

I haven't had a better meal in my life.  Kylan with kafir lime leaves in garlic and red chillies - sitting on top of a bowl of sticky rice - Made specially for me with star anise and jasmine. Mind blowing creation by Chef Pascal.  Jasmine tea gave me company- I spent 90 minutes here - all alone - reading at the Tepenyaki counter !  I loved every minute - thanks to Rajeev for his service - and Tepenyaki chefs Manvender and Pradeep for keeping me entertained with the sizzling sound of the Tepenyaki grill . Though I am vegetarian - but I meditated on the sizzling sound of food !
this is a a lovely chinese - Japanese option if you're staying at the holiday inn. the food is lovely, not cheap but very convenient.

Decent Fining Dining Experience at THE KYLIN, located on Ground floor of HOLIDAY INN HOTEL, MAYUR VIHAR, not quite far from South Delhi, Noida, East Delhi. It wasn't crowded unlike stand alone restaurants, so we had a very quite quality family time more than 2 hrs over dinner Interiors are good, though with dark theme- may be in sync with the THAI /CHINESE/ JAPANESE CUISINES, Music was not too loud. Lighting mood is Dim, tables are laid not quite close giving the luxury of some sort of privacy  Food is good, Helpings generous, prices not too exorbitant, Service is slick. valet parking available so no worries on that part  Overall a good place for cosy dine outs or get togethers

Getting There & Parking: Holiday Inn (Mayur Vihar) & Valet. Ambiance: The oriental decor is highly appealing and the defined seating is spacious.  Food: Strongly recommend - Malaysian Jungle Curry, Prawns in black pepper sauce with hakka noodles, bang bang angry chicken, nasi goreng, prawn tempura roll sushi and chicken coriander dim sums.  Service: Quick and courteous. I was guided well on suggestions till I figured what my palate likes.  Price: ₹3000 for two with semi priced alcohol. Overview: Will definitely spread a good word for these guys both in Delhi & in Chandigarh. Will take away half a point for potential improvements.

The word Brunch is a portmanteau of breakfast and lunch. Originating in England in the late 1800s, served in a buffet style manner, it has become popular all over the world. The first usage of the word can be traced to Guy Beringer’s article "Brunch: A Plea” in Hunter’s Weekly. The importance of a Sunday brunch is clearly stated in this article by William Grimes in the New York Times. Instead of England's early Sunday dinner, a postchurch ordeal of heavy meats and savory pies, why not a new meal, served around noon, that starts with tea or coffee, marmalade and other breakfast fixtures before moving along to the heavier fare? By eliminating the need to get up early on Sunday, brunch would make life brighter for Saturday-night carousers. It would promote human happiness in other ways as well. "Brunch is cheerful, sociable and inciting." Beringer wrote. "It is talk-compelling. It puts you in a good temper, it makes you satisfied with yourself and your fellow beings, it sweeps away the worries and cobwebs of the week." — William Grimes, "At Brunch, The More Bizarre The Better" New York Times, 1998 With life in the modern world getting faster, there is always a need for recharging your batteries for the week ahead. Corporate meetings, late night weekend parties and a sore hangover leaving you drained? Try the brunch at New York Café, Radisson Blu, Mahipalpur to spruce yourself up, physically and mentally. An open style seating giving you enough freedom to roam about the lobby coupled with the prompt and courteous approach of the staff make your Sunday afternoon something you are gonna look forward to the whole week. The spread alone sends your salivary glands into a overdrive and your mind into a tizzy. Drawing from innumerable cuisines, their chefs bring out the flavours from all over the world. The roast chicken with roasted veggies and the tenderloin in a red wine reduction with a truffle mash invigorate your senses. Soft juicy meat, crispy vegetables and a flavourful jus. Add to that the smell of truffle from the mash, you got a recipe for success.  Roasted Chicken with veggies The middle Eastern section houses a selection of breads along with the different kinds of relishes and artichoke, eggplant and bell pepper toppings to boost.  A selection of cold cuts accompany their wine menu. There's the Cadet D'Oc, white and red from Mas Rabell and a sparking Mirapiana Although their Indian menu does fall a bit behind with their Biryani falling a bit short of the mark, but the chaat corner makes up for it with some finger licking fare.  Their Pan Asian impresses with a Vietnamese lemon chicken, Prawn sushi and Salmon roll made perfectly. There's some pungent wasabi, salty soya and sweet ginger to accompany the decadent sushi. The desert section compiles Indian and European delicacies in one single place. A dropping chocolate fountain draws sighs from everyone around.  The rasmalai from the Indian section while the plum cake and the vanilla éclair impresses the most.  The vanilla éclair has a crispy pastry filled with sweet vanilla flavoured cream that rocks your pulsatory and gustatory systems at the same time. All this at 2600 INR, Radisson Blu pulls out all stops and puts out a buffet which can leave you spoilt for choices. Excellent taste with attention to detailing. This place will keep you coming back for more.

Courteous staff, prompt service and delicious food is what nyc stands for . Have been visiting the place for over a year now , each time there is a feeling of being at home with the service you receive from the staff . Mr Arun Tyagi remembered dads birthday on seeing us dinning at the place and surprised us with dads favourite cake and that really touched my dads heart , things like these make you come back to this restaurant over and over again . It's not about this gesture but going the extra mile and creating a bond and customer staff relation which matters . I would recommend people a must visit to this restaurant.

The "Coffee Shop" at the Radisson Blu Plaza is an excellent place for a meal. Apart from a very extensive breakfast buffet, their ALC offerings for other meals was also. excellent. We ate pizzas one evening and Chinese the next: both were excellent. Though the restaurant models itself on a Manhattan eatery, the staff are actually very friendly!
Food was excellent except thai curry. Special mention to host Nitish Rana, he was admirable and helped out choose our menu and drinks with ever smiling expression.

Located on the ground floor of Holiday Inn, Kylin is an Asian restaurant with authentic Japanese and Thai being their speciality. The fine dine has an aura that expensive and classy places carry along especially due to the subtle interiors here. Without wasting much time, we began our meal with their in-house specialisation, the Sushi. We had a variety of them and consumed them in all possible variants from Chicken to sea food to vegetarian. The sushi here is fantastic and cost you good money. Apart from the sushi, the 'Ginger Prawn' Dimsums and the 'Chicken Corriander' Dimsums were great. The distinguishing flavours of these Dimsums is what makes the restaurant an authentic one. The 'Chicken Satay' too was amazing specially with the peanut dip. The 'Clay Pot' chicken for the main course was again good. Finally, the best came in the form of a dessert. 'Chocolate Mah-Jong Ice Cream' was an engaging affair, a warm soft brownie, dipped with hot chocolate and served with Icey cold vanilla icecream. You got to love this. The Kylin Experience does not come cheap. For the fantastic food they are offering you and for the ardent service from their end, the place does cost you a bit. However, despite being in a five star hotel, Kylin Experience comes way under your budget when you compare it with the many other restaurants in a five star.

The idea was to ditch the city and hit somewhere calm and composed and what better than 'The kylin Experience at the holiday inn, mayur vihar'. The restaurant opens its doors to an extensive bar on the right and the tables laid on the left, extending further into another, quite an accommodating hall. We were five of us, made a visit over for a lunch, we skipped the salads and soups and straight went ahead with the appetizers. We had ordered- CHILLI GARLIC FISH WOK TOSSED CHICKEN CHILLI GARLIC BABY POTATOES AN ASSORTMENT OF VEG and NON VEG DIMSUMS AN ASSORTMENT OF SALMON, PRAWN and CRABSTICK SUSHI For mains we had ordered- CHICKEN HAKKA NOODLES CHICKEN PAN FRIED NOODLES CHILLI GARLIC SOLE FISH For desserts we had- CHOCOLATE MAHJONG WITH ICE CREAM GREEN TEA CRÉME BRULEÈ FRIED ICE CREAM SPRING ROLL All in all, The entire culinary experience was quite an overwhelming one. I'm sure to give this place a second hit very soon for it's delightful experience!

I went to Kylin experience for a fancy lunch. Overall I liked the 5 star experience and loved the ambience of the place. Ambience- 5/5 They had outdoor area as well. Service-5/5 very quick Value for money- 5/5 For a luxury experience, this place is recommended. Food - 4/5  In food I ordered the following items:  ✔Garlic chilli fish- Amazing, very well battered. ✖Wok tossed chicken with bullet chillies- The chicken was rubbery but I liked the flavour and spices. ✔Baby potatoes- Super tasty ✔Dimsums assorted- It had all types of dimsums, I loved the chestnut fillings. ✔Sunrise- Must order mocktail, not too sweet, not very sour. ✔Fruit bloom- It was a milk based drink, Very tasty ✖Virgin mojito- It was too sweet ✔Guava drink- It was very refreshing and tasty ✔Dark surprise-It was a ginger plus coke drink, I loved the flavour ✔Ice tea- Served in a big tall glass, chilled. I loved the taste. ✔Aspharagus avacado sushi- It was light and authentic. ✔Crunchy veg sushi- All the sushis were tasty, served with sauce. ✖Pork belly bao- I found it very dry ✔Steamed fish- Flavoured in curry, I tried 2 curries and loved them, Highly recommend Fish here.  ✔Pan fried noodles- They were full of vegetables, I loved the flavour and chunks of chicken in it.  ✔Fried ice cream spring roll- Quite unique and tasty  ✔Creme brulee(Greentea flavour) - I totally loved the taste. ✔All the desserts- Chocolate pastry, thins, everything was perfect in desserts. ✔Chicken dimsums- They were stuffed with super tasty filling, Highly recommended to order this.  Overall I liked the place and would love to visit and try other dishes as well. ✔Will go there again.

Kylin is situated at ground floor of spectacular holiday inn.It specialises in Pro pan Asian cuisine. Chinese,Thai and Japanese lovers can head here for some great ambience and seriously good food. We spent an afternoon here with a gang of friends over a laid back lunch. To start with we tried "mix vegetable and lotus stem" siew mai.  These were beautifully crafted,seriously good,flavorful and juicy.Never thought vegetarian options of dimsums can be that good. The vegetarian sushi had options of avocado and asparagus.Both tasted divine. "Pork belly bao" looked colorful and flavorful.The presentation is awesome.It tasted average though. Then was "Chicken coriander" crystal dimsums which were one of the best that I've ever tasted.The chicken meat was low on spices...just seasoned and it had a bite.It complimented with accompanying sauces. Next was my personal favourite "garlic chilli fish" It was perfectly cooked.Best part was that it was very thinly coated so it was perfectly crisp and made to perfection. "Wok tossed chicken with bullet chillies" was interesting and a good option for us as a substitute of chilly chicken.  "Chicken pan fried noodles" were showstopper.Perfectly done curried noodles with heaps of chicken chunks and crispy vegetables were a delight to have. "Kung pao chicken" and "hakka noodles" were our all time favourites for the main course. Desserts are unforgettable.  "Green tea creme brulee " is highly recommended.Feather light and caramelized or torched from the top,this creamy preparation is worth all calories. "Ice cream spring rolls" were yumm and interesting take on fusion.The crunchy rolls were filled with ice cream and sprinkled with honey and nuts. We also had following mocktails: Earl grey ice tea: a green tea based iced tea which was refreshing and not too sweet. Ginger ale: a basic all time favourite. Overall it's a vibrant place with tastefully done interiors.Its reasonably priced as compared to high end Pan Asian restaurants in same league. The place is great to enjoy it's ambience,a laid back lunch or dinner,light music and the company.

I really enjoyed eating Prawn sushi and Salmon Sushi. To the once who doesn't know every Sushi doesn't have raw salmon so you can any day try their Sushi letting them know you don't want raw meat. The sushi was not tearing apart and the meat was fresh. Tom kha soup is coconut milk base soup the texture of the soup is smooth and coconut milk was not at all over powering the taste. It had nice fragrance and fresh veggies. It was the best prawn dimsums which I have tried. The Prawns were fresh and juicy. Taste was delicious. I tried Chocolate Mazo which is a dessert. Being a chocolate lover I really prefer dark chocolate or a chocolate which is not overpowering my pallets. Its a must try dessert. Fried ice cream is another unique and delicious dessert. It's a spring roll filled with ice cream and you need to gulp it in one short. Star Anise Ice-cream was a wonderful experience as it was so beautifully made I really loved the flavour. Hotel is beautiful and even the poolside. For stay and dinein for Thai and Japanese it's the best dine in place.

Visited kylin, Last weekend with my  colleague as we entered in the holiday inn hotel, there is a big, beautiful and decory hall on ground floor and in the right there is way to enter kylin restaurant  .  We entered thorough the gate and a person  greet us there and  there is everything in Japanese and chinese style. Lights on sealing, a big hall and so many chairs, sofa's with table. We can see a live kitchen from our seats.  Lets come to food which is my fav part . Basically kylin serve Japanese and Chinese  food. So we started from sushi, We ordered special sushi platter in which they have around 4,5 variety of sushi's pieces. The color of sushi was so beautiful. We tried  sushi at very first time and must say they really did a good job. The salmon sushi is my favorite highly recommend if you are also a sushi lover. It cant be wrong if i say kylin serve one of the beat sushi in delhi or moreover. After this we had chicken tikka saatay. Chicken is something wch never disappoint  you.The tikka was yummy less spicy but   quality and presentation is good i will add pictures below.  After chicken satay We tried. Stir fried noodles and five spice chicken. The noodles  is so yummy as expectations as noodles concept came from china and Japan. Noodles size was fine taste was delicious. Presentation in a bowl and with 5 spice chicken it taste become double and so tasty. This is also a highly recommend dish to my all foodies. We tried their mocktails, all are good and refreshing you will not dissapoint and what good with them is. They explained the drink ingredients  in menu so it will be easy for order accordingly.   Let come to my fav part,thats is dessert. We had kylin chef specials  dessert plate it contain 3 items. Fried spring roll with vanilla icecream n honey. Thats deserve 5 star  rating. Second is chocolate cake that make my day .. and taste so good  and satisfying. Third is icecream with a special powder ingredient.   Recommendation - Please introduce italian food in menu it will be great. Rest maintain  what ever is there  is perfect.   My recommendation to foodies - Must try their chicken dishes n dessert are highly  recommend, you can thanks me later

what an amazing place to be . i went there with my friend few days ago . kyline experience is beautiful ,classy . interior are awesome . they serve authentic Chinese , thai and Japanese food. overall i like the experience and loved the ambaiance of the place . they also have live making food thing we ordered sushi platter - there are 4 different type of sushi . it was a perfect plater for any sushi lover . taste was good . presentation was nice dimsums - perfect authentic dimsum . taste good very well prepared chicken satay- fresh chicken piece well marinated and cooked wok tossed chicken with bullet chilies- fired chicken peice with some red chilli and black paper garlic chilli fish - fried fish with some honey chili flavor stir fried noodles - taste good five spice chicken -fresh and nice dessert fried spring roll- vanilla ice cream filled roll fried chocolate cake - taste was awesome .

Visited Holiday Inn Mayur Vihar today for lunch with my friends. I was confused which one to go for Kylin or Cafe on 3.  I had Asian food months back so we all decided for Kylin. Interior and ambience of Kylin is awesome. We started with Prawn, asparagus and crab sushi.  Sushi here are very good. I use to hate crab sushi from my past experience but today I came to know how good crab sushi can be made. One of my friend is dimsums lover aglfter debate on what to order we ended up ordereding three types of dimsums: Chicken, Corn(veg) and Prawn. All were good. At the end we ordered fried icecream this was somethi g unique which I had for the first time icecream inside a spring roll. It was WOW!!. Next in dessert, I had Brownie with vanilla icecream. Chef dhondup came and asked for a feed back.  Overall my experience was amazing. Will visit cafe on 3 some other day.

If you’re looking for a great fine- dining experience and sumptuous oriental cuisine, think of Kylin. I recently visited this restaurant for dinner and had a really nice time. I had an elaborate meal complete with a dessert. I went with a friend, who tried some non vegetarian dishes as well so I am going include a mini review based on his experience here. To start with, we ordered the sushi platter with asparagus sushi and prawn tempura with wasabi mayonnaise. Absolutely loved it. The asparagus and prawn tempura was nicely rolled in a pillow of rice and the wasabi was great. After the sushi, we moved to trying out their dimsums. We had the tofu and vegetable and mixed veg with lotus stem dimsums from the vegetarian selection. The dimsums were delicious but slightly over cooked and too delicate making it difficult to eat. From the non veg selection we had the chicken dumpling in sesame chilli soya sauce and that was delicious too. We also requested for a small portion of baby potatoes in crushed pepper and garlic chilli fish which was tasty and prepared well. Coming to the mains, we tried the veg Thai green curry, stir fried vegetables in fragrant garlic sauce, veg hakka noodles and chicken satay. I felt that the Thai curry was a bit watery though it tasted fine. Further, the mains in general lacked the authenticity of proper oriental food and were kind of street styled. The chicken satay was however delicious. To conclude our meal, we had the homemade ice-cream trio in interesting flavors – basil, star anise and coconut. The ice cream was rich and creamy. Overall had a good experience. The restaurant is extremely spacious with a smoking zone. I however felt that in terms of the look and feel of the place, Kylin has better outlets. Staff was courteous and friendly and service was good. I absolutely love Kylin and I will definitely visit again.

This by far is one of the most clean and quite cafe's in delhi. We visited for a tea break and find the place refreshing. The decor was pleasing and the ambiance calm. The place is very clean and the service was excellent. Looking forward to a lunch or a dinner here.
We ate at Coast Cafe on our last night in Delhi. The food was delicious, such a nice break from heavy Rajasthani food, decor was quaint and service was very attentive. A lovely evening!

Great ambience, good range of coastal cuisine - especially from Kerala/ Goa/ Mangalore. Excellent service by very courteous staff who are eager to please. Portions are not very big - but there are interesting food choices.

I am just so happy to write about Kylin - it makes you feel at home with a lot more luxury. the manager Faisal who made us feel so amazing and special each time I visited Kylin- Ive been to Kylin with my family, colleagues and friends. He ensures that Pascal who is the worlds greatest cook (he knows just the right amount of salt and spices to mix to suit our taste buds) makes food which is delicious, fresh and full of aroma which triggers your appetite. the young boys who served us Manish/Rajiv -thankyou for your hospitality. Its become my fav place and I shall visit it again very soon. Yogi thank you for taking special care for my loved ones.

Good food and nice ambience. Service is inconsistent. Recommendation is to sit next to the grill and you will be served well. Avoid tables next to rear exit as people frequently come in or go out (for smoke too) and becomes a distraction.

Went to Kylin twice in the last 2 weeks- and came back extremely satisfied . The j Kafir lime spring roll and Kailan on sticky rice is awesome. My colleagues from the US gorged on the Lamb Bulgogi. The home made ice creams are to die for - especially the basil and anise. The green tea creme Bruce here is a must try.  Special thanks to Chef Majumdar, Chef Pascal and Faisal for taking such good care of us.

kylin has good food they have good variety of pan asian food ....there sushi is also best tuna sushsi is just awseomw ....... main course are very delectable s well ... highly recommended not very pricey too
Brought back amazing memories from the backwaters in Kerela and the service was spotless. Restauranteurs in Delhi should come here and learn what it takes to be called a good restaurant.

Amazing Ambience : Perfect Service : pocket friendly & mind boggling food : fish tachos : rocket & goat cheese french lentil salad : appams vth chicken curry : prawn curry : obagine kokam curry : great desserts : carrot cake : tiramassu : & masala coffee & perfect rose tea !! Cheers for such heavenly food .. Must Go !!
The restaurant server great sushi. Tried many different types of sushi but they where all good. Green curry is also lovely. Staff is friendly.

I went for dinner with a whole bunch of cousins. It was a normal outing on a weekend. I don’t like eating anything heavy for dinner. I generally avoid Indian food. That’s why I like Chinese, because it sits lightly on the stomach. The best part about Memories is that it customises its food for you. So, I had a dish of broccoli and mushrooms in a nice soy bean sauce along with fried rice. It was made perfectly to our taste. I like it a little spicy, but not too spicy. And, it is some innovative spirit that has provided food for thought to an ever-changing and there drinks went great with the drinks . Thanks to Chef S.T. Lepcha for this great efforts and yummy food . a must plan for family dinners Kylin at Mayur Vihar
The food was simply excellent. And theyve done up the place really well. It was a thoroughly enjoyable experience.

See and be seen in this beautiful, stylish cafe. Small bites plates were tasty but pricey.  Cigarette smoke pouring in from the balcony made it hard to taste all the complex flavors in my sprout salad with kimchi.
Loved the experience having great coffee on the terrace, usually for smokers .. Had one of the better Cheese Cakes at The Perch.. The young Promoter had a great smile.. The staff is ever helpful..

You can walk past the entrance to Perch without realising it but my eagle-eyed companion saw the sign and an attractive door with plants on it. The entrance is up a steep flight of stairs (no wheelchair access). Inside, it's all blond wood, white walls and more plants.  Service was swift, the prosecco cold and the food delicious: buffalo mozzarella salad with cherry tomatoes, red onion, asparagus and olives; kimchi lotus hearts; pea, corn and spinach pappardelle. They even made a good flat white.  This would be a great place for brunch but I didn't see any brunch-looking dishes. We perched on high stools by the window - where we could look down on shoppers. Another floor up (another flight of steep stairs) is an open air room.  This is also where you find the toilets, behind mirrored sliding doors. The doors don't shut completely - there's a 5mm gap, which is a bit disconcerting. The normal latch on my toilet door didn't work so I had to slide a bolt at the top. It was quite difficult to get out! The toilets and the accessibility are the only reason I can't give Perch 5 stars.

The newly-opened 'Perch' Wine and Coffee Bar in the middle-lane of Khan Market in New Delhi is a delightful two-floor bar coming as a breath of fresh air with its spartan cafe atmosphere and and a great range of wines and beverages. It's airy, well-lit, with comfortable seating and an oak-wood look interior. Service is prompt and the salads, cheese and sandwiches go incredibly well with a glass of wine at any time of day ! Ideal for a quiet chit-chat or a business meeting.

I was hesitant to go here, as we were not particularly going for coffee or wine. But I was told that the food was good. I was not disappointed. The Staff was extremely friendly and pleasant. The service was fast and excellent, and the food was great. The prawn salad had great flavors. I had the lamb which was soft and succulent. The fish also was very good. There was really nothing to complain, and I loved almost everything there. topped mostly by the extremely friendly and helpful staff . The size of the helpings was fair. The prices were not cheap, but worth the good food.
Really nice restaurant in Haus Khas.  Food and service were both very good - it was the best meal I had in India!

I have arranged a dinner buffet for my husband as it was his Birthday and I just wanted to make it happen smoothly in absence of myself. I couldn't have ever done it without Mr. Amar Singh Rajawat (Restaurant Manager) and his team. He was assisting me to make the reservation and handled every related tasks by being very prompt and attentive. He has arranged a small celebration session which was completely a surprise! He is indeed setting up a great example for his team in terms of service exellence and go that extra mile to make the guest perfectly happy! The hospitality industry is emerging because of the wonderful people like you! Heard the food is really scrumptious and high in quality. Thank you very much for and we both will again hope to visit you!
Enjoyed buffet breakfast, lunch and dinner. Variety and choices in the food available here is nice. Staff also very courteous.

kudos to the founders - very cosy place, nice menu, tasty food. greatest surprise was to discover decent indian red wine by a reference of local sommelier. place is not cheap: ice tea, vietnamese coffee, glass of wine, chicken salad, baked french cheese, roasted chicken breast and a desert got us a bill of 4k rupees (taxes inclusive). banana sponge with caramel ice-cream was a huge success, absolutely awesome! highly recommend!

This place was on our bookmark list for a long time and we finally got a chance to visit this beautiful place. AMBIENCE: Very decent, calm, soothing and definitely mesmerizing. They are on the first floor with stairs leading to second floor which is bifurcated to provide space to to a small terrace. The balcony area is mainly for the smokers community who can enjoy their drink along with a smoke. They can sit, relax, spend some quality time with their acquaintances and smoke away their worries. We chose to be seated on the inside. The first floor gave a look of a lobby area of someone's home. once there, you are bound to get a homely feeling and feel relaxed instantly. We also loved their bar area on the second floor. DRINKS & FOOD: We had chicken and cheddar melted gougeres which tastes amazing. However the size of the quantity of the same was disappointing. One of the factors which they can look into. the taste of the same were amazing. Just a heads up, have them quickly as they can turn soggy soon. We ordered rose wine which was the highlight of the event. though they are famous for their coffees too but we preferred the wine and we loved the experience to the core. they know the glasses in which various wines are served and we just couldn't be more glad! Overall an amazing and splendid experience.

Friendly and helpful staff, good atmosphere, but the food is lacking. A colleague who ordered a meal with kimchie was unable to find any trace of it. Safer bet is to order something uncomplicated such as fried chicken.
Went here with some friends for dinner - great decor, really really tasty food and friendly service. Strongly recommend for good coastal fare in HKV!

The menu is relatively small but I was told that they change it once every few months. They serve European food. We were a group of 7 people so we ordered almost everything on the menu and everything was awesome. The produce was of great quality and all the dishes were very flavourful and subtle. The cocktails were exceptional. The desserts were also great. I would recommend this place as a must visit in delhi.

A couple of visits down, you can tell this place has a enjoyable vibe to it! With a wide variety of exotic wines and Ethiopian coffee selection, the restaurant definitely has something to offer every patron.  Although the menu lists creative dishes, it needs more variety for repeat visitors. The food seems to be a little generous on the oil and needs to be fixed.  The must try vegetarian creations: Lavash sticks with tomato salsa Wild Mushroom Risotto  Avocado & Palm Hearts Salad All in all, worth a visit!

Right upfront I'll tell u what I will set out to justify. Excellent place. Good food. They know what they are serving. Wine and choice of drinks are also nice. Would I go back. Yes. With a baby. Maybe not cos of the hi seating.  The chicken was cooked very well and did not feel odd. The salad spread was all but excellent in its preparation. Had a chicken main course which I don't recollect but loved it.  Go here and just cos it may not be crowded don't be worried. It's classy and superb...

So if u have been to PCO in Vasant Vihar and liked it. You will love this place to coz it has been opened by one of the co-owners. Attention to detail is spectacular. Some good starters and excellent wine collection. The seating a bit stiff specially somebody like me with a back problem can't sit there two long. But if u looking for a good evening not to buzzy it's the right places. Good for a second date. If u have visitors from abroad good place to take them for a relaxed evening! Have fun enjoy!
Second visit to this resturant . One of the best appams i have had in town . Served with a side order of veg stew . Food is good and has maintined standard. Service could improve a bit but
Coastal cafe situated right above Ogaan in Hauz Khan village is a fine dining place which serves amazing coastal cuisines. Best place for non veg lovers.

Had gone to Hauz Khas for the first time; Happened to have Lunch at Coast Cafe Although it was peak time but got good table The food and atmosphere was great, our food was all vegetarian Presentation and service excellent Given a chance will again visit
Very good restaurant in a nice setting. You will get an interesting variety of fares. Not so cheap though. Better to reserve in advance.

One of a kind coastal cuisine! I being a vegetarian cannot do justice while writing this review about a restaurant serving great coastal sea food but, they have a nice vegetarian menu as well! Tried out their malabari kokum veg curry which tastes delicious with malabari parantha and Appam.  Ambiance is very cosy. One can enjoy this comfort food along with a good company!

This brilliant gem is where the Haus Khas village begins...on the second and third floors of Ogaan..the third floor had a raucous group from Google, engaged in loud and noisy banter..the restaurant had reserved our table on the second floor..it could not have been more perfect, in a cosy corner of the calmer and quieter section of the restaurant..the food was excellent: we had Malabari kokum mixed vegetable curry, Kerala fish fry, mustard tossed home fries, the mutton sukha fry, rainy day stew, and ofcourse appams and malabar paranthas...all to die for yummy...not forgetting the warm sticky toffee dessert!! A good selection of wines, Italian and French and ofcourse Indian as well. The ambience is cool, chic and very very nice... a very special birthday treat !

This place is always buzzing whether breakfast, Lunch,Dinner or Snacking, Verity of options on buffet spread to suit all tastes. Need a good 90 mins to do full justice.  Our dining experience was amazing. Anirudh & Manager Amar Singh Ji were such a wonderful host. They served us with such kindness and joy that the whole experience became unforgettable. Chef ( forgot the name- tall Chef) extra care in serving us and his preparations were worth repeating. I'm sure we're going to come more often.
Was here for dinner ..booked table via dineout ...had a great service experience ...were served by Sumit and Karan .. would look forward to be here again ...thanks
Nice to have food over here. Water bills awas also very good thanks to the chef nikunj. Thanks to Annu malik for the taking care of our services. Will see you soon

I am thankful to sudhir to giving us well quality of professionalism, and really i appreciate his eyes contact on my table. I hope he is good assets of your restaurant, who never loose your customer, it was my great experience overall in my all visiting, all credits go to the superman in your restaurant sudhir.

I came with my family to celebrate the birthday of my son,it was excellent hospatality, and the chef who came on my table he was so kind and helpful chef pardeep kumar, and he offered Kaley chana ke tikki, it was amazing, but my server sudhir he was also good, we enjoyed the buffet.

I am really thankful to sudhir and chef Pardeep in live kitchen and Karan Singh. Amazing service and food was ausome specially mong Dal Ka Halwa and pasta was made by chef it was delicious.Thank you so much every one, come soon.

The food at dinner was scrumptious and at the same time variety was in place. Enjoyed the service by Rajnish and team who is all there to serve you to the best of their abilities .  Great ambience too I am a frequent visitor and would live to visit again !
Had a great dinner here and a wonderful breakfast buffet. Not your normal fare. Enough selection to please any nationality. Both buffet style and made to order.

Did an overnight stay after a 2 week Motorcylce tour. The restaurant food in the evening buffet was fantastic, such quality and flavours. So much variety to please anyone. This continued into breakfast too. What made me take the time to write the review was the guy and lady who looked after the front of the restaurant in the morning. They were well presented and so attentive without going over the top. Really really nice and a great way to start the day. Well done your staff are a credit to you.
Brilliant experience with the food, hospitality and ambience. Very nicely taken care by Sumit and Deepak.
The starters were good and awesome. The food was also tasty, however expected a little more. The service was great

I am thankful to Mr . Amar to giving us well quality of professional ,and really I appreciate his of eyes contact on my table ....!Thank you so much for suggesting for a good food , we really enjoyed well ....! See you soon ,And will recommend to others friends as well to visit.!!

Went to this place on a Saturday night when they had a live band. The place has a English cafe like feel with all the wooden furniture. I settled at the bar and ordered a cocktail, Bourbon toast, it was well made. So was the Welsh Rarebit but it came out cold with the bread being slightly chewy, but it paired fine with the drink. We neither tried coffee nor had wine at this place. Some other time perhaps.
Another Khan market special place.Nicely perched on the outer side and well laid out on several levels. Was not crowded so had a choice of tables. prompt service and good medley of food.
Nicely done up, this wine bar is a great addition to Khan Market. We could do with some more wine bars though we do need to have a more extensive wine list at Perch too....

In the trendy Khan market the doorway adorned with (droopy) fresh herbs leading to the first floor does not reflect the sparkling white washed walls, pine wine cellared establishment that lies within. Great wine list, cocktails and coffees but food as well that far exceeded that we tasted at the 6* Imperial Hotel. As a chef I understand the full process of sourcing ingredients, the attention to detail to get a stunning dish on the plate. Head chef Abhishek Pathak and his team have got this just right. Tremendous variety from Indian warm salads through to Welsh rarebit. Small bites, salads, small and large plates. Extraordinary food presented with flair and a gentle yet very attentive waiter. Don't be persuaded by those restaurants that have paid many rupees to get a mention on the tourist map of the market - this is the real deal. GO!!

I had heard a lot about this place and its ambience. I visited the place on 26th January so wasn't able to taste the wine but instead ordered loads of food and everything was simply delicious! The Chicken salad had lots of flavour and it was really fresh. The different textures in the dish made it scrumptious.  The star of the evening was the Braised Lamb Shank. It was to die for. The lamb was falling off the bone and the accompanying puree and jus were a perfect fit. It is a must place, not only for the wine, but for the flavourful food.

Food you get here will be of Italian kitchen, we ordered grilled asparagus n mozzarella and palm hearts and butternut squash for starters. Palm hearts salad is a nice option to go for if you are a salad person and asparagus with mozzarella is even better. As for mains we had asparagus risotto, grilled halloumi and rosemary baked camembert. Asparagus risotto was astonishing, probably the very best dish of the whole dinner we had there. Grilled halloumi was also succulent but nothing as compared to risotto, but the camembert was astounding. Banana sponge with honeycomb ice cream was served for the desserts and also we had a white wine n coffee sangria.

Very nice and classy place to hang out at in khan market. The view from the window is also good. The place is small and cosy yet offers privacy. There is lot to chose from wine. The low point is the food. they have nothing great to offer in food section and the portions are very small and no that tasty. Over all its its a feel good place minus the food

I come to Delhi a lot for work and hate it. Perch makes me hate Delhi less. It's a fabulous break from the in your face attempts at customer service and is actually what a lot of people are looking for, a place to have some good food and drink without being lectured by your waiter or having him up in your face all night.

The Perch is a lovely, quiet place by day - low-key, comfortable and full of natural light. I loved the big picture window view of Khan Market and the simple wooden furniture within. I had the grilled asparagus with couscous and it was light and flavorful (a little bread would have been a nice touch!).

This place is good for its cheese and salads. While the decor and ambience was aimed to be created like a cafe - and they have done a fair job with it - it can become like a bar with too many people sometimes. The problem with that is there's just not enough space to accomodate too many drunk people.Then you don't know what its supposed to be!!! Don't go to the upper floor and you will be better off if you just want a quiet meal and conversation. I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of the cheese served, was very good, coffee sangria is recommended as well. They have an ok wine collection. It's just small and cramped for space which can ruin your experience on a busy day.

Felt like a cafe in Goa or Pondicherry with white walls, sketched portraits, wooden decor, light music, etc. The place does a great job for a relaxing weekend evening or brunch. The menu is also good. Will like to visit again....
ovely place in khan mkt, loved the wine and salads.. Gave a greaat european feel to the interiors.. Amazing service, great place to hang out with friends

The cafe though isn't too big but is the real place for you to sit & have your lovely coffee and wine over a chat or while even working on your laptop. I was here on a frnz suggestion and I can never forget their delicious double chocolate parfait. You just don't want to miss this desert here.

this place will take you into a whole new level! so different from what you normally see in Delhi.. this closed hidden away Cafe' boasts rustic elegance with white walls and wooden furniture. the contemporary deco will put you at ease at once. i was particularly impressed that they grew there own micro greens !! 10+ points just for that!

Landed up here only for the coffee wine, ended up finding a great place to dine & hangout. Located at a prime location, it has a beautiful ambiance & siting. Mostly filled with young folks. The desserts & coffee wine are a highlight here

This place is one of its kind in terms of ambiance. Owner is helpful. We tried Goat Cheese Mousse Toast with Poached Pear and Coffee. Food was yum and fresh, presentation was perfect but portion was small, like really small. Coffee was Okey. Overall, it is a average place. Could be improved if portions are increased, else it is overpriced.

I went to their Vasant Vihar branch and love it. The food was great with an innovative menu and the restaurant gave real a cosy feel. A must visit is the hidden speakeasy bar Hoots which is run by the same management.

Great decor and excellent F&B menu for people who enjoy healthy and sensible eating. A refreshing change from other restaurants in Khan Market and I also love their Vasant Vihar location. A place I would like to visit for lunch and coffee and meet up with friends for catch-ups. Try the quinoa salad, lentil and sprouts salad and coffees!
Very near the Hauz Khaz gate. Good ambience and good service. Very good food. In case you like South Indian food - more of Kerala food, you can enjoy more here. Bit costly for the portions given.

My affair with Coast Cafe is new and original, a place which is a melody and as I and my friend are foodies, the best way to start a conversation is over drinks and some palatable food. The place was full when we arrived but lucky us, we got the table in 30 minutes. Coast Cafe is bright with ample amount of sunlight, the overall aura is positive and the gentry is impressive. Coast Cafe caters a finite menu including Continental and Kerala cuisine with drinks. Medleys in cocktails imbibed were Expresso Martini, and Bora Bora Margarita, a sunny afternoon couldn't be better. The bartender is not playing any Gastronomical game but he knows what he is creating. A bombardment of freshness and calmness soothes your mind when you have that first sup. We devoured Mustard Tossed Home Fries in Finger Food, long and thin, crispy and blazing hot. Main Course was simple - Malabari Cokum Mixed Veggie Curry with Appam and Beans Thoran as Kerala Veggie Side, the unification of all three made me wonder why did I never try Coastal food in South India when I was actually living there? I don't know why am I giving 4.5 rating to Coast Cafe, is it the homey feeling, the humble staff, the delicious food, the not-so-fancy but edgy drinks, or the company I had that day. Whatever, it was, Coast Cafe is now one of my GO-TO places and I'd like to see more of it.

Just before you enter into the hustle bustle and loud music of the HKV, this joint stands on 2nd and 3rd floor of the building#2. Should have sitting for around 50 people with a small balcony overlooking the Deer park.The food quality is good.

I live in Hauz Khas, and like to bring visitors to see the playground of Delhi's booming urban middle class youth. While many of the places can be fun, finding consistently high quality food in a nice atmosphere can be difficult. Coast Café walks that fine line between hip and relaxed what makes it my favorite warering hole. While staff maybe more attentive, food is excellent South Indian cuisine, and drinks are made to perfection. After numerous guest complaints, they seem to have finally stopped to deepfreeze the place. Great for friends, a bit too noisy for business meetings.

We went looking for a good, cost effective Malabari cuisine lunch, and this cafe was a very pleasant surprise. The appams were outstanding and the ambience was clean,cool, and relaxed.  The menu was small but well thought out, and quite adequate if you know what you are looking for.

We had dinner here after a long very hot day. Food was excellent, I had chicken pepper curry which was tasty and very filling with rice along with a refreshing Moscow mule served in a beautiful copper cup - albeit slightly weak in alcohol is was very nice. Only down side was service was slow and not all that attentive. We were sat down to a partially laid table which wasn't relaid for us at any point in the 30mins we were waiting for our drinks whilst all other tables around us got done. Would definitely recommend if your looking for something very relaxed.

I was strolling through Hauz Khas one evening with my mum and we chanced upon Coast Cafe. What a delicate, beautifully created ambience. We sat at one of the tables by the windows upstairs, and had savoury crepes, green tea and coffee in the sun. It's a great memory. I would love to go back when next in Delhi.

Walk into this place in Hauz Khas and you leave the hustle of the city behind. Inside lies a calm chic yet funky interior with a menu that tempts. The south asian parathas are so soft that they literally melt in the mouth. The meat dishes are really good and completely surpassed what we were expecting. This is a great place where you get to feed yourself well and feel great he next day - now thats a novelty in Delhi!

We have been regulars at this restaurant since quite sometime and I'm always impressed with the consistently good standard of the food! Must try the mutton sukha fry, the delicately flavoured fish moilee, black pepper prawns accompanied with the egg white appams. This time they even threw in a complimentary dessert! A nice gesture! If you like coastal Indian cuisine then you must give this a try! It's one of the best in Delhi!
Excellent atmosphere, impeccable service, particularly Sumit, good food selection of both western and oriental cuisine. Business centre availabile.

Really it was great time to have with the hotel, and with NYC I came for lunch with my friend and the service was provided by sushi and Ashoke it was amazing and thanks to Ajay as well. There was paneer tikka which I had it was amazing. Thanks to NYC team.

Traveling for work from Australia and good customer service experience from restaurants staff of NYC. Food was good and atmosphere was nice, special mention sudhir who was extremely professional and his eye for details was very good. Thank you so much for your great service.
Pleasure visiting  Annu Malik and Rajneesh is great host  Food and ambience is also really good Would love to visit again

We had lunch at NYC..Had a great and variety of food and taste.. satisfied and Anjana has treated us nice..Will surely plan next visit soon..This ambience made our day............... Great and passionate stafff

NYC is very beautiful and comfortable place specially for couples. We went there on valentine's eve.... We loved the food very much, they served us mouthwatering appetizers and verious verity of local Delhi street food. If you talk about food verity,it was huge and taste of all dishes was sooooo.....tasty. Buffet dessert counter was really very pretty with heart shape pastry,mousse and many more items, thanks to Chef for made our day with special dessert. Now talk about restaurant decoration it was out of the world,looks like romantic heaven on valentine's Day. The hospitality standard is top notch there. Manager Mr. Amar ji and Sumit made our day special. Thanks a lot restaurant chef Pradeep and Mr. Amar for lovely restaurant arrangements. We are regularly visit here because of you all..... Again 10/10 for overall experience.

It's simply brilliant, very difficult to fault this place great food superb presentation and informed service it's a near perfect combination, the decor is true to the place it's warm woody nice simple clean lines all fits in well. The champion is the food it's rare to say this but all that we ordered was great, would have given this place a 5 star and  though my friend paid I got a glimpse of the cheque.... It is a bit pricey

I am quite thrilled to be using "Delhi" and "wine bar" in the same sentence. Drinking in Delhi is expensive (in part due to the excessive taxation by the Indian state on imported alcoholic drinks) but I am happy to report that for a bar as slick as Perch (but with a very genial vibe) it is very good value for money. It features an adequate range of wines, and to some extent, international beers. I really liked the food as well - small portions, but with authentic flavours (unlike most other "continental" restaurants in India which take classic European dishes and suitably Indianize the recipes - fusion food at its worst). I liked the bar so much that I went twice in 3 days. Need I say more ?

A great find. Top class food and drink. Staff helpful and friendly. Gnocchi was my favourite. Not so busy of an evening which was surprising as was such a great spot (maybe was a post Diwali slow down) Lunch time seemed to be busier. Has a bit of an east London feel - in a good way! absolutely recommend Perch :)

This place brings some capacious and relaxed space for wine lovers to indulge while also offering very good food.  The service is great, the range is truly trans-national and the wine list is creative, contrasting and ecclectic.

Its sweet, curated little piece of 'somewhere in europe' Lovely place to catch up with friends over a nice drink or slightly over-priced coffee. But good coffee.Try the Vietnamese coffee. Nice selection of wines too.
For me its the best coffee i ever had. love the ambience. try sitting on the top floor. Like the handling of clients.

The gps navigation that misled the route resulted in us overtly searching a great deal in the old market area for this cozy cafe. Finally, we stumbled upon this lovely door at the entrance planted with saplings! After a brief climb we reached the first floor which opened up to the adorable cafe! starting with soup to dessert, they excelled in pretty much everythng! Service too was top notch!
The food was amazing, different and super tasty. The atmosphere was lovely and the whole setting makes you feel like you're somewhere in France.
