I visited this place recently and fell in love with the coffee here. forget costa and star bucks try the coffee out here. Food was nice and very authentic. Place is also nice and you can spend some quality time here.

We had a truly delicious lunch her (prawn and fish moilee with appam) and would thoroughly recommend to others. Some of the best South Indian food we've eaten outside of Kerala! Only marked down for being slightly slow with menus and delivering drinks to the table.

Our lookout for non-Mughlai food yet Indian food ended when our friends suggested that we try a meal at Coast Cafe. Located right near the entry area of the Village, the white building stating Coast Cafe cannot be missed. We started with mocktails (which were very average) and were wondering what to expect. But the main course was a pleasant surprise. The Sukha Mutton fry and the Fish with Kokum curry was splendid, and very different from the regular fare. To add to the pleasure were sides like Aapam and Malabar parantha.
Visited Coast Cafe twice in the last four months. I enjoyed it very much during my first visit. Great cocktails. I found the food and service to be better the first time. But it's a nice quiet spot!
serve a nice mix of starters & salads in a nice ambiance in the middle of Khan Market , service is Ok . serving takes a while
This place definitely soothes your parched throat! Plum Sangria is a must have! Chilled out place, quiet and calm ..I would recommend to pay a visit whenever at khan market 😊

Had heard about their great coffee. But, with a sweltering 43 C (110 F) outside, we were going to park that for a future outing. Armed with bags full of Khan Market “shopping” – trinkets, silver jewellery, Fab India kurta – my wife and I barged in and were lucky to get a table on their first level. The place was full. A bar. High chairs by the window, a mix of high and low tables inside. Wine bottles displayed on the wall.  Nice. My Fresh Lime Soda was perfect. The soda was chilled and bubbly, the lemons used were fresh, it had just the right amount of sugar. Sounds simple? Most restaurants and cafés fail to get this right.  The Coronation Chicken Salad perked us up. Pan-grilled chicken that was tender inside, generous strips, fresh croutons, cherry tomatoes and mixed lettuce, balsamic vinaigrette, italian herbs, and a sweet offset. But what had us swooning was the Shredded Pork Aglio Olio. The linguini al dente just right, strips of pork (well, not pulled, but I didn’t mind), little florets of broccoli, strips of bell pepper, spinach and generous drizzles of olive oil. Try it. Has got us optimistic enough to go and try their wine experience the next time.
Unassuming decor didn't prepare me for the delicious, tenderloin medallion.  Perfection on a plate!  Not cheap, but for the standard of cooking it was well worth it.

Vibrant wine & coffee bar has a striking European feel to it ,it's sets the mood for a romantic meal with its intimate ambience.The tall French windows with lots of potted plants overlooking the bustling khan market... Perfect place to unwind!!

Hidden in the back alley of Khan market on the first floor, it is difficult to find and is not differently abled friendly. The ambience is great...One can relax over wine although it gets crowded late evenings. Although it calls itself a wine and coffee bar, the Coffee options are limited. It mainly focuses on the wines wherein there is a wide variety but the prices are on the higher side. Also, there are few options for eating, with the emphasis being on accompaniments for wine.  Having read rave reviews, I had gone expecting a wider choice but returned disappointed.

I spent an evening with a friend visiting from the UK. Had coffee, nibbles and wine at this place. Was quite satisfied. The restaurant could be little noisy and the high chairs are a little uncomfortable.  Both the Latte and the banana cake that accompanied the coffee were very good. Wine is served by the glass as well as carafe.

This is a new restaurant in New Delhi. Food is very different and excellent. Worth a try for those which have not gone. The Bar menu is also excellent. Service is is good. The owner is very friendly. A very good experiance
As part of a new door concept store, it offers light lunches and a  good variety of drinks.Food is good, even the western dishes.It is a relaxing place during a busy day at shopping!

I must say Kylin is one of the best place for Chinese and Japanese food. Special thanks for Nitish for the hospitality and his recommendations for dishes. The food was absolutely wonderful, from preparation to presentation, very pleasing. We especially enjoyed the bar drinks, the dim-sums & Pan fried noodles were great.
Everyone in my group ordered something different and we all were extremely happy. I loved the curry!

We ordered Tacos and a Lamb Burger. The food was okay and the restaurant was clean. However, I was given an abysmal portion of four potato wedges - to order a further four will cost an extra 100 rupees. Four potato wedges for a portion is abysmal, terrible and so stingy its ridiculous. No complimentary water given. Service okay. Don't go here if you are hungry.

Had a delicious lunch with my partner, menu was delicious and both the dishes we had were well put together and warmly spiced. The interior has a stylish Nordic vibe. We tried the Spanish red and white wines by the glass which were very good.
Whether for a couple of for less intimate groups, this place is great to visit - the outside area is particularly nice.

Loved the vibe of the restaurant and wine bar. Well curated selection of dishes and small plates. While you will see people coming and staying for a while and sipping on wine or beer while constantly ordering some food, as well as, some drop in for a quick glass of wine to meet up with people in the evening or after work. The staff is excellent and very friendly.

I have eaten at more places in Khan Market than I have reviewed, and I will have to say, this is my new favorite! There are other notable, decent restaurants but this is flavorful, and wonderful find! The downstairs lounge was a bit loud where we had to wait for a bit before the table was ready. Its minimal in design but has a clean crisp look without looking sterile. It was bright without being overwhelming.  Upstairs was the same feel as downstairs. The wine list had a good selection and wasn't so overly priced that you don't want wine. There was a nice variety of specialty drinks as well. The Roasted Chicken was fall off the boney, moist and delicious.  The Gnocchi was firm with a great flavor. The Lamb Shank was the star of the show. The juices mixing with the rice, spices and flavors in the bottom of the bowl was bursting with flavor and a kick of spice. The Risotto was probably the best I have ever had. This is a place not to miss if you go to Khan Market, you will not be disappointed.

Went to this little bistro last week. Let me start by saying I love their decor. Very warm and unique. We had mulled wine, pink zinfandel and some lovely cocktails. All of them were rather nice. For food we had a roasted sweet potato, I would say given the price I can do a better job with sweet potato. We also had some salad and a gnocchi. The gnocchi was excellent. And salad quite good. The servers were very courteous and helpful, though the place was so busy that we almost didn't get our table. Overall a nice experience, though a little too expensive.
Very nice atmosphere and very good food! Popped in to kill some time and enjoyed it a lot! The large variety of dishes - good choice for expats!

With an amazing wine collection & a bar mixing cocktails just right, perch is definitely the place to be in Khan market. It has a super venue for all those who like the fine things in life, perfect place to take your time and enjoy the good things in life, coffee, a book, long conversations.

A pretty little bistro in Khan Market with great atmosphere. The menu is very adventurous but it works, in the sense that all the dishes we ordered were tasty and well presented. The same can be said for the drinks available, I particularly liked the Maharashtra red wine. Service was excellent, being pleasant but not intrusive. A really enjoyable evening!

Back again for the millionth time and why not. Well made food, great staff and very attentive, excellent spread and very tasty and healthy. Just so perfect and each time I feel they are getting better.
Great food and helpful staff, place looks cozy and clean! And last but not least the prices are fair.

Lovely little upscale restaurant in Hauz Khas. Nice menu with fish options (not common in Northern India). We had Kerala Fish fry and Chicken satay for staters. They were both delicious and nicely presented. The fish curry and prawn curry were very good as were the side dishes. We all loved the spiral bread (not sure of the name). Service is a bit stuffy but attentive enough. I wish we could have gone back for another meal here.

Had lunch and was very pleasantly surprised. I had the Goan curry which was delicious and perfectly spicy to my taste. Also had a chocolate cake and ice cream desert which was well done as well. Service was great and attentive. I have high standards coming from the San Francisco area and this restaurant was quite impressive.
We went to Coast Cafe for a light meal and to get away from the crowds in Hauz Khas.I had a really good spicy prawn Kerala style, with appam...but the real thrill was the Moscow Mule in a copper mug.
Coast Cafe offers very tasty Indian curries and fancy cocktails. It is located in the hip Hauz Khas Village and is a good retreat from chaotic Delhi. Do expect a somewhat bigger bill than elsewhere.

I found this place to be very good. Was selected by my son via Zomato and the food served was great. Unfortunately the recommended dish was not available (Eat with your hand Kerala grilled chicken) I tried Sukha (Dry) Mutton with paranthas (Indian Flat bread) and found it good

We were given a recommendation to Coast Cafe from complete strangers at the Dastkar Design Fair, and they were spot on! Soft, mini-tacos (3), quinoa salad and an exceptional Bloody Mary made my day! My husband had a pesto penne pasta, French fries tossed in mustard, and, of course, the Bloody Mary! These choices all had unique tastes for dishes that could have been "just the usual taste." That is the hallmark of a cafe worth your visit! Thanks Coast Cafe for being a restaurant that will make us remember New Delhi after we return home to Hawaii!

I must say they use best sauces and knows the recipe well !! Big five to hotel manager who let us know what we should eat as per taste !! He was very warm and polite. Loved his recommandation. We had a very sumptuous evening !!

Visited Nylon in mayur vihar Delhi . This is a amazing Resturant with great food but unfortunately the service in this place sucks. This can actually spoil your experience of otherwise a great restaurant. If you are willing to ignore the bad service, visit this restaurant for great Sushi and Fish Dishes.
Special thanks to Mr.Rakesh and Mr. Faisal for all the help and catering to our special requests all though our stay.
Great asian inspired food and fantastic staff. They had a buy one, get one free special on bottles of wine so we enjoyed that very much. The curries and the salmon were our favorite dishes.
Attentive Staff, Delicious Food, Overall an exciting experience. Recommended for both Friends and Family.
Good restaurants in this area are not so easy to come by.  This place is a good location for visitors.
Awesome place, for corporate dinners, we are regular user and I has always been a wonderful experience.

A nice little lunch restaurant hidden away on the second floor of a dress shop in the middle of Hauz Khas Village.  The salads are very tastefully prepared and they have some very unique non-alcoholic drinks like Masala Lemonade for the adventurous.

Nice clean restaurant with fresh decor. Tacos were "ok" fish and chicken in the tacos were over powered by chilli. But then again I'm from London and not a big fan of very spicy food. My 7 year old son wanted plain pasta without the sauce. The pasta was rubbery and had been burnt to the pan. However saying this the Tiramisu was to die for and the best I have eaten outside of Sicily and London.

We started our visit to Hauz Khas Village with lunch at this restaurant as it is rated very high on Trip Advisor. We had an excellent meal with well-prepared dishes both for our grand-kids and us grown-ups. The service was also excellent as is the ambience..simple, yet elegant. One of our group had diet restrictions which were very well catered to.

Tucked away in the entrance to Hauz Khas Village, is this little Cafe with a great ambience and food to match.  I've eaten there twice and each experience was wonderful.  The staff is friendly and the food amazing.

the food was great and to our taste authentic with a large variety. we stuck to the coastal fare which was excellent. the ambience was nice and the prices very reasonable , beer was chilled to perfection.
Now one of my favourite places to eat in all of india! Authentic Kerala cuisine with delicious Moolis, Appam & Kerala paranthas.  Great cocktails too!
This place makes everything well. They have a long waiting list, but the bar is pretty cool. Waiters sometimes don't exactly understand the food, but you can't go wrong with anything :)

excellent meal of appams and stew, the pink ginger root drink- reminded me of kerala. Food was lipsmacking and authentic all in all a great discovery. The mushroom pepper fry was a highlight of the meal for us. The ambience is soothing, relaxed and serene. what you may need after negotiating the traffic and ruckus outside.

We literally wiped the plates clean. The Kerala food is delicious. We had a Malabar kokum curry (forget the exact name) with appams, a peppery mushroom side dish. The chutneys esp one made of beetroot was so good my friend polished off the whole bowl (small). The drinks were delicious: a cucumber margarita and a non alcoholic watermelon one. The ice cream - the BEST lemony ice cream i have had. And the coffee ice cream - also excellent - you could taste the coffee properly. Will definitely go back...And the view from the 3rd floor - of tree tops - just blissful on a very sultry day.

Quite place....not really fine dining but close to it. Food is amazing - speciality is kerala food. Drinks are fab - you have to try the bora bora drink definitely. The best at Coast Cafe are the deserts...you can't ignore anything in desert menu. Churro and the chocolate pasrty is for sure suggestion.

The charming interiors of this restaurant make you feel welcome and relaxed. The restaurant has a nice young, cosmopolitan feel - though the food caters to old world nostalgia by serving authentic Kerala cuisine. I ordered an extremely well flavored vegetarian stew with aapams and though I am a frugal eater, who doesn’t jump at Kerala cuisine, I overate. For those who prefer to avoid the Indian fare, there is a range of interesting Continental options. I didn’t like my dessert though – which was an overly sweet chocolate cake. Overall, my taste buds are nudging me to go back.  If you want to visit during the weekend, its advisable to book ahead or go early and grab a table.

This was a short visit post a business meeting and had just a cooler, Fresh Orange and Basil burst which was nice refreshing and filling and chunks of orange was adding to the texture of the drink which had basil and mint leaves. The ambiance is amazing with subtle, understated luxurious layout, staff is responsive, view very beautiful overlooking the Hauz Khas greens, music was pleasant and temperature just perfect.  Place also offers a balcony for smokers. Will visit again to experience some highly recommended finger foods. Must try.

I really like all the outlets at Haus Khas village for the way they done them up. Huge options for a foodie like me. Only problem is getting in and getting out - mayhem specially on weekends Now the cosy outlet of Coast Cafe was just perfect for a quiet dinner. I had booked it beforehand, trying to avoid rush on a Sat evening. Ordered both chicken fry and mutton fry with malabar paratha. While the fries were average in taste the paratha was excellent. For drinks we ordered Moscow mule and Kafir Lime Tonic - both were very nice and light - great for a muggy evening. For main course we had malabari kokum fish curry with appam. This was a killer. Not had such great tasting Malayalee fish in a long time. Appams were just perfect - no other word can justify. Have to come back for more and the Kerala grilled chicken.

I thought the ambience was quite nice and the list of wine choices was great. They had so many options to choose from. I also really liked a lot of the food we had ordered to eat. We chose more of the starters and the sharing plates so we could taste a bit of everything and I enjoyed most of what I ate. I recommend this place to people that like and enjoy wine rather than for the food.

Had a lovely lunch here whilst in Kahn Market, it's a shabby chic type of place with quirky fusion food. Would recommend it as a cool eatery if you're looking for a mid priced meal in Delhi and don't want a classic Indian style. Loved the decor and the waiters were also very nice.

Ignore the grubby entrance in the alley between the shops in Khan Market. Walk upstairs where there are two levels to sit and eat. Top floor has a small deck where you can smoke. Good choice of food, very popular with locals and tourists. Young owner who is very charming and helpful

This is one of my favorite places in Delhi. I love to come here to have a glass of wine and some food, everything I've tried has been really good, though my favorite are the casava chips. Besides, ambience is pretty nice. It's a nice place to come solo or with friends.

Perch Wine and Coffee Bar has amazing ambience and courteous, efficient staff. The USP of this place, as said earlier is its ambience and decor. It has huge, wide windows, a balcony and a partly open roof for natural light to come in. I just had a cup of coffee, but was impressed by its airy dining area. The way they presented the bill was also unique- in a wine glass with coffee beans in it.
One of my favorite restaurants in Delhi.  Must try their fish curry with malabar parantha. Even vegetarian curries are also very tasty. Do not miss.

fan of the coast? fancy moroccon and kerela cuisine? is dining at a serene and beautiful place, enjoying some healthy and delicious preparations....your kind of thing? then Cost cafe is where you need to be. located right in front of the parking area, above ogaan is this beautiful, picturesque place. the white and blue seating, potted plants and the calm ambiance surely transports you to some coastal shack beside a beach. so on a sunny winter afternoon , i caught up with my girlfriends as this place and we chose to dine on the third floor, natural sunlight lit up the entire place, with a dreamy balcony, we knew we were in for a treat!! upon browsing we ended up ordering some well... really healthy options to kick off our new year resolution :p Our Quinoa Salad- a very healthy, light and refreshing option for those who want to keep a check on their weight, with quinoa, shredded arugula, olives, cherry tomatoes, pomegranate and feta. the salad looked very colorful and delicious. Moroccon grilled fish- its a complete meal in itself. a huge plate full of delightful components like grilled indian salmon/rawas with a tasteful moroccon spice rub, that is very earthy , slightly bitter, but certainly different served along with two warm tortillas, salad, chipotle sourcream and dill tzatziki. grab a tortilla, spread both dips, add some salad and enjoy with the delicious super moisy and flaky grilled fish!! Quinoa, beet and bean burger- yet another healthy option and boy let me warn you, the name might sound dull and weird but even the non vegetarians would love this burger. it was so delicious, i couldn't believe how much i relished that burger. the gorgeous deep pink and reddish colored patty was made of quinoa, roasted beetroot and kidney beans, which were lightly fried, topped with pickled zucchini, yogurt, mayo and caramelised onion, served along with potato wedges. Rainy day vegetable stew- now this was a dish that go mixed reviews. firstly the description says stew made with fragrant spices, carrots, beans and potatoes, which is supposed to be a mild curry, it was fragrant and mild alright!!! but the taste, looks and description suited more like their coast cafe vegetable moilee- made with coconut milk and vegetables- a sweet and spicy curry. we ordered appams along with it, which were very soft and light, the entire dish was served with three types of chutneys, tamarind, tomato and beetroot. for drinks we had mocha, which was good along with fresh orange and basil blast, it was freshly squeezed orange juice with basil leaves, tasted very refreshing, light, fresh and citrusy. i also got packed two of their desserts, banoffee pie- a crumbly biscuit crust with toffee sauce, banana, topped with whipped cream, sprinkled with belgian chocolate flakes was just decadent!!! it was a little too sweet, i would have liked more banana over toffee, but i loved it anyway. our potent tiramisu cake- okay so iv had tiramisu so many times, but here at coast cage, they take their desserts and their booze seriously....and dude its one hell of a boozy, coffee, creamy wonder!!! layers of rum, bailey's, light and chocolate cake doused with strong coffee and covered with creamy mascapone. the total bill came to 3800 (inclusive of taxes) which is completely worthy of the experience. i had the best lunch with my girlies in a long time, would highly recommend this place and def. will be back for more!!!
What a wonderful place it is. Employees are welcoming and smiling always. Mouth watering food and lovely presentation. One of my favourite place to go and eat food.
We were staying at the Crowne Plaza and fancied a different restaurant. This did not disappoint. Really tasty and timely service.

We really enjoyed our meal here. We also had really great cocktails with ICE something we didn't do many places. The ingredients were fresh and the dishes flavorful. Service was really good. It isn't a super-high-end type restaurant -- Decor is minimal and contemporary, prices reasonable and relaxed atmosphere -- no pretensions. Highly recommended.

We had a delicious lunch at Coast Cafe whilst visiting Haus Khan village. Highlights were the Pepper chicken curry and the appams! I'm gluten free so the appams were great as they are a type of pancake made from coconut milk and rice flour. Mojitos were good too and all reasonably priced. Would definitely recommend.

We visited Coast Cafe on a weekday for dinner. The place is well done up, and has a nice ambiance. We ordered few starters - mushrooms and fries. Like coastal food is expected to be, it was quite spicy but nice. We next ordered the main course - 2 vegetarian curries and 2 non-vegetarian curries along with sides of uppam, malabar parantha, etc. Service was good, with the staff helping us choose good dishes according to our tastes.

This is a great place to explore coastal flavours from the South. I tried the fish curry which was fantastic and followed it up with a great tiramisu. The coffee and tea menu gives you the chance to taste some great creations and more exclusive teas.  The ambience is nice and the restaurant is set on 2 floors with the first floor more casual and upstairs for slightly larger groups and feeling a bit more formal. The staff are friendly and the barman takes great pride in his cocktail mixing. Great flavours here at the Coast Cafe!

I am in love with the food served here !!! Amazing coastal cuisine ...Like: fish curry with kokam, appams, Goan prawn curry, mutton Sukha, Popsicles, Bloody Mary... And salads!!!!Average: the chicken pepper dish and the serviceOver all a great experience !!!!

Perch looks just what the name suggests - perched in the middle of a busy market area, this cute little wine bar has a great collection of wines. one can choose to buy wines by the bottle or in carafes - advisable if everyone's got a different wine they prefer.  And the sangrias are refreshing and definitely a great way to beat the summer heat. The food choices were limited but everything we ordered was delightfully tasty.
A good choice for a date. Not too many eating options. The cocktails are really good any refreshing. A good collection of wine as well. All in all a must try.
Loved the place but service was VERY slow. If you discount that, food is very interesting. They have a nice collection of wines and coffee. You can sit with a book for hours and sip away coffee.

We had an truly nice and romantic evening on the terrace. We had a bottle of Indian wine (when in Rome right...) and shared a plate of crispy lotus stems with honey and chili. The interior is nice and clean, kind of reminds me of a typical Scandinavian cafe. The terrace is what makes this place magical. The dim lighted evening, sharing a bottle of wine. Wear pants and long sleeves as there are a lot mosquitos there after the sun goes down.

First thing you will notice their open kitchen type ambiance, second their menu which really fascinating like my four folded question paper, third they have a pretty good choice of new flavor in menu plus 3 different add-on which is must try. relax their have couple of coffee and gossip. unfortunately i mostly visited during the day, so i never get the chance to try wine their.
Good place, with nice wine, cocktails, beers, food and snacks. However, it is a bit expensive. Good to start the party

Perfect place tucked away on the 1st and 2nd floors of Khan market. The entrance actually looks like a perch as the name suggests. Innovative & Distinct Menu - Drinks/Cocktails/Wines and Coffees... appetizers.. try them all, worth it. Sit on the high seats towards the windows for a nice view.

Food ok, wouldnt recomemd the sandwiches but bean and goat cheese salad is excellent. No kingfisher premium served which is ridiculous if only kingfisher ultra is available since its disgusting thus have to end up buying foreign beer.

I've always been cautious when selecting a break from wonderful Indian food to have some European flavors in Delhi, but I was really pleased with this the delicious food of Perch that have nailed it with just the right touches of local flavors! Courtesy, unpretentious service in an a casually elegant atmosphere. Really happy customer!

And I am not sure the food and other drinks are just as good although I didn't try them. The service is great - the owner Vaibhav personally handpicked each wine on his list and seems quite knowledgeable about food and wine in general being an old hand in the industry. Delhi needs more of these!

When I returned from Dubai, my daughter suggested going to this new place. I was pleasantly surprised to see that Delhi had such nice places to go to. They have a great selection of wines. If in doubt, do ask Vaibhav for recommendations. He introduced us to a couple of new Indian wines which turned out to be excellent. The other great thing is that Perch offers healthy light food, which is the trend today. No-one wants to eat huge heavy meals in the evening. The service was friendly and prompt. We visited twice in three days - tells you how much I liked the place.

I happened to stop by this place out of curiosity, and stayed on with a book, chilled chardonnay and a quick bite. It's a little abode in the mess that is Khan Market, way too much happening in too many little corners. But Perch with its light decor, polite service and good wine list was a welcome break.  Can't comment on the food much, but have heard mixed reviews, will try it myself on my next visit. Keep it up
Good collection of wine.Very good and prompt serviceFood not so goodMore variety in food expectedBit loud place.Overall good exp.

I heard a lot of good reviews about this place. Being a foodie, I was tempted to try this place. So today I went here with my friend and sadly my experience was not good. The moment I entered here, the ambiance was good. It seemed to be a good place to chillax. Then I ordered red wine sangria and cold coffee. Much to my dissapointment, I was not impressed with the sangria. I read a lot of recommendations for sangria here. But it didnt appealed me. Cold coffee was bitter and it was not upto the mark. Next I ordered Spinach pappardelle. The presentation was good but the dish was not good at all. It was bland and had no flavors at all. So overall my experience was not good.

You want fresh food, this is the place you should head to. I had Smoked chicken and red wild rice with roasted peppers and Kimchi for an appetizer and Pan Roasted Tenderloin in the main course and it was magical. Even my fresh lime soda was so fresh. The place is buzzing on Sundays and to get a table outside is tough. Try booking before you head to it. Highly recommended. Go now

Stopped by for desserts and coffee, not disappointed by either, love the simplicity of the place and the natural light, big windows, skylight and the overall look and feel. Service was good, smart staff and the dessert was delicious!

I met some friends for coffee here, the place is beautifully raw and bare and the service is good enough. The coffee is on the pricier side, but it seems to be so everywhere these days. I didn't try their cocktails but a friend tells me they are good!
Loved this place, a great ambience, a good wine list and a very interesting menu need one say more. Definitely worth a visit when in Khan Market
A conveniently located hotel close to the airport. The room was very comfortable and everything worked well. The breakfast was great with everything you would need for a perfect start of the day.

We had gone there with our family to celebrate 2 birthdays. All age group from 4-65.  They had good variety of food but it was not very tasty. Sushi was horrible.  Though kids liked black DAL and naan which was served hot. Golgappa were good.  Service was also good.

I love to visit NYC with my family and family friends. We enjoy to have our Sunday lunch over next 2 hours chatting with family and friend. We get good appetisers, starter kababs, soup, variety of salads, theme based main meal and then desserts, fruits and ice cream. Kids get all of their choices like pastas, noodles, chaats, Jellys etc. This is all accompanied with prompt and courteous service by the staff and culminates finally with payment of a very economical amount. A real Sunday lunch is the one which is good at not only taste, look and ambience but also on pocket. I like to visit here frequently.

The unmatchable duo of Valentine’s Day and Radisson BLU on 14th Feb was indeed a perfect destination to be at with your loved one, in order to experience supreme luxury in the lap of NYC at Radisson Blu Plaza with some romantic, memorable and warm moments with The Valentine’s Day special Brunch and Dinner.  I always wanted to spend my Valentine away from the daily hustle bustle and with extreme luxury. Extensive research about the place and the NYC’s Sunday Valentine Brunch special menu made the place an ultimate destination to experience delectable food and luxurious dining that too for a steal of a price Rs 5014 per couple + taxes.  We were greeted by the smiling staff and the elaborate buffet spread consisting of huge variety of Salad, sushi, cheese board, mains (Asian/western/India) along with some valentine themed desserts.  The rose on the table perfectly set the mood for the romantic valentine lunch with my gorgeous partner.  Salads (Veg & Non – Veg)  I absolutely loved the presentation and flavour of Zucchini roll with spiced feta cheese and a tinge of mint, but my heart went to the Beetroot “Carpaccio” with goat cheese, roasted garlic & honey basil syrup which tingled my taste buds with flavours coming out of the roasted garlic and honey basil syrup. Being a mushroom fan I absolutely loved the Three Mushrooms, extra virgin olive oil, sea salt and aged balsamic. Words literally die to describe the Duo of scooped Melon with chili basil Vinaigrette and frisse , very fresh and flavourful    Going a bit light on the chicken I tried the great Jerk Spiced Chicken Slice with Spiced Marinated Dry Fruits and absolutely loved it. The sea food lovers had ample options of salads to choose from this huge section.  Cold Sea Food Station  Being allergic to sea food could not try anything from this section but again the sheer variety presented in the form of Shrimp Pyramid, Green Mussels, Clams, Scampi would have been absolute delight if I was a sea food lover.  Cheese Board  I miss something and I got something too in the form of a Cheese Board. An absolute cheese lover – One with the capability to eat raw cheese too. Great variety of cheese on display and to satisfy my cheese hunger. I have been to a lot of buffets but not encountered the cheese board ever, hence this place earns extra brownie points.  Sushi Bar  Wow ! . Do not I need to say more, our expressions were enough to spot this sushi full of counter. Wide open eyes loved the cute little sushi on display. The assorted peppers roll and the cucumber and radish pickled roll won the day for me. Delicious and worth a thousand bites for sure.  The other available options were Salmon & Asparagus Roll and Prawn n Carrot Roll Mains (Western/Asian/India) When you see such huge variety of food and that too which is to die for, you go bonkers. This is exactly what happened to us. We were quite literally all over the place to check out the variety and making a game plan as per which we can try all the varieties without wasting food but failed, the only things we were left with was brim full stomach.  The dishes which I absolutely loved and deserves a shout out are - Peri-Peri Chicken Breast with Roasted Pineapple Salsa, Home Made Ricotta and Spinach Gratin, Creamy Corn Agnolotti with Confit Cherry Tomato Sauce. The potato cakes were aptly cooked and were less greasy too. From the Asian section of main I loved Broccoli and Black mushroom with garlic & pepper sauce (mushroom love), Burnt garlic & Kale fried Rice accompanied with delicious chili garlic noodles. The Indian Mains with caught my eye and became part of my meal were Kairi wali Bhindi (Super Crisp), Aloo Chutneywaley, Paneer Jaipuri, my all time favourite Dal Makhni and Subzion Ki Tahiri. Desserts Now the most loved and my favourite part of the meal. The dessert section was all Valentine themed, with prominent red colours and the cute lil hearts forming the part of almost every dessert on offer.  • Lemon Swiss Roll with Italian Meringue • Amaretto Tiramisu in Dark Chocolate Cup • Fresh Strawberry and Clove Semolina Greek • Vanilla Cardamom Crème Brulee with Roasted Nuts • Guava Baked Yogurt with Red Wine Cherry Compotes (Eggless) • Scotch Raisin and Chocolate Fudge Cake • Nutmeg White Chocolate Marquise with White Chocolate Fans • Fresh Strawberry Praline Cream Pavalova • Spicy Dark Chocolate Mousse in Glass • Wild Berry Trifle Grand Mariner Jelly in Glass • Chocolate Crunchy Whisky Cake • Marinated Strawberry on Sable • Gajar ka Halwa • Strawberry Shahi Tukra All the dessert were amazing, I was literally amazed by looking by the variety of desserts on the counter along with some exotic fresh cut fruits.  I truly think the food quality was unmatchable and the variety is something which I have already talked about in details in my review above. The servers and the staff were at their cordial best. In the end it was a decision well made to spend the some lovely time in serene peace and calm with muti –cuisine food and heavenly desserts.  Will surely visit them again

I was here for a lavish Mediterranean Brunch. I was spellbound by the ambiance and the interiors. The staff was cordial and welcoming. Brunch featured exotic Mediterranean dishes inspired from European countries such as Greece, Italy, southern France and Spain; North African countries, such as Libya and Morocco; and Middle Eastern countries, such as Lebanon, Syria and Turkey. I started from the soup and salads. Wide range of salads were there. My favourite was the watermelon with feta and mint. This salad was refreshing. From the starters, I loved the hara bhara kebab and paneer tikka. These dishes were melt-in-mouth perfectly paired with mint chutney. Bruschetta was also great. Eggplant and mozzarella fritters were another hot favourite. For the main course, I tasted the red thai curry which was just amazing. I also had matar paneer which looked absolutely delicious and tasted yummy. Dal makhni also deserves special mention. It is definitely one of the best. NYC also serves wide assortment of desserts ranging from gulabjamun to mousse. You would love to indulge in the sinful temptations of Desserts like Basbousa, Mohallabiah, Umm Ali, Crema Catalana, Zeppole , Greek yoghurt Cheese Cake with lemon poached figs.  My fav was the caramel custard and mango icecream. This exotic brunch served great varieties of Cold Cuts, Salads, Breads, Live Cooking counters, Main Course & tempting desserts. I am definitely recommending NYC to all those who would love to chill out and relax over food and wine!
A Wide Variety of Choice - A Perfect Layout of Mix Cuisines. I often have my Business Meetings here - The Service and Food Is Delicious.

Lovely ambience, decent food and great company. Enjoyed my visit to this restaurant. The show stopper for me was the sushi platter - a great variety of salmon, prawn and chicken. The chicken with cashews was lovely too. Just the right amount if spice and salt with dry red chillies. The satays were flavourful. Enjoyed the peanut butter sauce that accompanied it. The desserts were amazing! Loved the green tea souffle, chocolate cake with vanilla ice cream and the fried dough ice cream. One better than the other. Good going guys - keep it up!

An elegant and beautiful place, that serves authentic and crazy good chinese, japanese and thai cuisines. Kylin is situated on the ground floor of Holiday Inn Mayur Vihar, and is a great restaurant for some amazing portions. The food served was picture perfect and the taste too was amazing. They have indoor seating with live chef tables and a limited outdoor seating too. Also, the bar makes it a wonderful addition. The interiors and ambience too is something out of a royal kitchen, plain and quaint with a hint of colors. We went there for an early dinner, and loved everything. Coming to that only: Prawn Sushi - A proper sushi with, fried golden prawn in the middle that gave it a crunch and the rice that made it a smooth and tasty meal. Wok Tossed Chicken - Chicken tenders tossed in a wok with peanuts and fresh cut veggies, and a sauce to die for, the chicken was smooth from inside and crunchy from the outside. Loved this one. Veg Sushi - A vegetarian version of the traditional sushi, with cut veggues as the filling, something that was really good. Chicken Dimsum - So soft, juicy and tender chicken dumplings that were just too good, must try. Vegetarian Dimsum - Again a juicy dumpling that just made my day, a vegetarian one with fresh veggies and a very hot one. Prawn Dimsum - Juicy, tender prawns in a paper thin dimsum, very hot, juicy and tasty. Fried Chilli Mushroom - Fried mushroom with a crunchy exterior and a drizzle of chilli that took it to a totally new level. A fried goodness. Fried Chilli Fish - One more crasy dish, hot pieces of fried fish that were both tasty and crunchy and a light drizzle of chilli and other sauces that made it a great one. Fried Rice - Served hot and cooked to perfection a clear definition of the dish, each grain of rice looked beautiful and was perfect for a main course meal. Chilli Tofu - Tofu that was as soft as cottage cheese, mixed in chilli sauce and light spices, a great one for vegetarians. Clay Pot Lamb - Lamb strips cooked with veggies in a clay pot for a traditional and authentic taste, the gravy was thick and the lakb was juicy, all in all a great one. Ice - Cream in Fried Spring Rolls - Vanilla ice cream, filled in crunchy and hot spring rolls, drizzled with honey and nuts for a fusion of a dish that was a kick starter. Wotkrth the calories. For the drinks we had: Guava Sparkle - Guava juice with sparkling soda, and very light spices, that had a very refreshing taste. Cranberry Wonder - As the name suggests, a high on cranberry mocktail that will take you to a totally crazy trip, something you won't forget. Ginger Ale Coke - Coke mixed with gingerale and fresh cut gingers for a lovely fusion that was good on the tastebuds and relaxing for the body, do try this one. Overall the place and ambience was great, and the aithentic food that we had was a pure star. The dimsums and desserts though were the beet thing that i had, and will surely visit again.

We were staying at the Holiday Inn when we came down to Kylin for dinner. & we'd a great time. The drinks, the food, the service - all were outstanding. We'd Bira White, Daiquiri, Teppanyaki Grilled Fish, Chicken Coriander Dimsum, Thai Green Curry Chicken & Steamed Rice. The ambience was relaxed too. Our server was really attentive & ensured we had a good time.
we loved the salads, all of them! we returned to this place just for that. the main courses were a bit small in size but the flavor was unique and wonderful

During our recent visit to Delhi, we stayed for our third time here at the Radisson Blu Airport. The theme if the evening was (ironically, since we'd just had a 14 hour flight from New York) was Manhattan! Although normally quite adventurous cuisine wise, I opted for--after a local chicken thigh appetizer--the pepperoni pizza. It was surprisingly well done and very delicious. The following morning the restaurant turns into a breakfast buffet extravaganza! Everything you can imagine: Two eggs over easy cooked to perfection, fresh fruit, and a comprehensive vegetarian selection.  A special note: The service and attention of the wait staff was exceptional!!! a special commendation to the manager for his training and insistence of perfection in his superior staff.
Had a dinner with friend here.....starter was excillent.....ambience was nice...decent service......courteous staff... Rajneesh is my best server....
They have a really nice dinner buffé with all kinds of food. Dessert also nice. Employee Sudhir gave excellent service.  Will be back.  Thanks

It was good been there for lunch with a business associate. Close to airport but away from city. Less space give congested feeling as you can't discuss while dining. Buffet is good in taste and variety.
There is ala-carte menu as well as huge buffet available for breakfast, lunch and dinner. Buffet is excellent with fresh tasty meals. Variety is excellent. Staff is attentive.

So, I was trying to put on an old dress and realized how much weight I have put on. The only exercise we do is walk up to the car, sit, dine out and come back. This incident reminded me of how I almost hogged on the scrumptious food at NYC and haven't reviewed it yet. I remember, I almost gave up on sweets and oily eatable for a week, but then NYC came into the picture. Drool-worthy Desserts, annoyingly yummy chaat and off course some delightful dishes. OMG! The spread at NYC is jaw-dropping. Only for approx Rs. 2000, it's totally worth every single penny. It offers something for everyone! Be it vegetarian, non-vegetarian, authentic or western cuisines - they have them all under one roof. Located in Mahilpalpur, the lavish property - Radisson Blue Hotel homes some brilliant themed restaurants, one of them being - NYC. With a HUGE spread of various cuisines, NYC offers a balanced menu consisting of starters, soups, main course and desserts, both authentic and western. Our Experience: After a smooth drive to the place, we were lucky enough to be greeted with a warm and welcoming staff. NYC is just a few steps from the reception area and faces a comfortable lounge and bar area. As soon as you walk through the lounge area towards the bakery, you'll notice the attractive blue logo of 'NYC'. Take a few more steps, grab your seats and relish the lavish spread! To keep all the more interesting, NYC holds a different theme each day. As we were invited to visit on sunday, we got the chance to binge upon the chat section too. With golgappas, papdi, bhalla, khandvi, dhoklas and more - it was certainly the attraction of the day. Next, I made my way through the mezze section which was impressively huge. The spread consisted of babaganoush, Hummus, Tabbouleh, Labneh, Muhammara, Marinated Olives, Lavash with an option of Pita and Whole wheat Pita. Fresh & delightful! Moving on, we ordered Pizza, Pasta Hariyali Paneer Tikka and Glass Noodle & Vegetable Spring Roll. The paneer and spring rolls were tempting but tasted ordinary. Pizza & Pasta were cooked to perfection; however, the pasta was a let-down for me. I kept eyeing the welcoming desserts section but controlled my urge and moved on to the next course. To say the main course spread was huge, would be an understatement. I tasted Tomato Rice, Butter Garlic Pepper Fried Rice, Jeera Aloo Anardana, Bharwaan Dahiwali Bhindi- in the main course. And as they say, there's always a space for desserts, we couldn't control the urge to try an array of options including cut fruits, ice creams and various desserts. Among the many options available, my personal favorites were - Tiramisu, Chocolate Brownie, Raspberry Litchi Bavarious, Fruit Pavlova and Chocolate Walnut Pudding. The lively ambiance and courteous staff enhances the overall dining experience and so was the case here. P.S. I am coming back soon. To try other restaurants in the hotel and probably for a weekend brunch @NYC too.

The location is inside the lobby of Radisson Blu Hotel, so very near to the airport. The menu includes also western cuisine, even if not so rich. Buffet is also available. Service is very professional and price a little bit higher than expectation.

Kylin has been one of my favourite Asian restaurants for quite some time now. I love the ambience of the place and am completely head over heals over their attentive service, delicate flavours and extensive variety on the menu. I went their to watch the FIFA final and the bar and ambience were set for a thrilling finale!  Coming to the food, their sushi is one of the best in town with quality premium ingredients and freshness that keeps you coming back for more. Had their prawn California roll and salmon Philadelphia roll and both were amazing though I have to give an edge to the prawn one as the prawns were so succulent and fresh it took the taste to another level.  If you are a soup fan then you just can’t miss their Tom kha soup! It has such a creamy texture with beautiful coconut aroma and the nutty peanut flavour soaked in the tender chicken pieces. Its delight in a bowl!  I don’t usually order pork but had heard rave reviews about their Pork Belly Bao and have to say the dish made the reviews lacking! The flavours were so delicate and pork so succulent that it just melts in your mouth. I love dimsums and their ginger prawn and chicken coriander dimsums didn’t disappoint! They were juicy, light and flavourful. Whenever I go to a Thai restaurant I have to order the green papaya salad and their salad was zesty , nutty and the perfect start to the meal.  I consider myself a spring roll wanderer who is always on the lookout for the best of these crispy rolls. They definitely made their rolls standout with the unique flavour of lemon grass and kaffir line that cannot be missed in each bite, elevating the humble spring roll. I was so full with the amazing starters, I decided to skip the main course and instead dive into the garlic chilli fish and chicken satay. I love places that have the live station and you can see the beautiful preparation of your food. Both the dishes were great, especially the chicken satay that brought together complex flavours in its marinate and really gives you a mouthful of flavour in every bite. Last but definitely not the least were their deserts, I ordered the ice cream roll that are in my case the sweet version of spring roll and hence cannot be missed! The best part about these is that they keep the sweetness perfect and don’t let it overpower the dish. It results in these airy sweet ice cream filled goodness. The not to be missed dessert is their homemade ice cream trio that has star anise ice cream, the humble vanilla and basil ice cream. The star anise is such a unique flavour in savoury and when you are genius enough to create an ice cream from it then it has to be magic! Lovely flavour and a must try. Their basil ice cream is also very unique and definitely one of the ways you will have your greens!  All in all I really enjoyed going to the Kylin Experience. Now I know why it’s not called a restaurant but an experience coz it takes you and your taste buds on a food journey!

It was an amazing experience to dine at kylin experience. We were three of us, very different in taste. I’m a crazy sushi lover, my friend never tried sushi before and third one is allergic to seafood and eggplants. It was a hard task to satisfy all our needs, however we were taken care of so well and the food selection and attention to details in the time of service was exceptional!  We started off with beautiful Tom Kha chicken soup, fine Asian classic and executed too well, couldn’t fault it.  The starters were lovely. The prawn California roll was divine. I had the crunchy yasai roll for the first time and it was intresting.  The quality of the food exceeded expectations then on with plenty of meat, vegetarian and vegan options. chicken coriander dimsum, black mushroom and baby corn bao and grilled chicken bao wowed is over.  The live tepanyaki table was very entertaining.  The mains were top notch and simply impeccable. I loved their green Thai curry and wok tossed chicken in two pepper sauce.  We ended our meal with in house special trio of basil- star anise and vanilla ice cream.  Loved the whole culinary experience and definitely coming back soon!  Thanks to the whole team of kylin!

I went to Kylin to celebrate my Mom & Dad's anniversary today. I must tell, it was my worst experience ever in star rated hotel. 1st of all the waiter served Ramen, saying it was Tom Yum Soup. This shows the lack of knowledge amongst the staff working there. 2nd, they served the "Tom Yum" Soup after serving the Ramen. They should have served the Soup first. 3rd, the soup served to us was not Tom Yum at all, it was a normal clear sea food soup!! They replaced it and served it in the last course.  Over all, they completely ruined the fine dining experience. I request the managers to do regular quality checks and hire people who have knowledge so that people come and enjoy the food. We are spending good amount of money to have food at your restaurant. Please do not take your customers/guests for granted.  Thanks

I've been here several times. The service is good. The bentoo box is beast value for money. chili garlic noodles are very appetizing. Don't forget to have the Spring roll ice cream ...!! it is mUst to HAVE !!
I've tan here several times. The service is good. The bentoo box is beast value for money. Very convenient when you are a hotel guest.

This place was always known for it's quality of food, ambience but handling kids and a gathering of over 200 people needs a Special Mention. Thai green curry, fish in oyster sauce, chicken in black pepper sauce and a SUPER CHOCOLATE CAKE were highlights. Kylin team, WELL DONE.

This restaurant is on the ground floor of one of the most renowned hotels "Holiday Inn" and the experience came out to be amazing, especially when you are with some foodie friends I loved the Asian interior, the red, black and white colors with yellow flowers created a mellow atmosphere... Let's hope on to the food part We ordered: - Fruity Shake - Sun Rise - Ginger ale infused in coke - Wok toss Chicken with bullet chillies - Lemon Chilly Fish - Baby Potatoes - Assorted Veg Dimsums - Chicken Dimsums - Salmon Sushi - Asparagus Sushi - Prawn Sushi - Pork Belly Bao - Pan Fried Chicken noodles - Scallions and pepper Fish - Green Tea Creme brulee - Chocolate Brownies - Ice cream Spring rolls I know that's a lot The soft drinks were great, particularly the fruity one as it had vanilla icecream  The flavors in the wok toss Chicken with peanuts and bu was really yumm, the only negative point was that the chicken was rubber-like. According to me the fish topped the chart, highly recommendLemon Chilly Fish was amazing, crispy on the outside and soft in the inside... wooww! anddd Scallions and pepper fish justtt awesome.. it was so authentic as the herbs didn't dominate the fish flavours and maintained it's aromatic touch! This dish would be loved by people who dislike spice and savours the orginal. Do try baby potatoes, another tasty starter! potatoes were well-cooked , tossed in crushed peppers and herbs with a bit of sweetness. Next, Dimsums, just like you make it at home I particularly loved the broccoli, chicken and sui mui dimsums... the dimsum sheet was just melt-in-the-mouth. As I had already stated in the other reviews that sushi is not my cup of tea, but I would recommend you to do try Salmon Sushi.. I just ate a bite of it and it was better than the rest. Pork belly was yumm, the bao was soft, the only thing that it lacked was the amount of sauce.. because of that the bao felt dry! Pan Fried egg noodles was nicely made and was delicious. To wrap up we had some desserts, I was really interested to taste the green tea Creme brulee, the sugar was perfectly torched.. unfortunately I didn't got the green tea flavour. The spring roll consisting of vanilla icecream was something new and good ... The best was the Chocolate brownie... so dense and chocolatyy lip-smacking indeed! Although, there were some faults but the positive side dominated the negative side! We certainly would love to hop here again! For more pictures check out my zoamto page in the name of "lipsmackingfoodcommunity"

Whilst staying in Holiday Inn I tended to eat on 3rd floor but feeling adventurous I ventured to the Ground Floor and Kylie. On entering the staff were very accommodating and friendly. The choice was very wide and I settled on the Nasi Goring which to be fair was probably the tastiest dish I have had in long while, I also discovered the happy hour until 8 in the evening .I shall definitely be visiting on my return to Delhi

If you swear by Asian food and wish to indulge in some healthy yet delectable delights, head straight to The Kylin at Holiday inn Mayur vihar for a soulful experience. This place has a comprehensive organic kitchen menu with an illustrious range of fresh, natural and local produce to cater to each palate. The menu highlights traditional Asian recipes that are made in Teppanyaki-style to make the experience full of flavours that are inculcated in various dishes using ingredients like soya sauce, fish sauce, sake, mustard, sesame, chilli, and ginger, among others. We started with Chicken grilled on skewers and charcoal fire, which gives the dish its woody flavour really delicious along with Tempura a Japanese dish in which fried seafood (prawns, shrimp, crab) and Vegetables they are deep fried in a batter & went great with the drinks . There's also sushi on the menu that completes the Asian circuit and does justice to the Japanese favourite. The rolls are well constructed and the flavours just right. . In mains we had Green Thai curry with the clean flavours of fresh basil and herbs really wonderful with vegetables. The dish certainly hits all the right notes of sweet, sour, spicy and bitter, a balancing of flavours which is the ideal of Thai food . I have a sweet tooth so cant leave without desserts so we had Basil Ice Cream light and refreshing—perfect for evening to end on a sweet note .  I would definitely go back for the interesting drinks and fantastic food. It also helps that the music is fun & the service is attentive . Spl Thanks to the Chef for a wonderful food and presentation .Highly recommended for an evening out with friends.

Kylin is a nice experience and this Resturant is located in holiday inn Mayur Vihar . Tried there chicken & prawn sushi which were well made . Butter garlic prawns & chicken satay was flavoured very well . In dim sums we tried chicken which was again yummy and juicy . Love to experience this again !!

the kylin is located on the ground floor as u enter from a beautiful lobby where a automatic paino plays a lovely soft music . As we enter the restaurant u find a lovely Chinese n Japanese style decor with a vast variety of seats along with live kitchen n sushi bar n last but not least a vast collection of whiskey wine n beer n along big screens for matches . Now on food n drinks Starting from soup tom kha which I love it where ever I find I give my first priority to this Thai soup . This soup is a gem proper balance with chicken vegetables n coconut milk coz this soup is based on this only . And I'm seriously saying that soup get wow factor. Then we tried raw papaya salad proper mixed with this dressings n vegetables too . After tht how can we forget sushi as I'm a newly love for sushi so, i tried all variety rainbow sushi platter in which salmon prawns tuna crab all come in one plate n then , I had prawns one separate also as I love prawns a lot . then we decide to try veg variety of sushi too both veg n non sushi platter are awesome , they have proper cruchiness stick together while holding . A star dish n lively cooked In front of us on live kitchen tht chicken satay . I don't hve better than this till now earlier I love satay from jom jom malay in ansal plaza but now I find better than tht here . After tht we had some fruit mood mocktails n one coke float means coke in a ice cream hehe I love it so I ask the server to make it . then we had Bao as there was a Bao festival going on till 20th july so we try out all 3 bao veg non veg n pork bao. I try veg Bao which is black mushroom bao cute n colour full in presentations same with chicken n pork bao all three are awesome at there place. fresh n juicy bao. katm nhi hua Abhi desert left we try there home made trio flavours of ice cream based on Jasmine n Basil Star Nise ice cream they r superb serious nodoubt in it n also fried ice cream spring rolls also great to try .. overall experience for this place is gud love to come again
Awesome Service . Great array of beverages to choose from alcoholic/non-alcoholic. Good ambience and decor

Have been a regular at Kylin from the past 3 years and the experience only gets better. The Tepanyaki grill is our favourite past few months. Chef Manvendra does an amazing job. Must try the Thai Herb fish, Khao Suey. The Sushi is par excellence and my wife's all time favourite...

Kylin has always been a favourite place to eat out with great food and exceptional service. This review is inspired by the ongoing"Thai Khun" Thai Street food Festival. Various stalls have been put up and about mimicking the street food stalls..... Diners are given 'thai money' to go and buy stuff off the food stalls (which by the way is an amazing concept that really gets one to get the real feel!!) It could not have been more reasonably priced. Experience Thailand right here in Delhi. Nitish is an amazing person instinctively tuning in to your tastes and suggesting the perfect options for you. My experience says - when in doubt leave it to Nitish!!! ( Must try is the Thai paan available at the salad stall - the name eludes me but do get it)

I would recommend Kylin for all the Chinese lovers. The place, ambience (including music) and staff (service), everything is so welcoming and best about this place. The USP of this place is its great food and the ambience. The quantity of food is also very good and sufficient for two. I was recommended by a friend to try Kylin as our family fis a Chinese lover. We ordered Noodles and Momos (pan Fried), they were very delicious and the presentation was equally food. The food has all kinds of variety and quality. The place is peaceful with light music and a very good service from the staff. You will enjoy every time spent here and also won't regret even single buck spend in this place. All the options are available both in Vegetarian and non vegetarian. The whole family had a great outing, spent quality time and enjoyed a great Chinese food in the town. Kylin is recommended to anyone who likes to enjoy good Chinese food with a quick service time. I would love to visit this place and try other options.
we had a great time. thai promotion is doing very well. nitish rana were very helpful in suggesting.love to visit there again .
I must say, this restaurant has the most amazing food! Great food, the staff as so hospitable and brilliant Ambience.

This is one of my favorite places in Delhi (if not the favorite). I've tried every single dish on the Indian side of the menu and here is my favorite order - Malabar Kokum Fish Curry - Rainy Day Stew With Chicken (get boneless if you're sharing) - Malabar Parantha The food is absolutely spectacular and you will not be disappointed. In the winters, you could try their mulled wine as an accompaniment. In the warmer months, I prefer their mojito. Even though this place is in Hauz Khas Village, the restaurant has a very peaceful ambience. Looking out the window, you would not be able to tell that you're in HKV. The restaurant is done up really nicely and has a simple and classy character that is hard to find in the rest of the village. Overall, you MUST try this restaurant if you haven't already and don't be afraid to try different things in the menu. They are all solid. The dishes above are the best though!

I had tied up with Any Surprise Any Place to host a surprise dinner for my spouse's birthday at Coast Café. The restaurant cooperated well and we had a great time. The ambience suits a romantic mood to the hilt. The servers are courteous, knowledgeable & quick. They did not hover around unnecessarily & yet were available as soon as needed. They have a niche menu which is appetizing to the palate. We loved the Eat-With-Your-Hands Kerala Grilled Chicken, Our Garam Curry Chicken, Appam & Sukha Mutton Fry that we ordered. I washed my food down with a Moscow Mule & a Red Wine Sangria. Will love to return here...
NIce place for a sunday lunch. They have good cocktails and the food is superb. The mutton fry is exceptional!

A beautiful, cozy, and amazing cafe built on 2 floors, with nice hanging balconies, having a nice coastal cuisine and ambience of Kerala. Must Try- Avial, Malabar Paratha, Appams with fresh beetroot, tamarind and tomato chutneys. Must visit for a Malabari experience in Delhi
We visited for dinner, and being fond of coastal food, found it to be extremely nice. Staff is friendly and service was quick

Visited this place for the first time. The decor and ambience is great. The staff is courteous and the menu varied. The food is well-cooked and the portions are generous. Unfortunately, the dishes I tried had an overdose of black pepper which made them too spicy. A must try here are the Cocktail Popsicles. They have 4 on offer - Mai Tai/Rum, Dark Chocolate / Bourbon, watermelon/ gin, and grapefruit/ tequila. Avoid the grapefruit one- it's really quite bitter. The chocolate Bourbon one on the other hand is a stick of frozen heaven.  Overall, for Kerala cooking, I'd still prefer The Toddy Shop, but would visit this place again only for the Bourbon popsicle.

I have had their buffet many times, it' one of my favorites in Delhi, eespecially when I'm traveling at airport. I'm vegetarian and they have quite a big selection of dishes. I especially like their Italian ones. I also like their ala carte menu, but it's very pricey.

The breakfast area of the Radisson becomes NYC for the rest of the 24 hour a day. Had the post 7pm buffet here, and found the whole experience remarkably good. A good selection of indian, Chinese and Italian food available both veg and non veg. Hoppy to make a fresh aloo paratha for you, and follow with an excellent garlic naan. Friendly helpful staff.

The nyc restaurant us located inside the radisson blu plaza hotel. The restaurant looks nice and is very clean. You can either chose to take anything from the buffet or choose a dish from their menu. The buffet gas a really wide choice of international cuisine from indian, mexican, european.... The menu has also indian food, italian food and american food. The meal i had was reall good and served quite quickly. As it is located inside a high scale hotel i do believe that the restaurant is well overpriced but if you want to take a break from indian food while in india, you should definitely go there.

Nothing can soothen you up than a lavish widespread buffet brunch in a beautiful 5star property with a pool view and all these things match to my experience last sunday with NYC. The ambience and decor is very elegant ; many expats and tv-celebs can easily be spotted there anytime. The buffet menu had more options than the runs indian cricket team is scoring these days, from all kinds of salads, starters , tandoori dishes, indian street food , italian , chinese, desserts and what not. The presentation was magnificent and the dishes lookef like a piece of art in it self. I personally loved the italian salad section with different slices of hams , variety of olives and chicken mortadella sausage. The lebanese section was equally good which comprised of baba ghanoush and perfectly balanced hummus with freshly baked pita. The watermelon salad hit the right spots of my palate and was very refreshing. In the main course, I liked the gosht dum biryani with very tender meat pieces. The hakka noodles with hunan style fish was very delicious. The tandoori stand had very good kebabs too. Their dessert section needs an applause for the craft - kudos to the pastry chef. Amazing gulab jamuns, cheese cakes, pineapple halwa and the best of the lot - swiss choco mousse. The service was excellent and meetha pan in the end made me happy.  In a nutshell- a very good option if you are looking for a lavish brunch.

Radisson Blu Plaza is a 15 years old property, and proudly boasts its restaurants in particular, NYC(multi-cuisine) and The Great Kebab Factory, with a fairly new addition of Neung Roi(Thai). Last sunday, i finally decided to make the tremendously long journey from Faridabad to Mahipalpur just to get a mouthful of delicious spread at NYC. Located in the lounge area, this restaurant screams elegance, with great music, view of the swimming pool, and a wide spread. They have plenty of sitting options, and a very hospitable and friendly staff. The buffet was divided into various sections, namely: Salads(veg/nonveg)- 3 individual portions each in small bowls, and 4 in platters. My favorite among the individual portions consisted of Watermelon cube with feta, olives & citrus salsa, it was light and refreshing and the slight sweetness of watermelon along with the saltiness of the feta and olive, made it the perfect start. I also enjoyed Spicy Harissa chicken salad with aioli, prawn cocktail with coleslaw and tuna mousse in egg cups, all these salads were really delicious. Next came the Lebanese/ Mezze Station with babagannoush, tabbouleh, hummus, lebnah, olives, pita etc.... needless to say everything was tasty. then came the soup station for which they had prawn bisque and lemon coriander with a variety of breads(savory and sweet). For mains, they had three main sections namely Indian, Oriental, Continental and Italian, followed by a craving station and a counter of Chaat. The Gosht Dum Biryani was aromatic and flavorsome. Pan seared fish with lemon basil butter sauce was decent. They also have a live station serving an array of delicacies, the dahi ke kebab, Cheese and Jalapeno fritters were good. They had 2 dessert sections, one with indian desserts, ice creams, fresh fruits and a selection of cheese and the other full of delicious goodness like mississipi mud slice, passion fruit cheesecake, swiss chocolate mousse, chocolate eclair, Pumpkin pie etc.... and well am slightly partial towards desserts as they are my favorite things on earth and hence this was my favorite section followed by the salad bar. the overall experience was delightful.
A amazing food with Amazing staff perfect smile perfect Hair style of staff....The overall ambience is best,warm welcome.....recommend to Rajnish

Nyc is a great place for dinning...The ambience is great. & The staff is well trained...Food was very tasty....i visited the places several times and always had a gread experience with server Rajneesh....Thanks again...& Specially recommend Rajneesh Singh for great service
Great place to have lovely food. Hospitality was awesome and a special mention of Submit Kumar and Sheetal who looked after us very well. Thx for all the efforts.
Very delicious noodle and amazing Chinese bao！and the service is also good. With so much free food.Radha sudhir

This is my third stay in Radisson blu plaza near Delhi airport .i look forward to stay here every time I gets some opportunity to stay or travel via delhi.Best is the overall ambience, warm welcome and look after by the staff and above all the food.The buffet during each meal is exclusive right from the starters to the deserts.I love their grilled food and salads.The staff greets with broad smile and make u feel comfortable just like heaving meals in the cozy environment of the home.Thanks to Sudhir ,Rajneesh and Abgigail for their exceptional professionalism.
Sudhir is a credit to Radisson blu hotel. He is very attentive and offers fantastic service to all members of the hotel. Thank you Sudhir. Hopefully see you again in future :)
Very good service by Sumit Kumar  Ashoka Sagar. Everything is great food was excellent. Thankyou so much we will join you again shortly.

Surprisingly good food in this hotel restaurant! Nice pizza en good starters. Good value for money (considering the venue). Great gesture to bring some complimentary deserts to or table! Special thanks to Rajneesh, Ajaj and Sudhir

Suggested by my friend visit this place, really nice experience with you guys. Food was great and services was too good Sudhir and Ajay who made every thing special for us.love the hospitality we will be back soon.

It was wonderful movement to have brunch in NYC, I am very much happy to write comments about your brunch, there was live counter was excellent, and it was well Displayed of all the starters. Thank you so much, there was chef laxman who made good chicken starter, and thanks to sudhir for well careing to us.

it was good food, speaclly ckn loly pop and chicken shawrma. thanks to chef amit and sudhir. for good service. there was kids counter was also amazing, and good selection of shake.thanks to ajay who recommended good varity of beverage.

Love the place, we guys are regulars , the food quality is just top notch ...... not to forget there team specially “Nitish” who always makes our evening , by suggesting the best starters / main course combinations with excellent drinks five stars to you specially Nitish and the whole team ⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
This is one of the few high quality restaurants in East Delhi that can match the best in the city. Do try sushi, spring rolls and noodles.
An excellent restaurant with great food and really good service. Was staying t Crown Plaza which also had a good restaurant and this just made a nice alternative

There are times when we crave for Thai food and start scavenging for places that serve the best, coz if you want to enjoy Asian food, you need to get it from a place that knows how to prepare it! Post an extensive R&D session, we came up with the name, Kylin, at Holiday Inn Mayur Vihar. Greeted in a professional manner we were welcomed by a lavish bar that showcased every genre of alcohol known to mankind and the lobby opened up to a vast and enormous area dedicated towards seating in the most strategic way ensuring ample space for moving around along with regaling hungry souls hovering all over. The décor is a classy one with hints of Japan adorning the walls, giving an authentic feel to the restaurant. Still stupefied by the view we ended up gorging upon Tom Kha Vegetable soup, Crunchy Yasai Roll, Broccoli and Almond dimsums, Black mushroom and baby corn bao wraps, Veg curry dumplings, chicken Coriander dimsum and Ginger prawn dimsum. The chef knew what he was making and every item served was prepared with finesse and full zeal and vigour. The soup was a tasty one with the dominant flavour of coconut and a slight sweet taste of Thai cuisine being reflected nicely in every sip. The Yasai roll aka sushi was a veg one which we had, just coz we were curious how it tastes like. We loved how the rice used on the outside was not the regular ones and doing full justice to the cuisine, were imported ones and had a certain level of crunch to them. Anyhow, we loved it! The dimsums were curated fresh for us and hence took some extra time to reach our table. In a hierarchy from top to bottom, the first comes the chicken dimsums, then ginger prawn and then the veg curry ones. Evenly boiled and with a perfect shaped pieces, we were just too ecstatic to see them and gobbled up in a haste. The bao wraps were decent, where we expected the filling to be covered in a bao but somehow the chef presented us a different genre of bao where baby corns, black mushrooms with a sweet and tangy Thai flavour formed the filling along with veggies and were tucked in between a bao made of fine flour. Though the taste was amazing, we had different expectations altogether. All in all, we loved the place, we loved the food, we definitely loved the hospitality and we are more than definite that we will be back soon. A special thanks to Mr Yogendre who ensured we had a good dining experience and also to Chef Dhondup, who explained every item that was served to us in detail. Cheers ;)

We recently went out on dinner with family and decided to have Asian food. The closest restaurant to our place that served authentic Asian food was Kylin and since the prices seemed decent, we decided to give this one a try. We loved the interiors of the place and the authenticity given to the décor. We had Veg kylin lettuce wrap, Lohan veg in spicy garlic sauce, Veg mapo tofu, Thai veg green curry, Five spices chicken, Chili garlic noodles, Steamed rice and fried Ice Cream Spring Roll in desserts. The food is no doubt one of the finest we have had till date. With every ingredient, texture and taste to the point, the dishes were flavoursome and worth every penny. The wrap was a nice one which had well sautéed veggies to be eaten with lettuce leaves kept aside.  The tofu was the highlight of the evening with utterly moist tofu sautéed well in Thai gravy along with broccoli, baby corns and other veggies carrying a tantalizing flavour and an amazing texture making us drool all over. The curry again was a delight with the dominant flavour of lemon grass remaining on the palate for long. With multiple veggies in this dish, they had absorbed the essence of the gravy to the core making the dish a must have when here. We liked the chicken too with nicely fried succulent chicken being cooked beautifully in five different spices but somehow despite the texture on point, the flavour seemed quite decent. Thai spices are generally not spicy, neither we were expecting this one to be, but considering the presence of 5 spices did not make it mark. Considering the other items served, we assumed it to be once in a blue moon scenario but nevertheless, we were in still awe of this place. The noodles were fantastic with a bold flavour and spices that can make one run for water, and we went all gaga over this one. The prima facie look did not entice us but the aroma and the first bite of the dish took us to a whole new level. A very nicely done dish. This concept of fried icecream has taken the industry by storm and Kylin was no different. Vanilla icecream, was deep fried in pastry sheets, out of icecream cones are made and was outlined with honey and garnished with roasted peanuts! Needless to say, this was the perfect way to end our dinner. In the end, Kylin seemed a ravishing place which satiated our hunger to the core with sumptuous food, lovely ambience and a loads of memories.

Looking for some authentic Thai ,Japanese and Chinese food? The Kylin Experience at Holiday is the perfect place that will fullfill all your such cravings. The kylin experience is located at the ground floor of Hoiday inn , The nearest metro station is mayur vihar extension , it will take around 5-7 mins to reach the place by foot. Few days back I and my friends planned to have a dinner and we all we're craving for some amazing thai ,japanese and Chinese food . We we planned to visit The Kylin Experience and trust me the experience was just amazing and classy. From the food to the service everthing was just perfect , A very decent interior along with such nice seating space. I started with sime fresh virgin mojito , Now coming to what all we had : ♦Shushi - They have a wide variety of shushi available in the menu ,though i am not a big shushi lover but the way they made the sushi was just mouth watering from the presentation to the taste. ♦Sap roll ♦Veg Philadelphia roll Both the roll was just amazing. ♦Prawns Dimsum ♦Chicken coriander dimsum ♦Broccoli almond dimsum As I am big dimsum lover and got to have many different varieties over their , my personal favourite was prawns & chicken coriander one , from the stuffing to the presentation just outstanding. ♦Grilled chicken bao ♦Black mushroom bao Their are many bao lovers in the town and if you are one of them , then do try their grilled chicken bao & black mushroom bao , you gonna love them. Soup : ♦Tom yum chicken / vegetables ♦Beijing soup The taste was soup was super good as i am soup lover and love eating healthy , the soup served was really good and flavoursome. ♦Chicken satay ♦Wok tossed chiken ♦Kylin lettuce rap veg Chicken satay and wok tossed chicken was just mouthwatering and super tasty , for all the chicken lovers out their a must try diah from the kylin experience. Coming to the deserts now we had : ♦Fried ice cream roll - Great presentation and taste as i love fried icecream roll , the wrap was very well fried and overall flavour was really good. ♦Chocolate mahjhog - One of the most amazing chocolate based desert i have ever tried ,Chocolate mahjhong is a must have for all the chocolate lovers out their ,it was so fudgy and chocolatey at the same time a long rectangular shaped desert served along with the vanilla ice cream. My experience at The Kylin Experience - Holiday inn was super amazing , a great place to visit with friends and family. A big thank to chef Dhondu and team for making such good food and to all the staff out their for making the experience such amazing. Thankyou and keep it up:) P.S - Do have a look at the pictures:)

A lover of Thai and Japanese cuisine, i always look for authentic Asian restaurants. Recently paid a visit to Kylin, holiday inn and it made my weekend as the everything was just perfect from the ambience to service and obviously the food was heavenly. In this scorching July sun, i started my meal with a refreshing lemon grass mojito.
