Things i recommend: Tom kha chicken soup- A perfect combination of all Thai flavours Dimsums- had both chicken and prawn, as it was difficult to choose one! Raw papaya salad- One of my favourites! Pork belly boa- It is hard to find such juicy pork, it was divine! Keeping it light, for the main course we had chicken satay and garlic chilli fish, delightful and appetizing. And to always end on a sweet note, we had: Fried icecream rolls  Homemade icecream- basil and stat anise- Amust must try! Green tea créme brûlée- tempting and tasteful Don't just read the review, go visit and try it on your own! You will not be disappointed!

If you are in Delhi between July 28-Aug7 don't miss the Thai Khun festival underway at Kylin. They are serving the best Thai street food one can get in Delhi- and at unbelievable price points.  The fare is brilliant. I started off with the Veg Tom Kha which was a tidal wave of flavours. Followed it up by 2 sumptuous appetisers - the Soya chap roared on a Japanese grill and served with peanut sauce and the Thai grilled Dimsym with a spicy chilly sauce. Mouthwatering and finger licking good stuff !  For my mains I tried the yellow curry with sticky jasmine rice . It was heavenly to say the least. Wrapped it up with a homemade coconut and basil ice cream - with lots of fresh Chinese tea in continuous attendance. To make the experience complete they serve you in bamboo cups and plates - it really brings the street side experience to life. Don't miss the live soup and grill counter. Last but not the least - it's a steal. My dinner cost me lass that 1000 Rupees!! Too good to be true! So much so that I landed up 3 evenings in a row. Don't miss it . My special thanks to the chef and service brigade - Chef Pascal, Faisal, Dushyant, Aniket, Nitish Anup, Prema Subir and last but not the least Mr. Suprabhat and Chef Sudhir. Brilliant team indeed !

We always look forward to Chinese.this time it was my husbands birthday but it was ruined because of leaking A/C vents we had to change 3 tables but the water kept dropping. service was extremely por despite the restaurant having just 20% occupancy.snacks arrived 45 min late and main course came much later. we had to leave without eating. The manager Mr faizal called that very night to apologise and he sent us dinner the next day. it was a good spread and we finally enjoyed.i really appreciate his concern and kindness.

yesterday i had the chance to experience the hospitality of the "KYLIN EXPERIENCE" resturant at Holiday inn ,I would say after a long time i really enjoyed the pan Asian cuisine.Apart from the food the ambiance the staff and the surrounding all were like the WOW factor. so keep up the good work and would definitely going to suggest my friends, colleague family member to visit it.

Went on a hot Saturday afternoon. Nice summery ambience. The person who took our order gave us good suggestions. we ordered calamari(were over cooked & hard to chew), warm chili olive oil prawn salad( okayish), Kerala grilled chicken( spicy but amazing), beer battered fried fish taco (outstanding), prawn moilee & aapams were good too. We also ordered some coolers. Watermelon & Monday morning coolers were ordinary. however the Pink lemonade & the choc-peanut butter shake were nice . we als had the signature minus 30 vanilla ice-cream- which wasn't bad. Definitely coming back for more.

Amazing little sanctuary in Hauz Khas Village quietly tucked away. Coast Cafe has some of the best Fish Tacos outside of Mexico. Service is good, prices are fair. Highly recommended. There is also a small outdoor terrace to have a drink and smoke if that suits you.

Loved this café... we went twice! First time was just for a coffee - best in our whole India trip. Second time was for dinner. Had the best Aubergine curry with parathas... absolutely amazing! Interior is stunning and waiters very tentative.

We stayed in the hotel for 8 days and had breakfast here most of those days. The breakfast selection was great- different kinds of dishes like omelettes, pastries, Indian food etc. The quality and taste of food were varied. The space was bright and welcoming. The service was mostly good, there were a number of staff members that were not as friendly as others. We also had lunch a couple of times and mostly had sandwiches- they were good. Service at lunch also improves for some reason- I suppose everyone were awake at this time already.
Its a very good choice for business meetings and anniversary Dinner/ lunch. Must visit.The buffet is better then few other expensive places.

Centrally located and very close to the International Airport. The NYC is the coffee shop of Radisson Hotel. The staff is very courteous and the food good and tastey. The Buffet dinner was very nice with King size prawns which is a delicasy of NYC. The Alacarte menu has also been done over and new dishes added.

As it was well past buffet time, we selected a simple roti sabji, followed by rice and curd. while the rotis were crisp and paneer soft, what amazed me was that they served fresh kerala papad. I thoroughly enjoyed the meal. Value for money too.
The food and the service were good. Very nice people, the breakfast menu could be much more but Keep it up!!
One of my favourite restaurant in Delhi I live to visit again and again I love your buffet and service  Definitely visit again  Thanks again  Ajay and Rajneesh
great selection of food for breakfast. food was delicious and service was too good. thanks sudhir for your service.
Wow service, thanks to sudhir,it was great service, and graet professionals.thanks to Ajay for assisting us for buffet. Good food.

Great lunch buffet, great service by sudhir, nice decore, and good ambiance, thank you ashok as for warm welcoming, manager mr amar too, thanks everyone one. I enjoyed my lunch with my family. Good selection of starters, and soup was ausome. Great place for lunch.

Thanks you so much nyc and team, and thank you sudhir for real hospitality, I saw during my lunch. It was my great time, I came to Delhi and enjoyed my lunch with my friends. And all credit go to sudhir for good service.
Food is very nice, serive provide is in a very good way Mr. sudhir, gives a comfortable zone on table , really appreciated

The food at Coast Cafe last night was horrible ... it seems they have a Gujarati chef as the uppams , malabar parathas , the spicy gravy prawn n chicken dishes we ordered we all overpoweringly sweet !! The uppams could have been passed off as pan cakes ... the piture of drink we ordered - Red wine Sangria was also pathetic ... very disappointing food indeed !!

This place is neat clean and tidy .... menu here have nice dishes from coastal cuisine .... must try are Malabar Paratha .,, appams ... cheti chicken ... highly recommend who loves to eat South Indian food
An authentic place to try new flavours. The malabar breads and dry-mutton fries of this place are simply lip-smacking plus its located in Hauz-Khas! What a Delhi exploring person can ask more for!

Had dinner at this lovely South Indian restaurant that is located in Delhi’s hip Kauz Khas neighborhood. The food was beyond description, just divine. I tried their fish stew Malabari style, as well as beans and peas sautéed in coconut. Both were amazing as was their egg-white appam. The service was prompt and the ambience pleasant. I would say this restaurant easily counts among the top Indian restaurants at which I’ve had the chance to!

This was our 2nd visit to India and Hauz Khas Village. The café we had visited last time wasn't open, and Coast Café was just next door, above a fashion shop. We made our way upstairs unsure of what we would find. It was quite busy so we had to wait 10 minutes - the outside balcony was a nice place to peruse the menu and a table was soon available.  The staff were lovely - confident and clearly took pride in their business and their food, and what a revelation the food was! Often a menu gives no idea of what is in store, and it is not until it arrives that you realise this is something exceptional - this was the case here. Sadly I can't remember the names of what we ate, but the breads (parathas?) were utterly memorable as the best by far we had in 3 weeks in India, as was the rest of the meal. Our friends are real 'foodies' and desperate not to waste any meal time on bland safe cuisine - they were very impressed and we talked about the food here for the rest of our travels in India.

This cafe has a chilled out lunch ambience and the most delicious food. Appam (bowl shaped bread), buttered chicken and vegetarian curry were fantastic. INR 1000 per person - great value. Highly recommended.
Seriously some of the best Indian we’ve eaten since arriving. Highly recommend the eggplant and Fish curry! Good drinks and restaurant fit out too.

This is the most contemporary cafe I visited in Delhi, it's got great interiors and the food was fantastic, Indian cuisine as well as some unique dishes - I had a brocolli & bean in Asian pesto salad which was a refreshing change from all the curries I've been having.  Great drinks too. Staff were lovely and efficient.

Truly a hidden gem amongst the chaos! We have recently moved to Delhi NCR and taken some time at weekends to explore the different neighbourhoods. Haus Khas has some history attached to it and is still the home to some very interesting Indian Clothing shops as well as memorabilia and restaurants. One such restaurant is Coast not easy to find but worth the effort. Very simple layout spread over two floors and serving South Indian food for the most part. All the dishes we have tried have been very good and prices very reasonable. Service is straightforward and effiicient.
very cosy ambiance..great food..amazing variety...love the place..should definitely try mutton and the malabari paratha.

One of the best places in Hauz Khas Village. It's bright and airy with natural sunlight bathing the largely-white space. The food was excellent - light appams, a rich prawn moilee and Thoran. The filter coffee was a perfect way to end the meal. Ambience is lovely and food is great.

I visited The Pullman Hotel, Aerocity, near the airport for a quick bite and ended up becoming a regular. Pluck is absolutely amazing. the food, the ambience and the hospitality is rare to find. We had Vegetarian burgers, Grilled chicken and snapper and I think every dish was to die for. Special thanks to Topesh & Ashutosh and all the staff for making the visits memorable every time.

We visit this cafe quite regularly and must say we love the food here. Situated right above Ogaan (a popular designer store), this place is charming yet serene. Its perfect for a lazy weekend lunch/ dinners. Being vegetarians, our favorites are aubergine cokum curry and vegetable moilee paired with piping hot malabar parathas and appams.They serve a variety of delicious chutneys with their food : tomato,tamarind and beetroot.Their tacos are a must try too - they are simply yum. There are options for salads and pastas too but haven't tried them so cannot comment on it. Another plus is that unlike many places in HKV (Hauz Khas Village), it's easily accessible by lift so one can get elderly people in your family to accompany you too. The service is slightly slow but coupled with ambience and the good food, we don't really mind. But do book a table in advance (esp on weekends) as this is one of the popular restaurants.

Whenever I think of going to Hauz Khas Village, the first cafe that comes to mind is Coast Cafe. Every time I have been here, I have loved the food and the ambience. It's a small cafe but the food and service is exceptionally good. Yesterday, I went to this cafe again I tried their hot chocolate which was perfect for this weather. It was thick and perfectly sweetened for my palate. Next we called for was the Sukha Mutton Fry (Rs.420) and with that we called for Appam (Rs.60). The mutton was grilled and was a treat to eat. Appams also were thin and crisp and went well with the mutton. Since the food was so good, we wanted to try Eat-With-Your-Hands Kerala Grilled Chicken (Rs.440). The gunpowder dip served with the chicken wasn't very impressive but the chicken was flavoursome. This will always be my favourite place for coastal cuisine in Delhi!
At the start of the village on a good location, not far from the car parkFood, wine and prices are all good.Would visit again
Came here for a quick lunch with some colleagues after walking round hauz khas. The food was great and the service was friendly. Well worth a visit. The fish fry was so good we ordered it twice!

Coast cafe mainly coastal food cafe in Hauz Khas village but as I’m veggie I got little choice but that doesn’t disappoint me it’s malabar paratha and all drinks are very good deserts also nice part.. Ambiance and service both I give 3/5  And food 3.5/5

I've been to this restaurant many times. Everything I've tried has been delicious, and the staff is helpful and friendly. This restaurant is located near the main entrance of HK Village, so it's also convenient and "no hassles" if you don't want to navigate the HK scene. Lovely food.
Had the beer battered veggies and the beetroot arugula salad during one visit, and a couple of curries in another. Everything was to die for.

Wanted to try many things but for a constraint of time could only taste few dishes.Had the spicy lamb burger whose size was just perfect and taste perfect, had a minty lemonade along which was refreshing. Will visit again to try more

Coast Cafe serves authentic Kerala cuisine. I would recommend one of their spicy prawn of fish curries to be had with hot appams. They also have some interesting cocktails - I tried the Black Pepper and Mango gin and tonic, which was nice.

A really international crowd adorned Coast Café when I visited mid week in Aug 2015. The food was tasty and I had perhaps one of the best cocktails I've ever had- Gin, fresh mango and Black pepper. Service was attentive and prices reasonable.
loved the food and the ambience of the restaurant. it was a little noisy and overcrowded, and the menu had limited options. But other than that, fantastic. Would love to visit again soon
Located in Hauz Khas Village with a modern style Indian food. it seems there is no parking so you have to go there by taxi.

We dropped in for a late lunch after reading other reviews and were very pleased we did. We really enjoyed the food, including the Malabari kokum fish curry; it was the best meal we had in Delhi. It wasn't very busy when we were there and the service was OK. We will look to return next time we're in Delhi.

An excellent restaurant in a very lively part of town. Friendly staff and great food. The issue of WiFi "not working", despite the fact that it was clearly registering, was referenced a number of times in the guest comments book within which the bill arrived. A minor issue on a lovely evening.

Hip, welcoming decor. You climb up a spiral staircase 2 floors to get to the restaurant. Seems to be popular with youngsters. Excellent food - good choices, many vegetarian options. Beer choices were limited. Service was incredibly slow. We will come back, hoping that the poor service was a one-off and not par for the course.

A stylish modern restaurant overlooking the trees of Haas Kas. Good food although the curries are a little spicy for Westerners. Service was slow and the staff seemed to prefer to huddle by the till rather than communicate with their guests.

Its nice place with lovely Indian coastal food, good service in compact place, its advised to make reservation before planning for food there, good tasty food, try Malabar Parantha with any curry of your liking

After the parade of spices, color, and people in Delhi, this was a delicious retreat. Food had a basis of Southern Indian but with interesting flourishes like tacos, mustard fries, and quinoa salad. Manager was very attentive and accommodating. Reviews cite problems with service but on a Friday night, we had no issues. We eat tiramisu all over the world. This was the most flavorful ever. Would recommend highly. PS easy to get an Uber back to hotel/guest house.
One quiet and refreshing cafe in midst of buzzing Hauz Khas village. On top of ogaan, with some refreshing salads and yum keralite food options too. Ideal for a close chat
Great food but lousy service. Shape up if you want to see us again. Not service mindes at all. Many things to impove if you want to keep your stars in Tripadvisor.

The food was terrific, but on a weekend, please book a table and then go. Me and my son went without a reservation, so we had to finish our meal in 20 minutes flat before the next guest came. It was worth it. The price was a bit steep, but good food!
Very good flavors and authentic yummy Coastal food, must go there if you are in hauz khas, they have an amazing balcony seating.
Cheerful place with good food and even better drinks. Enjoyed my lunch with friends. The Cocktails were yum and food well prepared. Must do if u enjoy Coastal food.

On a whirlwind tour of India after 31 years away…. I mean, this used to be the stuff of my baaaaaaad dreams for many years: I am visiting India, somehow get stuck there and can’t leave, and my home, family and work in NY are all falling apart…. I’d wake up disoriented ~ relieved to be back in my bed on this side of the planet…!! …so ~ This was a quick sprint around the whole subcontinent, to catch up with everyone I really wanted to see... and with a refreshing degree of success !!!! Yay! Yikes: I don’t know about anyone else, but I don’t do these “after 31 years away” trips too often, and this one was a mindblower… Kolkata….? aaaaaah cwap !! I mean what’s with these name changes ? Can you really rename Calcutta, or any other place, and change history ? . .or what the people are ? At least they couldn’t (or didn’t ?) change Delhi’s name… And so… on my 3rd stop around my former homeland: much changed and much abused. Delhi has changed …But happy to find that in their hearts the folks are ALL the same... older, but undeniably and wonderfully, the same. These 31 years have NOT been kind to Delhi. The former PM’s vision of making Delhi the “Paris of The East” (anyone remember Indira Gandhi’s megalomania ?) which could possibly have been eked out of Lutyens’ vision for a “New” Delhi, has undeniably suffered a huge blow from rampant, exponential overdevelopment and overpopulation, and is now a teeming 18+ million deep mire of smog, building sites, concrete rubble and overgrown trees… all sadly covered in brown dust. The wealthy have become wealthier, and escape this gloom by disappearing into their cocooned, private developments where they are safely screened away from reality. But it’s not ALL bad ~ Hauz Khas Village did not exist in it’s current, “chic” iteration in my day. It was mostly a poor-man’s colony in the middle of a neglected forest with a stagnant lake, and surrounded by better neighborhoods where these residents found daily work. It slowly became a go-to place when local artists and craftsmen moved in because it was all they could afford. Today, in the evenings and late into the early mornings, this place is just cooking… people throng here to window-shop, admire art and crafts, eat and otherwise hang out and be “cool”. And there are over twenty restaurants, “clubs” and galleries that seem to be pretty darn popular, kinetic, chaotic and exhilarating. There’s a lot going down here. Now THAT’s development …..aaaaaaaaaah, magical, mystical India !! Coast Café occupies most of a structure that may have had other uses before becoming a foodie haven. The spaces are varied, bright, sunny, cheerfully decorated and open. The cuisine is mainly South Indian based, but with a fresh twist and sleight of spice, with infusions of international cuisines and influences all blended together in a very exciting mix. The menu offers finger food, burgers, tacos, salads, pastas and grilled foods ~ quite the international mix, with a Deccan influence. But, delightfully, of the three restaurants I went to during my trip, this was the only one that served alcoholic drinks. Thank Heaven !! Does anyone remember the days and nights of the “Emergency” in the ‘70s ? Delhi was “dry” as a bone, and we were forced to go to Faridabad and “smuggle” in illegal beer and rum from Haryana, the neighboring state, and some Delhi hotels served beer in teapots to very special, known guests, so no one would notice.. while all did, and did partake if they could. So we started with a glass of white wine, and one of Black Dog scotch whisky, which is bottled in India, that I used to drink back in the day ~ aaaah, memories… memories. Food was served fairly quickly, and with a smile: “Appam” – a south Indian pancake eaten with gravies, though it was somewhat thicker and less coconutty than it should be; “Beans Thoran” - very nicely done blend of just-so cooked beans, mustard, curry leaves and coconut; “Coast Café Prawn Moilee” - very tasty, sweet and spicy gravy, and really good with the Appam; “Mamabar Parantha” - the bread filler, with a southern spice mix in the batter, and finally “Nutella Buckwheat Crepe” – dessert… a throwback to our childhood sweet-tooth days. All in all: great value at about $20 each… amazing !! And they don’t tip very much either… 10% goes down very well. I wish we had explored some of the other places in this village.. there certainly seemed to be a lot of promise here.

We were here for dinner. The food was great, the Appams are excellent and the prawn dish was great. The fact that you are in an "affluent" part of Delhi you are expected to pay a little more than expected, but the food was of high standard so worth it. Go for a walk, and shop around first, then come here for dinner. We really enjoyed it!

We had an early dinner at Coast Cafe after walking around and doing some shopping in Hauz Khas and really enjoyed it. The place is very nice, with good light (we sat on the top floor by the window). Not much of a view (except for the peacocks on the roof of the building next door) but a quiet place with tasty food.  The prawn moilee was delicious - a nice break from the usual food we had for 2 weeks. The coconut milk made it sweet with the right amount of spice and we ordered extra rice to clean the sauce out of the bowl. The drinks were also very tasty and refreshing and service was attentive but unobstrusive.  Overall, a great, quiet place to take a break from the craziness of the city and enjoy tasty food and drinks.

The food is amazing here. My favourite are the prawn moilee with appam and the fish tacos. The salads are great too for the health conscious ones. The ambience is lovely with a nice terrace. Perfect for a lunch out with friends or family. End your meal with either the tiramisu or the banoffe pie. Sinful!
All the reviews didn't steer us wrong.  During our three days in Delhi, we ate here twice.  Amazing food!

Dined with a friend recently and must say, enjoyed the food much more than I had expected. The Kokam-Coconut mixed vegetable curry was delicious, the appams just perfect, and the malabar paratha was just awesome. Among the chutneys, the beetroot mustard chutney was very unusual (for me).  I also tried the Mutton Sukka - very spicy with fried curry leaves and coconut. That too was very good.  All in all - great food though not cheap!
Thumbs up: Ambience (soft white colors), Kerala cuisine (tried the eggplant gravy with appam), and polite service

As we wanted to have a small get together for our friends birthday , we decided to go to Pullman aerocity to try what they have in store for us.... suggested by a family friend... we went and after seeing the lobby, i couldn't keep my eyes off it.... its beautiful... went to the bar 'Pling' and the hospitality which I received from all the team members is overwhelming....I had few drinks and starters and was looking out for a place to dine. .. bartender Atul suggested us to try 'pluck' their European restaurant... without even giving a second thought, we went to pluck to try their unusal cuisine... on arrival, we got such a warm welcome from the lady restaurant manager Nidhi ... she has been of extreme support in choosing what we want to eat...sunil along with Nidhi took extra care of us and made us feel like home.... we enjoyed our meal throughly and let me tell ' it's a must visit ' for its employees and the food .... from the presentation to everything else...it's a wowwwwww.... your people are your assets I must say.... will definitely revisit this restaurant on my birthday....... a must try!!

Writing a review for the first time on this site about this restaurant named pluck. very near to the international airport one of my friend suggested to visit it as its new in the city.Found a totally new mood all together very modern and classy. Went with my fiance' to have breakfast after a long hour of flight thought it would be a little troublesome as it was our first visit to the place but no...  We found two very beautiful ladies Ms Rimi and Ms Naheeda ready at the entrance to greet us and it doesn't ends with the greetings, they help us find a very comfortable place and asked for our preferences, breakfast has never been so wonderful but these two ladies made our day.  Food is good with a nice spread to choose from.  Excellent hospitality will be back soon.

The food was excellent. We have the aubergines, prawns and fried mushrooms with appans: fantastic. The place is nice, kind of modern artistic atmosphere and the service is very good. I like it a lot. The drinks were also very good: an strong mint lemonade.
Went for lunch with family and enjoyed each dish we ordered as it was different. Kerala Roast Chicken was something very different and the best. Would definitely visit again.

With posh ambience, good food, lovely desserts coast cafe is a must try at hkv... Located on the 2nd and 3rd floor above ogaan, this place has the best mall food in delhi.... Their preparation of classic dishes is great. You can enjoy the tacos and burgers if you're not in the mood for some kokum and malabari parantha....

Went for dinner twice in two weeks. I would still go back there for the Malabar cuisine. But the service can be slow and sloppy. One of our dishes came to us via another table, another was probably meant for a different table but we ate and paid for it anyway. The cocktails also seem pretty inconsistent between one day and the next and between the first order and the repeats. We were at first seated with total nonchalance under a dripping AC. I just hope the food stays good and the restaurant won't start taking its customers for granted.

Went to this cafe at the hauz khaz village. Skip the rest and go straight for the Kerala coast cuisine. Ordered the masala fish curry and the Kerala chicken curry with two rounds of appams. Good service and hot food. The filter coffee is served in cappuccino cups though good is not worth the price paid compared to saravana Bhavan or nearby navedyam. Good service.

Its a great location with delicious food. I had the grilled veggie tacos with a wonderful fresh salsa sauce. Sadly the service was appalling. There were at least 4 people behind the bar working and none of them came out to take our order, instead after sometime I had to go and order it myself. The food came quickly but when I asked for some butter the guy went off and never came back. The plates weren't cleared, nobody came to ask me how my meal had been and I had to go and find someone to get the bill. Such a shame. I wonder maybe if they are so reliant on a good reputation that they have stopped trying.
A lingering dinner with friends over delicious south Indian cuisine.  Lovely, relaxed, modern dining room and great variety on the menu. Hope to return!

My favourite restaurant in delhiLove every single thing on the menu specially the kerala grill chicken, aubergine curry with appams, Nutella crepe, basil Cucumber martini, spiced up burger and both quinoa salads
Coast cafe is located right above Ogaan in Hauz Khas Village. It boasts of wonderful coastal cuisines which are mouth watering

We came here for the first time and discovered a gem of a place with food and ambience and excellent service. Since we are towards the end of the trip, we decided to return. warmly recommended. Thanks! d

Set on a couple of floors above Ogaan at the very beginning of the Hauz Khas village shopping/culinary stretch (and elevator is a bonus), it has an eclectic menu, great mixed drinks and fresh, delicious food. Good for a break and a friendly chat.

Delicious food....great ambience unlike what you see in hauz Khas village...it's a silent cozy place and they serve delicious food! Their drinks are really nice and well done....we tried the ones with herbal infusion and it was really nice..

In the buzzing hauz khas, Coast Cafe offers an almost serene ambience with light music where indulging in conversations is amazing over their mix of various food offerings!! Try out their veg pasta, crepes (everytime) along with their coffee/hot chocolate.. Yes, the drinks can be better but then as the name goes, it is a cafe and a good one at that!! :)

Tried two curries with rice - Coast Cafe Vegetable Moilee and Malabari Cokum Mixed Veggie Curry. Both were extremely tasty and the quantities are large, so make sure you are hungry when you order them.

I decided to come here because I had never had the pleasure of trying South Indian food before. I was surprised to find not only amazing food, but also good service and a pleasant ambiance! When I asked a question about one of the menu items, the waiter immediately said that he'd ask the cook and returned within a few minutes. He gave detailed explanations and sincere recommendations.  From the (compared to Indian standards) short but definitely satisfactory menu, I ordered the Coast Cafe prawn moilee, an appam, and a malabar parantha. The moilee tasted incredible! Creamy and spicy, not stingy with the prawns. The appam came with three chutneys, and was delicious with the moilee. The parantha came last, and really was the star of the show. It was crunchy on the edges, soft and chewy on the inside, and very flaky, as all good paranthas should be. I ended up having this by itself instead of with the moilee, because the flavor was so good.  The cost was high-- about 800 Rs for my meal-- but I would say it was worth it. Overall, amazing experience! I would definitely return here if I am ever in Delhi again.

The menu is somewhat limited but what they do serve is done to a nice standard and the portions are very generous. One thing guys, a mojito should be served with crushed ice, not ice cubes. Other than that service was attentive with a nice balcony on the top floor for those wanting a view and a cheeky cig!

Coast cafe has excellent food. We tried the chicken stew and avial and it was truly well cooked and so flavourful. The ambience of the restaurant is also good.Certainly a place I would recommend and go back to. A very satisfying dining experience.
We ate a chicken curry and a veg curry, both very good 😊 Friendly staff! Well  worth a visit if in this area. A little bit pricey and the portions size  wasn't that large.

This cute little cafe was a pleasant surprise! When I was told about this cafe, I was only told that if you are ever visiting Hauz Khas, you have to try the hot chocolate they give in this cafe called Coast Cafe. Trust me, this recommendation didn't at all justify my experience. To sum up my experience, I turn a corner in Hauz Khas village to look for this cafe and find this cute, lil white building with a balcony to die for (I am a balcony person), I take the lift to the balcony floor (obviously) and the courteous staff directs me to a seat right next to the balcony. My menu was as vague as it could be! It included Grilled Veggies Tacos, Appam and Rainy day vegetable stew, all of which tasted like nothing I've ever had before. It was experimental and had unique flavors. But but but what I fell in love with was the thick Belgian Hot Chocolate!! It was love at first sip and there were fireworks too ;) Perfect density, perfect sweetness, perfect temperature! I had the perfect hot chocolate, had the perfect balcony view, all that was missing was some rain! Whether you are in love or you are heart broken, whether you are going on a date or going for a solo travel; this hot chocolate covers it all! PS: When you order your hot chocolate, don't forget to ask the bartender to keep it thick.

We visited early May 2018, at the end of 3 weeks in India. We had thus far mainly eaten in our safari resorts, but now had a few nights in Delhi B&B, and so ventured out to eat locally - an experience we found a bit daunting. The Hauz Khas village eateries we saw on our walk tended to be very noisy, live music places. We (2 couples and with recovering 'Delhi Belly') just wanted a nice quiet tasty 'safe' meal. We struck lucky with Coast Cafe. Tucked away down a side street and up a couple of flights of spiral stairs, was an oasis of clean, tasty, airy restaurant. Specialising in Southern Indian food (which was a nice change), the dishes were spicy, tasty and plentiful. I can recommend the Moilee.

Notwithstanding its location and potential for attracting a trendy, upmarket crowd, the Coast Cafe's characterization of a boutique destination for casual dining is a rather misleading one. I'd visited the place on a Thursday evening after work and walked in to what resembled a restaurant with a bunch of waiters spread across the floor. One was sitting by the stairwell listening to music, another was supposedly taking an order on the telephone, and a third was cleaning glasses at the bar. Neither felt necessary or important to welcome a patron or pay attention, least of all provide directions to a table for two people. Nonetheless, after making myself comfortable and waiting for a considerable amount of time to be catered to, I was compelled to call upon a waiter and request for a glass of water and a menu card. Both dishes (i.e., prawns in chili olive oil and a fish fry) arrived much after I drowned a glass of fresh orange and basil juice in spite of explicitly requesting the entire order to arrive together. As I was prepared to wrap up, leave, and pay the bill, one of the service staff requested me to climb a flight of stairs to the building's third floor to pay by debit card as "their machine was immovable". I would have been happy to leave a positive review with constructive feedback for the Coast Café to take note of and improve upon, but a brazen lack of any understanding of maintaining and upholding hospitality standards whatsoever would leave any customer with a lot to say albeit devoid of any confidence or trust in the management's ability to deliver.

My wife and I visited here earlier this week. It's a really lovely restaurant. The moilees and the appam were excellent. For the price paid (it's not a cheap restaurant) we would've expected the portions of prawns and vegetables to be much more generous though. The vegetable moilee in particular hardly had anything in it and this let-down an otherwise very tasty curry, which was a shame! More generous portions of vegetables and prawns and this review would've been five stars.  Make sure you try the gluten free chocolate cake - it was brilliant.  One of the best restaurants in Hauz Khas Village.. well worth a visit.
Went to Coast for my birthday and was delighted. We really enjoyed the food, the atmosphere was chilled and it was surprisingly cheap!

I am always a bit worried when visiting a "trendy" cafe or restaurant in South Delhi. So many of them are poor imitations of western eateries designed for the burgeoning Indian middle class. These usually disappoint. Coast wasn't was of these and certainly didn't dissapoint. Really excellent Kerelan cooking in Delhi! You must try the aubergine curry. The setting was also pleasant and the staff very good. While I was boring and had a beer my friends who had the special soft drinks said they were really outstanding. I strongly recommend this place for a change and a bit of a treat.

Like most restaurants in Haus Khas Village, this one too is vertically spacious. The decor is simple and gives a sense of being airy and bright. I loved it instantly. And the food just enhanced my experience. We went as a group of ex-colleagues meeting after ages so we were noisy but the staff were ok with it. We had the opportunity to order a variety of things as we were many of us, but we decided to focus on few. The mushroom pepper fry, sukha mutton and Quinoa salad were starters. Delicious is the word. And then came the main course. The starters had filled us but what was served, blew our minds and made place. Rainy Day Stew with grilled chicken, Mom's aubergine Cokum curry, vegetable coast cafe moilee with Appam and Parotha. The stew like this I have never had. And the aubergine was finger licking. All in all a great meal. If I have to ask them to improve something, it will be the filter coffee. Too milky. Nowhere near authentic. Keep it up, will come back soon.

My wife and I had a meal at Coast yesterday, and I have to say that the guys in the kitchen are masters of flavor. The Goan prawn curry was the best that I have had. Even better than anything that I had in Goa!! If you are looking for an experience that is above and beyond the usual fare in Delhi (which is a pretty high bar to reach in a city with such great food), go to Coast. The interiors of the restaurant are also top quality.

This is a very pretty restaurant. However, the service was lacking-- we had to ask multiple times for water and then they FORGOT to bring us our main courses. Instead of taking responsibility for it, they claimed that it was because there was to be a gap between the appetizers and the main entrees-- which was weird, as they brought our daughter her meai. We also found the vegetarian curries to be very salty and they did not have very many vegetables-- same for the eggplant curry in terms of the salt level. The appams were very good, though, although 60 rupees an appam seems quite high!
Very nice lemonades and quinoa salads, the Kerala veggies sides were wonderful and also the prawn curry

If you like coastal seafood and authentic malabari dishes then this is the right place in Delhi. It is located in Houz Khas village - you would need to park your car and walk a bit to get there. As the number of tables are limited and there is normally a crowd please give some time for waiting. While waiting you can enjoy drinks at the bar - they have a full bar. The fish and prawns were fresh and very well made. Malabari parathas were good. Even the vegetable cokum dish was nice. The mocktails of different juices very refreshing. And the biggest surprise was Tiramisu - in which liquer and rum was floating. Over all a very delightful experience. Highly recommended and the prices are reasonable and would not make a hole in your pocket. So if you like seafood - you would definitely enjoy your visit.

Situated in Hauz Khas this restaurant is on the second and third floors but the owners have thoughtfully provided a lift! The coast in the title is the Malabar Coast which provides a clue to the origin of the specialities. I would urge the first...

It was an experience I can never forget, Coast Cafe in Hauz Khas Village is serving the  most authentic cuisine from Kerala.We ordered Mom's Aubergine Cokum Curry with Appam and Malabar Parantha. Everything was super, the flavours were sumptuous and the food was as...

This place is great if your already missing abit of the western touch in food. Its a sheek restaurant and has a tasty tea menu and lunch and dinner. I would recommend it highly for a meal once in Delhi. Its situated on the second...

Located on thev3rd floor at the entrance of the Hauz Khas village this restaurant has all the Kerala delicacies to offer. This outlet dishes out the best Kerala cuisine along with some South American snacks. a pretty decent bar menu to quench your thirst. They...

In the madness of the Hauz Khas village is this cute little cafe - there is an elevator that takes you to the 3rd floor. Service is excellent, the food is great. We had the arugula orange salad as a starter - very fresh and...

Have been here earlier for dinner but thought id try it for lunch. They have a casual decor and a relaxed setting. The sandwich and in-house salads were outstanding for lunch options and the appam with egg white was simply great. Have tried the malabar...

If you like Kerala food then this is the place to go to. Here is what doesn't work for the place:1. Narrow entrance and a very small elevator. 2. The restaurant is not on the ground floor so you have to use the winding...

Situated on the 2nd and 3rd floor above Ogan, at Hauz Khas village, thank God there is an elevator, the Cafe has a nice buzz about it. Obviously popular, even on a Tuesday - table reservation recommended in advance - the place has a pleasant...

We had a simple lunch, nothing fancy, just moktails, tacos and burgers. The service was quick, the food not bad (just not a lot) and it was not expensive. It is located on the 3rd floor not far from the start of the village. It...

Coast cafe is located on the 2nd Floor above Ogaan Store in Hauz Khras Village. The cafe is very clean & comfortable offering a good varied menu. I just had a melted cheese sandwich with coffee for a snack which was tasty. I noticed other...
Hotel is just mazing and the NYC staff if so friendly and always ready to help.wonderfull restaurant...I really recommend to Rajneesh n sudhir. And manager Mr Ajay for wonderfull service.
Thanks you so much NYC and team, specially thanks to sudhir and Manager Ajay for great service.Thanks to rajneesh as well. We enjoyed buffet food.

Hotel was nice and certainly convenient  Liked free car service to airport  Room comfy and clean and large  Restaurant buffet both breakfast and dinner really super  Especially enjoyed helpful and knowledgeable wine service from suneet kumar

Me and my friends were there at NYC for dinner. We had buffet food was excellent from starters to main dishes and desserts all yummy.A big thanks to Sumit Kumar for his service he make us feel comfortable throughout the meal.
Great ambience of NYC, and specially thanks to Sudhir for great service, and manager ajay . thank you both of you for your great service. And good quality of food. Thanks to NYC chef.
limited options, stinking aroma & non co-operative staff. Better to stay in Marriot which is a much better value for money.

The NYC CAFE Radisson Blu plaza boasts of a vibrant decor...justifying its name...the whole ambience being so lively, lovely channel music playing, courteous staff, excellent food which has a large sumptuous menu...from papri chaat for starters to italian pasta, penne, indian and continental cuisine and not to be missed yummy desserts....if you are going on a sunday there's a treat for the kids...a lovely tatoo artist cum balloon artist sits near the lobby for their entertainment...NYC beats its cousin the kebab factory (in the same hotel) in terms of the wide menu....kebab lovers ofcourse will have a different choice...what say ya...

Since it's an international restaurant, in buffet breakfast I could see a lot of variety in Indian and international dishes. Live kitchen counters include pan cakes, Parathas, and Dosas. Besides those, there's a large variety. For The first time I finished masala Dosa, sambar, and all the there chutneys relishing every bit of it. Usually, either sambar or chutneys are not that good but here the complete set was awesome. Aloo paratha and mixed Parathas were fabulous. Loved both. I had beetroot juice, fresh and well made. These are excellent sides. The restaurant is vast. Now, the improvement side. The staff serving the guests is in plenty but somehow not very attentive. Like, I had a table away from kitchens and adjacent to the huge glass wall. To order coffee, juice, Dosa etc, I had to wait and call for the attention. When I went to the Dosa and Parathas counters myself, I told a staff there to send a bowl of fresh curd, he acknowledged, but that never came. Service can be a little better in my opinion.  Overall, cool place to enjoy breakfast.

NYC is all day long restaurant at Hotel Radisson Blu New Delhi Airport Plaza, serving multi cousins breakfast, lunch and dinner. It has a comfortable and reasonable sized sitting and a wide variety of food. The test of various vegetarian dishes tasted at breakfasts was acceptable, though not delicious. Nothing great to write about, but at the same time, if you are staying at Radisson Blu on working business trip, the place is suitable for breakfast.
This place is amazing. I remember i visited this place in February last year. Food, Ambience, Staff.. Hats off

Pluck is a large space within the New Delhi Novotel Aerocity building in which a very comprehensive buffet offering is laid out daily for breakfast, lunch and dinner. The food was always hot and much of it was being prepared freshly by chefs behind the service line, so there was never any dried out or unappetising food out for self-service. The buffet is made up predominantly of Indian dishes although there are many oriental and western offerings too at each meal. Service was good. We enjoyed masala tea after each meal which the waiters happily brewed freshly for us. The one aspect I found a little disconcerting was the volume of the music as in such a large space the sound was a bit off-putting. We look forward to returning to this hotel and restaurant in a week's time.

My husband and I stayed at the Radisson Blu Plaza for three nights, and ate at NYC the first night we were there. Rajneesh took such good care of us and ensured that everything went wonderfully. Food and service were both so good that we came back every day of our visit!
Food and drinks were good. Service wasexceptional. Sumit Kumar and Sheetal - thank you guys for the wonderful service.
The service was pleasant and appropriate. Worth mentioning is the wonderful waiter Sumit Kumar who integrates personality well in his way of waiting. All in all a pleasant experience.

The food is good, we had excellent service not too pushy but the waiter, sumit Kumar Deepak ravat, were there every time you needed him, and even gave complimentary desserts.  I'd recommend the ceaser salat

We welcomed 2020 with the New Years Bash at Radissdon Blue Plaza Delhi Airport. Had Dinner at NYC Cafe, the food spread was nice. Loved the dessert spread, multiple options to choose from, and all of them simply delish. Had a gala time. Also, Sumit Kumar & Deepak Rawat from the NYC Cafe catered to our requirements, and looked after our dinner so well. 5Star to you guys, kudos! Keep up the good work!
Delicious food with great hospitality. Thank you Sumit Kumar for great help for serving and arranging food for us.  Specially made Omlet and 3ways chat was amazing and staff is very cooperating.

The dining atmosphere was exactly what we needed after a long flight and overnight stay. Everything we ate was fresh and delicious, and we enjoyed the cooked to order omelettes and breads. The restaurant offers a wide variety of breakfast options for any palette, including western style and local choices, fruits, beverages, cereals, pastries, and hot and cold choices. The service was attentive and courteous. Tables are arranged to allow for conversation and peaceful dining when you are weary from traveling. The decor and presentation of foods adds to the attractive setting. I appreciated the helpful care of the staff, including our server Sudhir. When traveling through Delhi, the Radisson Blu Plaza Delhi Airport makes a good choice for rest and lodging, with care by the professional and courteous staff. I hope to return in the future.
It was an amazing experience over here. Best place probably to spend time with family. A big thumbs up to the staff, Sumit kumar and Sheetal, for being so kind and generous
Ajay Sudnir is an excelent professional in a very Good restaurant! I recomend strongly his work, bacause hi is very atentive and very kind!
Great food and excellent service.by sushi and ajay. Excellent hospitality. Food buffet was Nice. Thanks to rajneesh.
Excellent service and food we enjoyed lot at the time of lunch thank you so much for great recommendation in starters by sudhir capitan in nyc. Thanks to ajay manager in nyc.
Enjoyed my stay. Restaurant service was very good. Our waiter sudhir Vinod Ajay was an amazing person and waiter.
I had lunch at pluck....... Food was amazing and service was fantastic.Sanjay was fabulous and he made my day.... I would thanks to pullman... great experience

We had possibly the best continental food ever at this lovely restaurant. Great ambience, taste and the delicate flavours of the dinner stayed with us for long. The chef came out to ask us about the food and we thanked him profusely.

After hearing many rave reviews of Pullman's Sunday Brunch, the family got together last Sunday to give it a try and are we glad that we did! Loved the concept of an "Art Brunch"! Pluck had a perfect Sunday feel to it and the never-ending champagne and food left me happily stuffed till this morning! Not only was the food presented well but it tasted equally as good as it all looked. Chef Nitin's parmesan burnt garlic soup is definitely one of my favourite's as is the Quinoa Broccoli Cake! The dessert buffet was a big hit at our table, loved the mini jam and custard filled doughnuts, the spicy chocolate tart and the macaroons! Deifinitely coming back for another yummy leisurely brunch!
Amazing food thanks to Karan Bir Gulati and Chef Nitin Bajaj had a wonderful time and amazing experience with food . Great hospitality Keep up the good work.

Went to Pling as well as Pluck to celebrate an occasion with the family. Starting from the drinks to tapas to food, everything was amazing. I had read the mention of their mixologist Topesh in a few articles and I must say, his creations did not disappoint. The burgers were good, dal makhani served was one of the best I had in a long time. The hospitality extend by the staff was an excellent gesture. Big shout-out to Mr. Topesh, Atul and Ashutosh.
there are plenty of various food,local indian food and continental food,a lot of choices.Food is very fresh and good favour.
The ambience and decor is ok, The spread is quite minimal. We got shrimps only when asked for. Considering the price, they should serve better food.

We went here for Sunday brunch after this place was in top 5 in zomato list for Sunday brunch.. the ambience is fairly usual.. service is quick.. food quality is good.. the spread has everything you can ask for! Chaat counter to live sushi, live starters, Italian, Chinese, Indian.. awesome desserts and salads.. wholesome meal!

Radisson, Mahipalpur is one of the oldest and the finest 5 star property in Delhi-NCR, started back in 1998, it has a journey to tell, from a dull jaipur-delhi road to delhi-jaipur expressway. In Radisson there are a couple of property that are their from the inception like the great Kebab factory or the NYC-New York Cafe. Recently, I made a visit to NYC, during the time of lunch, though the cafe is open whole day, but during the lunch time they have a buffet system. On a regular day buffet costs for Rs. 1700++ and on weekends they have 3 variety. 1. 1800++ a regular buffet 2. 2000++ regular buffet with drinks(non alcoholic) 3. 2500++ regular buffet with alcoholic drinks. The day I was there, they had a sizzler festival going on. So we had a chance to have the NYC buffet and the sizzler as well. NYC, has a variety of cuisines to offer. There are in all 10 different cuisine served. With 1 cuisine per day. And they repeat the cycle in 11th day. On the day I visited they were serving the Spanish cuisine. In the while day, they have a cuisine theme along with that they serve the Indian theme food. They have a complete 5 course meal serving package, which includes from soups, salads, starters main course, desert, live kitchen. And many more. The star attraction is definitely the desert section, with a lot of varieties. The day I was present they were running a sizzler festival and I tried the chicken sizzler with was amazing. With small portion of rice and chilly chicken with good amount if raw veggies.. it was a delight to have. I would recommend if tiu have a sweet tooth then have the deserts after the starters, because their is so much food that they offer, that you will have to skip the desert part.

I was at NYC to try their lavish Sunday brunch buffet. If was worth getting up early on a Sunday morning because they didn’t disappoint. The grandeur was totally worth it. The place is classy and will leave you spellbound. The buffet area was divided into various section. From salads, cold cuts, live grill counter, pizza and pasta counter, oriental, Indian, continental, street food section, Indian desserts, western desserts, breads counter, pickle counter as well as a separate wine and cheese section they absolutely had everything. It was a complete food extravaganza. I began my food journey with Golgappa, loved the stuffing and it was served with tamarind as well as mint water both of which were really good. Choley Kulcha, loved the spicy choole preparation and the kulchas were quite good. Red Lentil & Bulgur Soup, I tried this soup with an assortment of breads. The soup was mild in flavour but delicious nonetheless. The breads were quite average.  Lamb Mansaf, this Levantine dish is cooked in a sauce of fermented dried yogurt. The lamb was soft and the flavours were fantastic. Pan Seared Fish which was incorporated with lime, olive, asparagus and herbs. The fish was perfectly cooked and the sauce was delicious. Veg Noodles, well cooked and had the right amount of seasoning. Mix Vegetable in Hot Garlic Sauce, the vegetables were beautifully cooked and the sauce had a red tinge to it.  Dal Makhani, had rich creamy texture and was spicy as well. Hing Aur Jeere Ki Dal, this was quite a normal dish beautifully executed. Chicken Biryani, I would have preferred for the flavours to be a bit more bold in this dish. Handi Gosht, the lamb was nicely cooked but the gravy was quite average. Suke Dhaniya Aur Lal Mirch Ki Machhi, this was without a doubt my favourite savoury dish. Adored this spicy fish preparation and is highly recommended. From the desserts section I tried, Mango Brulee, perfect dessert for summers. Light texture and delicious in taste. Strawberry Mousse, this looked pretty as a picture and was a layered dish. Sponge topped with mousse then topped with jelly. Fantastic combination and was truly toothsome. Chocolate Souffle, this had an extremely rich texture, only hard core chocolate lovers would like this.  Blueberry Panacotta, this has a lovely bobbly texture. The balance of sweetness was just right, highly recommended. Tiramisu, eye pleasing presentation but the coffee flavour could have been a bit more prominent. Chocochip Gelleto, this was the last dish that I tried. Suffice it to say it was the perfect way to end my meal.  Summing up, I had a really pleasant experience. This place will totally leave you spoilt for choice. I only tried a few dishes but they have got a lot more than this to offer.
Continental Chef can make some fresh food to your taste and specification. Worth trying when you don't have time and appetite for buffet.

NYC Radisson Blu Plaza – Dine In Style. For Those With Large Appetite Food, they say, is a luxury in itself. But food at NYC Radisson Blu Plaza is a luxury most only dream of. NYC has a very hyped Sunday brunch that is served in the form of a lavish spread of food catering to every taste and culture. What is good to know is that the buffet at NYC stays true to this hype and the Sunday Brunch ends up being a memorable experience for all. As you enter through the main gate of the Radisson hotel, a walk towards the right and a slight blend onto the left will lead you to NYC. If you are on time, you will be welcomed by a grand display of food and it will only come as a pleasant surprise that you will gauging into this beautiful presentation for the large bucks you will be spending. Placed comfortably before the swimming pool and a few steps away from the tea longue and the Savannah Bar, NYC is a fine dining area most consumed by customers who are either the guests of the hotel or those who are here especially for the mighty ‘Sunday Brunch’ to be a part of probably the best buffet spread in the capital. As one settles down, it is better you make up your mind regarding what you really want to have. Or you can just have a bite each of everything and still leave with aching, over-filled stomachs. Salad : Like every other meal, NYC’s Sunday Brunch too begins with a pretty looking spread of Salads offering good options for both vegetarians and non-vegetarians. The orange and Walnut salad was a distinctive salad choice by the staff in the salad menu. For the meat lovers, the salad options are humongous. Prawns, Grilled Chicken and Roasted Lamb, all make their way into the Salad menu and taste should be the least of your concerns as it is exquisite in taste. Mezze Station : Not a fan of Mezze section but what’s the harm in tasting it all? But the pita breads were not too my liking though the hummus and kisir tasted just about fine. I was never a fan anyways. Platters : Platters section is a complete delight. The delicious smoked Salmon, tempting ham slices, pepperoni and the favorite of them all, the chicken mortadella. Platters section makes the non-vegetarian lovers happy. I am hungry once again. Chaat Station : Like we said, NYC has taken complete care of everyone’s tastes. So yes, you can have golgappas sitting in Radisson and its five star ambiance. Their chaat papri and dahi bhalla matched the street food standards but the Chole Kulcha stood out as the top performer from the chaat section. Mains : When it comes to a sumptuous meal at NYC, the main course is grand. You have more options than you can think of and we recommend you to take some time out to decide and settle on what you can have. You have curries from Asian to Indian, you have biryanis be it veg or non veg, the choice is humongous and the staff’s help to make a good choice will be beneficial to you. I personally had tried maximum of the curries available and was pretty pleased by most of them if not all of them. Live Kitchen : What’s more fun than ‘ordering’ food at a buffet place. Murgh Malai, Moroccan Prawns, Pizzas, Paneer Achari, the live station has it all with no compromise as far as taste is concerned. A heavy meal makes no sense without an equally wild attack of desserts on your sweet tooth. And when one speaks of desserts, NYC succeeds majorly. Desserts : The beautiful display of the desserts is enough to make you smile and that twinkle in the eye will make its way back when you are all set for the final attack. So yes, the desserts list alone can count to about 20 and trying and tasting each one of them is the best thing you will be doing in the day. We tried the Vermicelli Kheer, Tiramisu, Fruit Tart, Blueberry Panacotta, Chocolate Cupcake, Kesarai Rasmalai and much much more. It all came down to being a fantastic finisher to an immensely happy meal. Yes, NYC hits it hard on the taste buds and effortlessly had us all drooling over the experience. Be it the service, ambiance or the outrageously eye-pleasing display of the delicacies. You will not wanna miss this one. In terms of cost, Radisson asks you to pay for what it offers. The entire brunch costs 1800/- plus taxes and the meal with drinks costs you 2500/- plus taxes for one. Food - 4.5/5 Ambiance - 4.5/5 Service - 4.5/5 Value For Money - 4/5 Overall - 4.5/5 Cost For Two – 4000 to 5500 Note : NYC is that experience where you will be pampering yourself with a lifestyle that you have most desired. A must visit. The food is deliciously haunting. Reach with a large appetite.

Food and ambience was great .would recommend to visit it once to have a great experience , would love to visit this place as many times as I can  Rohit was great in greeting and assisting in every single thing ..it was great to have such a friendly and comfortable atmosphere

Visited this restaurant on weekend for dinner along with family. The location is superb - just opposite delhi airport, dot on NH 8 in Mahipalpur. Valet parking is available for the customers. Buffet spread was large enough with varieties to taste, eat and be satisfied, but service need to be improved - one instance, they served us soup which was not accompanied with sauces. we had to wait and then ask for the same. It is a popular joint as enough footfall was there and being week end people were pouring in groups/families, but one can have comparable or even better options elsewhere too. Other thing of satisfaction was that despite being inside Radisson Blue Plaza, it is not pricey.

Went for the Sunday brunch at Pluck and it was a refreshing experience. Right from the reservation process to the welcoming and escorting to the table was handled professionally. The options offered on the Menu was well explained by Mayank as it could be a bit tricky to get it right the first time from the menu card offered.  The Buffet had wide variety of choices in offer, Indian, Chinese, Modern European, Pan Asian etc.etc. They also have a roast section which offers two kind of roasts, sushi counter, live pasta etc...in all ample options for all kind of palette... Service was un obtrusive and attentive, a special word for Mayank and Syed who were ever attentive to our needs and offered seamless and clutter-less service without being overly intrusive, at the end of the day its the Human connection which bring you back to a place.  Just a few observations: - The Salmon offered as a main course in the Modern European section did not tasted and as a Salmon should ( it felt a bit chewy in the mouth), it could be with the quality of the fish or overheating in the pan. -The offering in the dessert section could be increased. - The kids section is quite far across the lobby from the restaurant specially if you are seated at the far end. - The live music is not clearly audible at the far end of the restaurant. - The view to what apparently was the pool will be improved when the landscaping is completed in the area, right now it is an eye sore in an otherwise beautiful place. In all a must visit place with friends and family with high quality food and service. Marissa is marshaling her troops well.

What a great find just a short distance from Delhi airport and a 15 mins metro ride from the centre of Delhi. Great food & drinks, special thanks to Sumit at Pling for educating us on Indian wines - give it a go, you won't be disappointed!

Passion marries intelligence - Nitin Bajaj is the head chef - the creator of contemporary Indian - degustation to die for. Farm to plate concept with many of their herbs & vegetables grown in their own garden. And it's delectable, flavours so piquant they unfold on the palate & burst open the taste buds! Here are just a few dishes - truffle & porcini cream whipped, with delicate baby sized greens; Indian street food with a Middle Eastern & English fusion chutney followed by palate cleanser of compressed melon & cucumber gazpacho...that's just to start... Deconstructed Mulligatawny soup - flavours of coconut with lentil puffed rice like you've never tasted before! Tradition & contemporary marry beautifully. And who would have thought that Indian wines could rival their western counterparts? Sommelier Kritin introduced us to wine appreciation Indian style with a stunning red Zampa Chene 2010 Grand Reserve. Juicy, oaky, full of berries that perfectly complemented the menu. A delightful surprise. In fact the evening at Pluck @ Pullman Aerocity was one of most wonderful guest dining experiences we have had in a very long time. Loved the personal attention by the chef, sommelier & cheeky but respectful service of our waiter Ajae combined with contagious passion for their crafts. Highly recommended if you are a foodie!

Visited for a quick light lunch post salon treatment at the Radisson Hotel. Ordered two grilled non-veg club sandwiches and milkshakes.  Two toasted (not grilled) sandwiches arrived along with lukewarm milkshakes....not ideal in the 44 degree Delhi heat. The chicken breast in the sandwiches was pretty cold & very hard...couldn't carve it with the cutlery leave only chew it without doing serious damage to our jaws! The bacon was full of fat & inedible.  We decided not to eat and called the waiter to explain why. He wasn't interested in hearing us out & simply got us the bill. We asked for a manager but the waiter said he wasn't available. After several minutes of getting nowhere, the waiter abruptly went off and fetched the befuddled chef!  He told us to explain what we didn't like to the chef. The poor chef looked completely discombobulated as I launched into explaining that we had ordered grilled not toasted sandwiches and whilst we were not really complaining about that, we were definitely unhappy with the stone cold, hard, jaw breaking sandwich contents. The chef just gave a small apologetic smile and walked away.  The waiter stood over us until we begrudgingly paid the bill. As he went to get the change, we clearly saw him discussing the matter with the Chinese hostess at the entrance to the restaurant as he was agitatedly gesturing towards our table and she looked towards us and gave a long, hard stare that was colder than the dead chicken breast on our plates!  We then spotted a suited man who appeared to be the manager. We called him over and explained what had happened. He showed great concern at what had happened and took swift action. Firstly, he brought the waiter over to us and commanded him to go fetch our uneaten plates. Having examined the sandwiches, he then told the waiter to give our money back & only charge us for the milkshakes. He then apologised profusely for the poor quality experienced and for the waiter's inadequate way of handling the situation.  The professionalism of the manager more than made up for everything experienced that afternoon and it is only due to this that we shall definitely return again.
We had dinner and breakfast and the prices to the menu was over priced. There was a service charge to dine in. We ended up checking out and booking with another holiday

Visited this place NYC at Radisson hotel at Mahipalpur with a few friends for a brunch on a Sunday afternoon and would say it was one of the best brunches I had. Their menu had so much varieties from fruits to street food from different states of India to a huge spread of main course including live cooking counters and desserts. I've always preferred having buffets over ala-carte as this gives me more choice top eat from a lot of variety at an affordable price. Things I really loved here were the chats, salads, Smoked Salmon, Pistachio Chicken Mortadela, Smoked Ham Slice, Pepperoni, Poached Prawn with cocktail Sauce, Grilled Chicken, Grilled Cajun Spiced Chicken,Banana Wrapped Fish, Keema Kalegi Dosa, Mushroom & Spinach Stuffed Chicken breast with Natural jus Pan seared Prawn with saffron dill cream Grilled lamb with Pommery Mustard, Sliced Chicken with Ginger, Garlic and pepper Sauce Braised Bean Curd with Bok Choy and Hot Garlic Sauce, Awadhi Gosht Biryani, Murgh Makhanwala and desserts like Jalebi Rabadi Ke saath, Gulab Jamun, Kalakand, Kesar Phirni, Kulfi with Falooda, New York Cheese Cake, Chocolate Mud Cake, Wild berry Yogurt mousse,Maple honey nut tart, Pineapple custard jalousie, Date and Apricot Almond Pudding,Fresh Mango Pavalova and Vanilla Crème Burlee with Caramelized Nut. They had a lot of vegetarian variety too which I didn't try I didn't have that much appetite. Overall it's a perfect place for a nice meal with friends and family.

Team ChicLifeByte was recently invited to Radisson Blu Plaza Delhi and it’s time to share my experience. We tried out the multi cuisine buffet at NYC which was laid out nicely on a big table in the centre with lots of lights & lamps hanging tall from the ceiling so that we can see each dish clearly. I was overwhelmed seeing the great variety on offer. The live kitchen counter was built in orange & black bricks in one corner where the chefs were cooking some amazing recipes. It was a rainy day & I could see trees & plants from the big glass door with natural light coming inside giving a clear view of nature. Now it was time to raid the exciting food items and we started with Baba Ganoush which was roasted eggplant & Tahina puree with some juicy red pomegranates, tomatoes, bell peppers & carrots. The eggplant tasted very nice & I was refreshed by the flavour and thought it was a great start to the meal. The Hummus was also flavourful with nice consistency and very pleasing to eat. The next item on my plate was Foul Brit Ziat which was basically green peas & chickpeas with braised onions & tomatoes. The vinegar onions gave out a nice flavour with chickpeas. The cherry tomatoes & the green olives tasted good & delicious in the recipe, very juicy & the taste of bell peppers made it all the more refreshing & tasty. I loved it! The next thing was Grilled bell peppers & babycorn with cilantro garlic vinaigrette (soft crisp bell peppers nicely cooked). I particularly liked the yellow & green bell peppers and the addition of baby corns to it made it a healthy recipe. Next was Bazinjan Mahchi which was Aubergine packed with garlic cilantro, cumin, pinenut & lemon juice. It was a good recipe & quite chilly. A must try for people with spicy cravings! Now it was time for the Roasted Pumpkin Soup which was one of the best soups I have ever had. It was served with sesame soupsticks which were very crispy & crunchy. The best part was that it wasn’t only tasty but healthy as well. The pumpkin scores well on the health meter. For the main course I had some great stuff. I started with Thai Green Curry Rice which were served with thai leaves. It was hot & spicy on the tongue. Next was Noodles with Pokchoy & Peppers. They were nice, tasty, hot, not really spicy & not really the regular noodles but thick ones. The Mixed Veg kung bao style had a sweet sour flavour. They were spicy saucy vegetables (bell peppers & corn) in gravy. Wok Fried Beans, corn & chestnuts with chilli bean sauce had Thai flavours along with soya sauce. It was sweet, crunchy, tangy & spicy at the same time. I also tried Ful Medames which was from the Lebanese cuisine. It was cooked brown kidney beans dressed with lemon juice, olive oil & garlic. Very nicely made, not overly cooked just tender & lovely. Everybody should try this coz it’s a Lebanese twist & spice to the desi Rajma. I also ordered the Falafel Wrap which was nice. It had a tikki made of vegetables mixed in masalas. Parsley & chickpeas patty tasted good. Now, it was time for Indian-my favourite cuisine. I started with Paneer Tikka (from the live counter) which was soft, nice and juicy served along with a salad of cabbage, onion, green chilli, tomatoes & lemon. Next was Malai Makai Pulao which had chunks of paneer and corn. The paneer was real fresh & soft and it tasted nice with the corn. Indian food is incomplete without Dal Makhani. The Dal we had there was perfect as it wasn’t very spicy and salty. Great to have with Pudina Paranthas which had soft layers and was nice & crisp. The other items which I had were Dal Tadka. Yellow Dal was very nicely cooked but it was a little spicy though. Palak Paneer had tender Soft Paneer pieces to bite, nicely done & great in taste. Aloo Dum Anari was great in taste, spicy but nice. Kalonji Waley Baingan were eggplant done differently. They didn’t only taste nice but is also a great source of energy and are healthy. What amazed & surprised me were the brinjal (baingan) recipe which I ate with all my heart giving a 10/10 on my health meter. One interesting thing that I spotted there was the Tandoori Phalon ki Chaat which had pineapple & apple cooked together in tandoori marination. Found them juicy & nicely cooked. Fruits were naturally sweet & gave a different taste. We also met the Executive Chef Sreenivasan who told us he loves to experiment with his food and loves the fact that Radisson gives him a free hand to invent & innovate in the kitchen. He runs his imagination and loves to execute various recipes using zucchini, eggplant, tomatoes, garlic, bell peppers, fresh herbs. A lovely team of 70 chefs serve the people sitting across different restaurants, banquets, bars and lounge housed here who are constantly looking at different options making new dishes with their experience in hospitality. When his recipes are liked this inspires him & motivates him to execute more & more. The chef specializes in Italian & Mediterranean and has an impeccable talent to fuse food stuffs together & substitute it with more masala. I mean spices & flavours of different worlds. His personal favourite is Spanish Paella Rice. To relish my sweet tooth, I chose my deserts from the infinite options available. First I had Chena Payesh which were very soft & tender balls like snow. The Seviyan was yummy, nice & enjoyable reminding me of the Id seviyan. Kalakand was quite sweet, nice & soft. I had little amount to taste the divine & pure desi khoya & malai. Kala Jamun was nice & fresh. It was good & the best part was that sugar content was not too high- right & perfect. Baklava, the authentic Indian Sweet was the best one. The filling gave a nice flavour & it was wrapped in sheets. When I bit it, it melted giving the Indian flavours like a mithai. It’s a must try here! Strawberry White Chocolate Parfait (eggless) was nice, tender & soft and had a fruity taste. I had a great time and was definitely a memorable day with hearty talk with the chef & the fond memories of the food, ambience & the service with the quest to discover more such multi cuisines in future.
We only tried the Western dishes this time.  The mushroom soup was one of the best we ever tasted.  Everything was fresh, crisp and tasty with lots of extra nice touches.  Responsive service.

Pluck at the Pullman was the venue of a Delhi Wine Club event. It was the best food ever !! Indian with a twist. Everything we ate was fabulous. The decor was great, the service was amazing and the whole experience was just divine. Will certainly come back here if i get a chance.

Pluck at Pullman hotel is opulent with a bar and a waiting area. On one side it has huge glass windows overlooking the garden area and on the other hand, there were buffet tables used for Sunday brunch and breakfast. We decided to go for fixed menus as there was 1 + 1 offer the day we dined at the restaurant. While we liked the European food but did not like the modern Indian version of the dishes that we tried- For example, Golgappa and Caviar Tzatziki Dip Dried Ginger Gastrique: The presentation was excellent. Golgappas were not fresh, caviar lacked the tanginess of the original golgappa water, and dahi was substituted with tzatziki. Paneer Lababdar Involtini Adraki Spinach: This dish lacked flavour, too greasy and was a bit chewy. On the other hand, all European dishes were excellent - Three Mushroom Risotto Truffle and Porcini Jus Aged Parmesan: Three mushroom risotto truffle was good, flavourful and texture of the rice was perfect. Pan Roasted Lamb Rack Mustard Praline Pomelo Threads. Caramelized Onion Spinach Puree: again Lamb rack was done well. All the desserts were also splendid.   Must try for people who love European Food.

We recently celebrated an occasion where the main guests were Jain vegetarian (no onion no garlic). Usually this is a challenge at many places, but one call to Head Chef Manish one day prior and he assured us of an extensive selection. Located at lobby level, the ambience of Pluck is pleasant with a long buffet table and a huge mirror at the end. Due to our dietary requirements, we went a la carte but it was no less than a buffet! With 4 Indian starters, 2 European starters, 5 Indian main course and 2 European main course dishes, we were spoilt for choice. A complimentary cake added to the celebrations. At the restaurant, Chef Manish and Chef Vishal took personal and exceptional care - this was hospitality at its best.  While all the preparations were enjoyed, the highlight of the meal was the Indian main course - we cant wait to go back. The buffet also looked very tempting with a wide selection. Once again, a very big thank you to Chefs Manish and Vishal for making the evening a big success.

Not sure about foreigners

For ages have been craving for a buffet which is perfect both in terms of variety as well as taste. Pluck at Pullman Aerocity has made earnest endeavour to provide an experience where variety, taste , hospitality , exoerimece and a value for money deal awaits one. The experience starts at the entry itself the smiling face of the staff and over all hospitality amazed me. The staff guided us to the seating of our liking and there after took us on to show the entire spread would like to compliment Abhishek and Mayank for the high standard of hospitality. One can even oot for the Alacarte menu but I choose the buffet. The spread consists of Asian, Europiene, Indian and Continental cuisines, along with an elaborate chat counter, salad and mezze spread as well. Started off with the Fresh veggie and noodle soup in a spicy broth which was refreshing and could taste the crunch of the veggies in this wonderful broth with chunks of juicy prawns and chicken chunks. Then I tried the Roast Pork Belly with Mapple butter glaze which again was simply yumm .Succulent pork belly with a crisp skin and the sweet Mapple butter glaze complimented it to perfection with the roasted veggies. Numerous live counter offering Fish, Chicken and Veg indian starters cooked fresh on grill were there opted for the Chicken and pineapple from the grill which was perfectly charred and the smokiness of the grill and sweetness of pineapple complimented well with the chicken and cat fish starters. With so much to eat from I tried a bit of Chinese and the Lal mass and Kukkad in Indian, along with dum biryani and raita. Tried a few drinks with Master Mixologist Abhishek, who suggested me to go for the Oak aged spiced Negroni, well the drink served like the traditional old fashioned is a must try , the smell.of oak and the spices just enhance the flavours and perfect drink after ages. The deserts also are equally good with the kiwi burfi being my favourite along with the caramel caramalised crepe served with artisian jaggery and hazelnut ice cream topped with whipped cream and Fresh exotique fruits. Over all simply loved the place will be visiting soon again.
