We ate at this in-house restaurant on several nights. We had no trouble finding something different and inviting each night. We love Indian food, but not when it is heavily spiced with chili. We stayed with dishes which the staff recommended as mild, but still found them at the limit of our 'heat' range. That aside, all meals we delicious, well presented and accompanied with all the little extras you expect from a good restaurant - right down to the finger bowls on completion of the meal. .  Finished in granite and marble throughout, the restaurant has a modern, elegant feel. On each night, a trio of local musicians added to the ambiance with traditional music.  Service was generally superb. Very attentive and polite. We never had the opportunity to fill our own glasses or serve out a little more rice or curry. There was a few variations to small issues such as towels on arrival, finger bowls and condiments from one night to the next - not sure why.  Prices are high by Indian standards, though not excessive. The exception was the wine - bottles prices started at around $50.  The only minor detractor occurred on one evening when the small children at one table were allowed to run noisily through the place without restraint. No great issue, but a little distracting.  We judge a restaurant primarily by the quality of the food and then by the service. The Dvar scored highly on both counts. If there were other 'safe' restaurants nearby, we probably would have tried them, but overall we were very happy to eat at the Dvar each of our stay.
Interiors and seating are upto the mark and serving time is also fair. I recommend it for business and formal dinners. I won’t recommend for family ones..

I and my wife had a dinner at this restaurant recently. The food was authentic Mughal cuisine. The service was excellent provided by ever smiling staff. The quality of food, decor,service was excellent. I would recommend the starters for non veg buffs to be platter of Kababs. The Main course of Cooker mein Kukad was also unique. The evening was memorable with wonderful rendition of ghazals which enhanced the mood of the evening. The quick service added to the enjoyment. I also liked the cocunut and jaggery icecream

We spent a couple of nights at the Raddison Blu Dwarka and dined one evening at Dvar. On entering the restaurant we noticed a number of other Indian diners, we felt this was a good sign that the food was at least authentic. I would describe the interior as contemporary Indian with an open view into the kitchen. There is plenty of space between tables so perfect for couples or those preferring a quiet dining experience. The menu is very extensive so we settled on one of the vegetarian set menus, which included a starter, main courses and desert. The dishes actually arrived in individual bowls on a round metal tray. Really very innovative idea - made it easy to mix and match the dishes and nothing gets cold. The dishes were beautifully cooked, we are not vegetarians but found the dishes very flavoursome and filling.   Service is attentive and the staff are ready to take your order according to how spicy or otherwise you would like your dishes cooked.  The bill including a couple mocktails and service charges totalled £40.00 UK sterling. Bearing in mind this is a 4 star hotel we felt this was good value. I would def recommend this restaurant for guests just wishing to dine in or others seeking a contemporary dining experience.

We visited for Valentine's Day dinner, which was priced at ~ 2500/- per pax. The food included two starters, paneer and another dish, both of which were well prepared. The main course included three vegetables, gnochhi and paneer, gatte ki sabzi and 1 more along with dal, breads and biryani. Dessert constituted of 4 varieties which were all great barring the brownie. The meal included unlimited imfl drinks and juices! The food was great barring a few aberrations. The vegetable gravies were all relatively sweet given that they were all almond based. The brownie was very hard and doughy and there were numerous service lapses since the place was jam packed. Given that you're paying top dollar, the service lapses are really not acceptable. Chef Manish made it a point to visit all tables and ask for specific feedback and all of the feedback provided was received well.

This is one of the best hotel near New Delhi airport. This is five star hotel with competitive room rates. Their moto is "Yes, We can". Hotel Manager Mr. Puneet Chaudhari is really excellent and helpful.  Food quality is very nice.
The best buffet in town . And if u love kebabs and sea food ! A must visit . Great hospitality by everyone especially the manager Deepak and his lovely team. Cheers!!

Great Selection of Sushi, Dimsums, Live Grills and Indian Cuisines with a fabulous spread of Tapestry and Unlimited Pour of Champagne ! Their Leg of Raan ( Lamb) is outstanding and a must have. The Desserts are great as well.

Brilliant food well cooked and presented. The staff were amazing Excellent service nothing was to much trouble. I had lamb which was cooked to perfection, my husband opted for roast cod. The sweets were to die for and very moorish

I was here earlier this month for my birthday lunch with my fiance, and we both have fallen in love with this place. The moment we entered, we were spellbound with the beauty and perfection of this exquisite fine-dine restaurant situated in Raddison Blue Airport, New Delhi. Beautiful interiors, scrumptious food and warm hospitality make this place just perfect. From their exquisite interiors to inevitably delicious food, this place is surely something you just can't miss to explore. I was here last Monday with my guy for my birthday lunch, and I totally loved it.  I'm a huge fan of seafood, and eating live cooked spicy lemon garlic shrimps is something I've always loved. A glass of sparkling white wine along with freshly cooked shrimps made my birthday lunch a lot more scrumptious. From the live starters we also loved chicken tikka and tandoori aloo ki chaat. If you planning to visit Nyc anytime soon, then you must try their exclusive live kitchen starters. They are amazingly delicious.  In the main course, my favourite amongst all was Harey Masale Ka Murgh, damn it was scrumptious. I haven't eaten something so delicious in long time. I'm a huge fan of white sauce pasta penne, and ordered the same. It was one of the finest cooked pastas I've tried so far in Delhi. Creamy, cheesey and of course authentic in taste.  I loved their chicken sausage soup with cabbage, onions and herbs. Also, their smoked salmon salad was mouth watering delicious. From their slew of extraordinary desserts, my personal favourite was blueberry mousse. Woah, I'm hungry again  My experience at NYC, Raddison Blue was pleasant, they made my 25th birthday a lot more special by organizing my birthday cake. It was truly a heart warming gesture. Thank you NYC for making my special day a lot more special.

All the staff at the executive lounge were friendly and helpful. We arrived late from the airport just before their closing time, but they did not rush us. The different times we used the lounge between November and December all the staff helped my husband and I with our idiosyncratic needs. One of the sous chef gave me his secret recipe for a delicious appetizer. The manager, Sahil Seth’ went out of his way to help my husband with some computer printer problems.
JW lounge is a nice bar in JW Marriott Hotel. It has a beautiful outside seating area by the side of a pool. We enjoyed having drinks in the evening in the bar.

Great people all around at this hotel. Always smiling and give you respect. Group working at the lounge is amazing and very helpful. They go above and beyond to make sure you are happy. Rohan, Aman and others I thank you guys very much. Stay safe and hope to see you soon.
the place is so good and clean and the food was yummy I like this place everyone should go for this place it's really good

We thoroughly enjoyed the meals at PLUCK. They had so many fresh and tasty options that everyone had something they enjoyed. The service was great. We will definitely come back.

I visited this place a few days back and I can definitely say that this a perfect place to have food with great staff and so many options in food . And a special thanks to Mayank Pandey who was the manager for his suggestion in food and his courtesy . Also Abhishek Pandey who suggested us amazing Cocktails.
Great food with polite staff. Manager Mr. Mayank was humble and helped me with some bill issues. Guys do visit there if you are around aerocity.,☺☺

Although posting it late but, it is worth it. Great place to have a fine dining experience. Thank you Mr. Mayank Pandey (manager) for taking me to the buffet tour. Loved the desserts. Staff is courteous and makes you feel special. Would like to visit there again.

Heaven for food lovers. Pluck is one of the best destination in the city for people who are looking for luxurious experience both in terms of food ambience and service.  For soup lovers Kashmiri chicken yakhni is a must have, if you are looking for a vegetarian option go with sweet & sour pumpkin chaaru.  In starters mutton pepper fry is something you really want to try.  In main course go with malai prawns and trilogy of lamb.  In desserts classic warm ghewar is really really amazing.  In beverages- Manhattan and whiskey sour were truly magical and were prepared by Mr Abhishek Pandey.  All these amazing dishes and cocktails Were recommended by our amazing host Mr Mayank Pandey. He and his staff are one of the best in terms of hospitality.

Service is always friendly and good conversation. I’ve always been treated with respect and will always recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Delhi. Jayant has become a recognized face to myself and me to him, due to my business trips here repeatedly. A+ service. Thank you
Thank you Debashish for the lovely hospitality. You always make me feel comfortable at The Lounge. I wish to see you soon there. I will be coming again on Wednesday. See you Take care

I have been to JW Lounge three times during my stay in Delhi and it has always been a wonderful experience. From the staff welcoming me at the doors to the waiters, like Jayant, I always very welcomed and well catered for here. It’s a nice peaceful place to grab a drink before a flight and such places maybe more challenging to find in Delhi :) I especially enjoyed the outside seating with a beautiful reflective pond, greenery and some interesting flame decorations. Would gladly return to this lounge.
War super! Unglaublich freundlich und flexibel! Danke!  Was fantastic, friendly and flexible with strange requests.  Thank you

We stopped by desperate for a beer after a long day trip to Jaipur, before refreshing for dinner. Excellent selection of beers, including a very tasty one from Bhutan. excelente serviço. cordial e atencioso.Sumit Kumar was excellent. Made my first day of the year dinner wonderful. Recommend the restaurant and his service very much.

Mr Sumit Kumar and Ashok both are really great and they are serving the food and the quality of the food great and its value for money ,we are really feeling the test of 5 star hotel in Delhi .
Amazing atmosphere and service by Deepak Kumar and Sumit Rawat. Brilliant service and ambience and a thrilling performance by Fakhri band
NYC restaurant was delicious. The stagg was great and very helpful with all of our dietary needs! We recommend it to anyone in Delhi. Thanks to Sumit Kumar and Mac for all they did!

Food is for survival, Food is for partying, food is for style.  Rarely food is an art of work.  Pluck is an experience in culinary delight.  This is an opportunity to feel that you are live on Masterchef show with one dish after another coming,,, in Very very Impressive presentation.  The taste is exceptional and so is the service.  The chefs interact and are clearly happy on what they created.  The staff is attentive and amazing.  If good food, service, ambience, and a lovely evening is on your mind.... Head straight, you won't come disappointed.  Topesh the assistant manager and Karanbir Singh the Assistant . Director F&B , walked miles and bent way back to see that our evening was perfect, and if you are to visit, you can expect the same royalty.

apparently the restaurant is known for plucking? Our muse Bouche came in the form of a rock with some lettuce on which we had to "pluck". Interesting I thought, I ordered the chicken and hoped it didn't come with feathers. Scallop starters were lovely, the mains, chicken with fig jam was lovely and roast cod with black lentils. The vegetables accompanying were lovely too. Amazing presentation of the food and tasted amazing. What let the place down was the music, at one point there was a "I love tequila song playing" and the lighting was really bright so although the food felt special the ambience was lacking? Small things in an overall lovely night.

My wife and I took our dinner here while staying at the fantastic Pullman Hotel for a short vacation. The style is very unique with a blend of Western and Indian cuisine presented in a variety of different and fun ways. They have a full course menu, as well as a large ala carte menu with a number of dinner options. The presentation of the various items was fun and something we haven't really tried before. The price for dinner and drinks for 2 people was very affordable considering the setting and quality of the restaurant and food. Dinner for 2 with drinks was around 3300 rupees for our selections, which was excellent considering the great service and quality of the food. Try this if you are in the area for business or vacation, it's a really unique experience and we thoroughly enjoyed the dinner and the value.
We had a very nice and great diner. We did not liked the aircon so much but they put them off for us.  Nice appetizers, very attentive staff. We enjoyed it a lot

The European restaurant is one of the best placed in the vicinity to have a perfect venue for breakfast and lunch. The breakfast buffet spread is one of the best and I am especially fan of the variety of flavoured honey on the spread. Just amazing. Service is ok, could be better but nothing to complain for sure. It has become a venue of choice off late for a business breakfast and more importantly when I have an opportunity to date my wife. Proximity to home makes it easy for us to dash there for a breakfast after dropping kids to school, spend some quality time and then carry on with the our own days. Lunch is a la carte european menu and I have not had better flavours anywhere else. The options for vegeterians are not plenty but whatever we had was very very well prepared and presented. Kudos to the chef. Keep it up guys.
Located in the lobby of the Pullman, this is a reasonably good restaurant, the service is good, the food is quire nice and the ambience is great
Mr mohd Hassan was extremely polite and prompt in the service. The choice of starters and appetiser was good. Desserts were excellent.

The attire and staff of restaurant is too good.

Prego has a great ambiance with nicely spaced furniture. I like the soothing environment there. Food I had Bel focaccia Asparagus which had bell peppers, zucchini cheese, rocolla leaves, cherry tomatoes, mozzarella cheese. These were stuffed in the base made of flour and yeast. The dish was healthy and fresh due to presence of such vegetables. The cheese was appropriately used. Then I had asparagus soup. It was lip smacking. I loved the colour, texture and taste of the soup. Presence of crispy pistachios made it more healthy and delectable. Soup was soothing on the stomach and satiated my taste buds. Then I had Gnocchi Di Ricotta E Ruclla Alla Sorrentina. It was irresistible and I relished it. It had crunchy vegetables, spaghetti Bronte pistachio pesto. I specially liked eggplant roasted thins. Dessert was millefoglie which was mouthwatering , perfect in sugar, soft, crunchy. It had crunchy puff pastry , custard and berry sauce. The combination was outstanding. It was light on stomach. Ambiance is marvellous and one who loves Italian food will love it for Chef Emiliano Di Stefano is back

I am quite regular at NYC for almost a decade and must complement them for the variety & quality they offer in their buffet. It is such a pleasure to be served by Dinesh & to eat fresh starters prepared by Chef Amit. Looking forward to more food times at NYC

My visit to this restaurant was for a business lunch , it was a unique experience of how difference in presentation and layout of meal brings in a super appeal to your dining experience.  The attention to detail is remarkable, right from the serving staff to the presentation of each and every dish..and not to forget the explosion of fresh flavors that you get (thanks to their farm to table philosophy) and their in-hotel farm. Pluck is known for its fusion cuisine so don’t be surprised if you are given a bowl of freshly plucked lettuce with garlic mayo It sets the tone for the rest of the meal- irreproachably fresh vegetables with delicate presentations and subtle sauces for taste. You also have a choice of Modern Indian menu, the meal pricing of their set meals is great value for money . If you are in Delhi a must try and be floored by the super dinning experience.

Tandoori, tikka, sheesh kebab, naan, chicken byriani and many other specialties are available. Service is good and rather fast. Good list of drinks available with local wines, at a price... Beer is of course available too. A good experience.

I would love to see you soon for the same experience. Please maintain the standard. Thank you! Good luck!

Came here for dinner, having stayed at the Novotel aerocity. The staff was incredibly firendly and courteous. Presentation of the food was first class, you could see there was serious design and effort behind it. One thing to improve is that the tablecloth and seat both had spots and stains that were not appetizing. Other than that, a great experience.

Sampled some awesome food at Pluck.  Chef Ashish Dhar’s search for new ways to innovate, improve, explore and delight his audiences was very evident in the dishes sampled.  His creations were pure in flavor and executed to sublime effect. Exceptional cuisine, wonderful experience over all, worth a special journey to come to Pluck.

Always enjoy breakfast at this restaurant in The Pullman Hotel at Aerocity. The Indian options are always tastes and well done and there is a nice selection of western breakfast and other cuisines. The staff are excellent too - helpful and responsive - particularly Binay who is always welcoming.

It was after nearly ten years that I was visiting Radisson Blu Plaza hotel for dinner with family and a friend. We wanted something that would appeal to the taste buds and be light yet. Bhopal Sharma, who was serving us that evening, was such a delightful person. He made great recommendations and made sure that we were comfortable. He took special care to get noodles prepared according to our six-year-old daughter's preference. Besides dining, my wife also picked up some pastries from their great selection. Overall, it turned out to be a great evening with good food, cosy atmosphere and warm service. I am sure to visit this hotel more frequently.

I had visited the restaurant with my family on the 16th March 2018 and would like to change the rating of the restaurant. First of all we were served with paper napkins and the cloth napkins were not put on the table. The table layout was not there only the mats and fork, knife and spoons were kept and the paper napkins were kept in the centre of the table. There were no quarter plates kept on the table. It looked as if we were sitting in a local colony Club. The wine served to us was not a sealed bottle and had signs of moisture going into it as there were crystals at the bottom of the bottle. It was definitely an old one and not fresh.We complained and were given a new one. The food was not as good as it used to be. The deserts in the buffet had a very very ordinary taste. I think that the main chef may have changed. We were in the restaurant for approx 2.5 hours and did not see the manager even once in the restaurant.The ambience of a 5 star restaurant was not there at all and it looked as if we were sitting in a very mediocre kind of place. I have been a Prestige Club card holder of the hotel for the last 7 years and have been seeing the quality of the restaurant and the service going down. Majority of the staff looked liked trainees and there was no one to guide them. I have been seeing the quality of the restaurant consistently dropping with each passing year. I request the General Manager of the hotel to guide the managerial staff of their duties and try to give the customer a 5 star experience.
A restaurant we visit very often but never disappoints.  Ashwani exhibited immaculate hospitality.Made sure we are completely taken care of.
Went there for a mtg & ordered a Chocolate Mud pastry. Very poor quality & tasted a few days old. Really not worth the prices.

My trips here are few and far between and yet she remembered me and welcomed me with a warm smile as soon as I stepped in the the lounge. As always, service is topnotch. I must mention a couple of the team by name as they happened to be the name I managed to get Anjali, Sumana, Josua, Rahul and Surnit. Would highly recommend

Well i would start with a thank you to AMIT and Deepanshu for taking special care while at this restaurant.. it was a messed up evening s I initially planned n booked my table at JW Marriott but it didnt go well for me as it was all crowded n had to wait for 20 minutes, hence i called all the big hotels but it was only Radisson Blue Plaza at Mahipalpur delhi has managed to offer me a table n no crowds... hence we immediately went to NYC Radisson n i must appreciate the efforts by the staff n the hotel is well decorated... awesome ambience n best part is 24x7 ready.... we were offered any table we wish to choose as it was not crowded unlike others... luckily my spoiled evening was saved by this hotel...actually i bought a diamond ring for my wife n i needed a cool n quite place to present her n this was a perfect place for us n the staff here r quite courteous n helpful....the food here was limited due to it was late nite after 12, we opted for butter chicken which was not upto d mark n the team along with the chef tried every opportunity to make us happy by offering to change the dish.. but we already had half of it so we let it go...but the service was excellent... we loved it.... what makes all this big hotels stand out is the service n ambience.... otherwise a good n tasty food can also b found in a regular restaurant..... hence i would give 4 out of 5 to NYC Radisson for making all the effort to keep customers happy ....just 1 point less for the food options being limited..although it was late nite n understandable... cheers to the hotel team .... it was a time well spent ...thanks for saving my evening..its a beautiful hotel though n s per the hotel staff there r more restaurants available on regular timings till 11:00pm so we would love to comeback n try again...

I spent more then two decades In Hotels Sales & Mktg and 15-20 years back it was a challenge to sell hotels rooms & F&B services even places like New friends col, which was considered little out of place, So during commonwealth when hotels were coming at all extreme ends of Delhi the general perception was who will go there. But to our surprise hotels of Gr. Noida, Gurgaon, East or North Delhi all are getting their share of business. Yesterday when we were invited by good friend and celebrity Chef Rakesh Sethi for a food review at their Indian speciality restaurant Dvar we were quite surprised to see the buzz. Hotel was pretty lively with lot of guests in all their F&B outlets in house as well as walkins. It was a mind blowing experience and has to be with the passion for innovations by the seasoned Celebrity Chef Rakesh Sethi who has been in this for over three decades now and was a pioneer in cookery shows in television and other Medias. Each and every dish was out of this world be it flavor, tastes, presentation and cooking style. Enjoyed the live Flambe`and some great food presentations and discussion with Chef Sethi for his passion for innovations and molecular gastronomy. Started with Papri Chaat, yum cute little papri and yoghurt sphere (molecular style) on top of it and a touch of sweet n tangy chutney , decorated with a fresh mint leave….which had set our food journey of the evening. Then came the tiny cute samosas, crisp and with a filling of lentil and urad dal shorba was poured on it. Really amazed how these little samosas were made to perfection and shorba was with a nice aroma. Saffron sorbet a speciality of Azerbijan was soothing chilled with a subtle flavor of saffron and nice pinkish colour. Makhmali Chicken Pindaliyan was delicious to the core served in a copper vessel with hot coal inside to keep in warm, had a stuffing of minced chicken and served with green chutney. Paneer tikka was soft and melt in mouth. Sabz ki kulfi was another signature dish served in small glass in stick with sugary n tangy syrup. Neebu ki Machhi was soft sole fried and delicious with nice yoghurt dip.  Jannat e Jameen was yet another great starter of Potato and Mushroom, the veges with walnut dust on top. Sabj ki Galouti and Mutton Galouti were yummiliciously delicious with small thin rotis to hold it and very neatly presented. On mains we tried sabj Kofta, paneer Navabi, Kukkar ka Kukkad and Nali Shahnai Gosht and Dal Makhni. Each was better then the best. In my recent travels, I have never met a more joyful, inventive bartender than Mayouk.  I live in NY, work in DC, and travel worldwide for my company — so I am happy with a solid pint or G&Ts in most spots. Where there are cocktails, even disappointing ones, I rejoice. So to be served by Mayouk in a place NOT known for any of those things, I was almost moved to tears.  Truly — there is so much to say about Mayouk and not nearly enough time to say it.  If you ask for a standard gunshot whiskey sour, he’ll urge you to taste the fresh squeezed lemon lime sour mix and upgrade it with organic egg white foam.  If you say your friend is done with wine and wants to switch to liquor, he will genius-ly remix your whisky sour with a port wine floater and make for a gorgeous couture cocktail.  And if you want an inventive salt-rimmed passionfruit-vodka highball with a sambuca back he will... demure. Ask if you’re sure . And then steer you to the perfect cocktail that you were ACTUALLY looking for, all from his sorcerous little notepad.  Mixology is hard to come by in Delhi. And at a hotel bar, my standards are usually woefully low. But Mayouk is a breath of fresh air and I was glad to give him all my money. Three cheers and bbravo. Can’t wait to see what’s next for him. We’ll be first in line.

NYC offers a fine dining experience by 24/7 and serves global cuisine featuring dishes from across the world in buffet breakfast, lunch, dinner and an à la carte menu. Every day is a new culinary walk as NYC changes its menu throughout the year.  It was a review invitation from the restaurant and frankly speaking, I’m a little skeptical about buffets because Ala Carte always wins and buffet doesn’t get prepared whole-heartedly. I was amazed to see the spread, it was elaborate and was served in attractive cutlery. I could see individual portions of salads, a Chaat Corner, Mezze Station, Indian food, a well-arranged wine cellar and what not!!!  I love to taste everything in buffets and my journey started with every Indian’s favorite fast food – Chaat. The CHAAT STATION had all the possible options - Pav Bhaji, Papadi Chaat, Dahi Bhalla, Golgappa, Bhel Puri, Khandvi, and Dhokla. Papadi Chaat, Dahi Bhalla, Bhel Puri and Dhokla were good. Khandvi was better than any sweet shop could offer, and Pav Bhaji was a perfect blend of veggies and spices, very yummy. I wish Golgappa’s water could be less salty.  The Salad Bar was decorated in individual portions and in platters. Individual Portions included varieties like Grilled Zucchini with Feta cheese, olives, & citrus salsa, Seared Fig with Blue cheese & walnut and Mustard Marinated Mushroom with Apple Slaw. All the salads were beautifully plated in small cutleries and were fresh. I’ve never tried such an amalgamation of veggies, fruits, dry-fruits, etc. You guys nailed it. In Platters, I tasted only Beetroot & Orange salad, it was a fantabulous combination.  Then I moved to the Mezze Station with a hell lot of expectation. Babaganoush, Tabbouleh, Hummus, Labneh, Muhammara, Marinated Olives, Pita, Whole Wheat Pita, Lavash, Sesame Lavash and Paprika and Cheese Lavash, was surprised with the menu. Everything tasted good, nothing extra-ordinary.  They have a Live Kitchen, where I tried Hariyali Paneer Tikka and Glass Noodle & Vegetable Spring Roll. Both were very subtle and were going well with the sauces.  After eating so much, had the guts to try their main course as well. In Asian, I really loved the Butter Garlic Pepper Fried Rice with Baised Pokchoi, Carrot and Snow Peas in Hot Garlic Sauce. In Indian, tried the whole main course, it was great except Mutter Mushroom and Paneer Khatta Pyaaz, which didn’t go with my palate. Jeera Aloo Anardana, Bharwaan Dahiwali Bhindi and Tomato Rice were surprising in flavors.  Being an Italian food lover, had the Veg Pizza and Arrabiata Pasta. Pizza was properly cooked and smelled lovely and appetizing. Pasta was a disappointment, didn’t like it at all.  Last but not the least, the desserts section was inviting and all the desserts were individually plated. They were looking like a miniature version of the cakes. The desserts were toothsome but for me the exceptions were Chocolate Walnut Pudding, Tiramisu in Chocolate Cup and Bakhlava. I was little dis-heartened when I tried the Suji Ka Halwa, it could have been cooked in more Ghee.  Allover, my episode was worth remembering, the hospitality blew my mind, the service was prompt, the staff was smartly dressed and informative, the cutlery was appealing and most important, the food made me happy.
Excellent service by NYC team Rohit Amit,Rishab, Sanjay chef Gaurav&Vishnu.keep it up cordial team all the Best.

We were three adults and we stayed at Radisson Blu Plaza for three nights and for two of those nights we ate dinner at NYC. We had breakfast here everyday. The breakfast buffet was good...the dosa counter was great (the sambhar need more pep though; it is thick and insipid) and the pork sausages are more like chicken frankfurters. Apart from that...top marks for the breakfast buffet. Good variety and excellent taste. Great service too with tea/coffee at the table. We probably troubled the chef the most at dinner because two of us were not good at handling spicy stuff (mirchi) and I was down with acid reflux on my last night (bummer). But really, really, really all thanks to our young attendant Mr Sumit (I cannot praise his smiling, and friendly (never familiar) attitude enough). He saw to it that the spice level remained at Zero and on the last night the paneer tikka simply swept the floor...though the ajwaini fish tikka gave it a close run. The spinach-corn made with ZERO chilli was a hit on both days and the Indian breads fresh, warm and soft. There is just one thing I did not like and that was the ambient level of music...it was more like noise and I could simply NOT tune it out.

We looked at various options at Najafgardh road, Dwarka and Gurgaon, from banquet halls to buffet restaurants to five star hotels. And finally settled for Dvar restaurant at Redisson Blu Dwarka. Be it ambience, the look-after, the food, live music; every thing was the way we wanted them to be. We were happy and we returned happier that evening from Dvar after the celebration. Went to this restaurant with my father and brother for a quiet dinner. Well, I was reading reviews about this restaurant on TripAdvisor and decided to call the restaurant. The number on TA got me connected straight to the Director of F&B, Mr. Gupta, who was very courteous and kind enough to take my reservation even when he wasn't at the hotel. He made sure that the restaurant manager, Jai called me up and confirmed by booking. As we reached there, we were greeted by Jai and the hostess. 3 virgin mojitos awaited us courtesy Mr. Gupta. We ordered for a Laal Maas, some Indian breads, a Dal Makhni and a chicken biryani along with a bottle of JC Chardonnay. The Laal Maas was not as spicy and red hot as it is usually supposed to be but was good if you considered the dish to be like an Indian mutton curry. The same feedback was shared with the chef of the restaurant as well when he came and met us at the table. Biryani and Dal were fine and so were the breads. Portion sizes were quite nice as well. Service and the staff were warm and courteous. Pity that it was a Monday and we couldn't enjoy the live music as their musicians are off on Monday. Maybe next time! All in all, a decent family restaurant.

I generally avoid hotel restaurants in favor of local favorites, but while on business in the Dwarka suburb of New Delhi and staying at the Radisson Blu there did not seem to be many other accessible options. So I tried the hotel's Dvar Restaurant. It was simply wonderful! Specializing in Northern Indian cuisine, the food was outstanding (especially the two "celebrity chef" signature dishes that I chose from several such choices on the menu). The service was superb, and the live Indian music fascinating. And it's clearly not just for visitors, since I was the only foreigner there that night. The rest of the venue was taken up by an Indian birthday party, whose guests enjoyed the food and music as much as I. Highly recommended!

We went here on our last night of our Indian tour and after eating in some expensive restaurants I have to say for value and service this was the best on our 10 day trip. Food was really nice and the live music was good. Shame me and the Mrs were so tired that we could not stay longer

Visited Dvar at Radisson Blue Dwarka along with my sister... we were in a mood for mughlai quisine... and let me tell you...we were not disappointed. Food was delicious and service staff was attentive and cheerful... and icing on the cake was the soulful live music... all in all an evening well spent.

We try out the Chef’s special menu that showcases the best of his genius. Paired with a red Merlot we started off with a Kesari Murgh Shorba and a Dal Shorba with desi samosas instead of croutons. The uniqueness of the two soups is the clarity of flavours. The starters include a Galauti Kebab with a sweet Varqi Parantha and a Creamy Afghani Chicken Tikka. Chef Sethi also whips up something contemporary for us. A Dahi Kebab with a Varqi Parantha beneath and some hummus on top. The sweetness from the Parantha along with the sourness of the yoghurt combines like a magic with the ground earthiness of the hummus. The Bharwan Aloo Tilnaaz are beautifully sweet with flavours of cottage cheese and dry nuts.

RADISSON BLU DWARKA…..Impressive infrastructure….is right on the main road, very easy to locate. Nicely constructed circular, open in the centre and covered on the top like a tomb…..magnificent view. The 200+ room hotel has two restaurants…DVAR, the Indian Specialty and RICE, with Chinese Cuisine, a Coffee Shop and a Bar and also a Disc, Shopping Arcade on ground floor. Met Mr Rakesh Sethi, the Celebrity Executive Chef. Such an expert, experienced person and so humble. His passion for food is clearly visible from the innovative food that we savored. We had the food at DVAR where they have live music all days except Sundays. Food here was quite innovative and was served in unique and different style of serving dishes. Presentation was again great. Started with the Chef’s signature dish…PAPRI CHAAT WITH YOGHURT…the molecular style yoghurt sphere on top of crisp papri with chutney…was yumm and presented nicely. TINY SAMOSA WITH LENTIL SHORBA…..very very tine samosa with perfect shape and dal filling, crisp with moong dal piping hot lentil shorba. SAFFRON SORBET…..specialty of Azerbijan, milk with saffron, this I found a bit sweet. MAKHMALI CHICKEN PINDLIYAN……roasted and stuffed with keema, were awesome. PANEER TIKKA….Very colorful with red and green dips and stuffed with cashews. SABZ KI KULFI….served in small glass , spicy veg kulfi with sugar syrup , so good to taste with great style of presentation. NEEMBU KI MACHCHI…..sole fish well marinated with lemony flavor was served with yoghurt dip, again served in fish shaped dish. JANNTE JAMEEN…..here potato, mushroom was marinated with yoghurt with walnut dust on top to give crunchy feeling . SUBJI KI GALOUTI & MUTTON GALUTI…..both melted in mouth , very soft and very tasty with perfect balance of spicy and very thin chapati base to hold it. Although we were full with all the above delicacies….but the Chef Rakesh Sethi and the team insisted to try out few more dishes from the mains. So came the COOKER with COOKER KA KUKKAD…..served in pressure cooker itself chicken in mouthwatering curry with keema added to the curry. DAL MAKHNI and SUBZ KOFTA , perfect….DAL MAKHNI was served in Handi and SUBZ KOFTA was served in thin long serving dish. NALLI SHEHNAI GOSHT……Lamb Shanks in gravy, well done in tasty gravy was presented in longish dish. PAN KULFI & GULUKAND CAKE…..delicious kulfi with ice crème, gulukand cake and very thin fried beetal leaves was eye catching also. KULFI ALSKA or SULAGTA HIMALAYA……kulfi with unique way of serving with flambé technique…….one can feel the burning pahad. Enjoyed the evening along with Chef Rakeh Sethi with warm hospitality, delicious food and nice chat with him, live music, our favourite old song and gazals.  Expertise of Exec Chef Rakesh Sethi is all set to take people to culinary journey from Kabul to Punjab with their special menu at DVAR……must visit !!

While staying at the Dwarka Radisson Blu, we had dinner twice here. We liked it so much the first time that we came back for a second. We went for the fixed menu. The variety of dishes served are practically a journey through north Indian cuisine. You don't have a choice, you get it all! I recommend that if you are a couple you take one vegetarian and one non-vegetarian (if your diet allows that), as the quantities are a lot! We washed it down with couple of Kingfisher Ultras. A simple lunch, made with pride and served with pride and eaten with pleasure. We were watching the chef making Pani Puri (I think its called) - light pastry balls stuffed with herbs, spices and fillings drizzled with a couple of sauces when he brought a half dozen to the table. What a treat. It perfectly rounded off our lunch and set us up for the long flight home. Thank you Shaquib, Aman and Leena for shortening our jouney. Full marks from me.

I took the family there for lunch. After we were seated, we were handed iPads with the menu on it. Nothing that stood out since some places have shifted to electronic menus.  However the moment we took it in our hands and started exploring, we figured this is unique. You click on a dish and they show you the rushes of how the dish is prepared in 10-15 seconds. After seeing a couple of videos, we were rapidly going from hungry to ravenous. But the curiosity got the better of us just a bit longer. So we lingered on with the iPad. It had buttons for ingredients, clicking which brought up key ingredients in each dish. There were more options to explore, like which wine would go well with that dish, how do you pronounce the name of the dish, etc. What I would call the engagement of all senses before you actually sample the food itself. We had two starters. One was a paneer with lime. The other was a chaat with feta cheese, served in an aloo ki tokri. Both had unique flavours and tasted incredible. Not a morsel was left. We also ordered some soup, which also had great texture and flavours. After a late Sunday breakfast, we were rapidly getting full but we of course had to sample the main course. So we ordered a vegetable biryani which is served with raita and mirchi ka salan. Though the raita was a bit sour, the combination worked out well for our palate. No meal is complete without desserts and we ordered a rasmalai with sorbet. It was quite well done. The rasmalai neither looked like nor tasted like the traditional fare. It was true fusion stuff. The true test of good food is you end up overeating and not a morsel is left. This meal qualified on both counts. All in all, we had a very memorable lunch.

I visited this place for the Mango menu. Pluck, modern day eatery at Lobby Level serves Modern European and Contemporary Indian flavours has introduced Mango special menu on the onset of the Indian summer amidst a flurry of flavours and colour. With subtle and beautifully done ambience in royal beige, this was one of the best fine dine experience i had. It is always very relaxing to dine in at some places very cosy and soothing, having a sublime touch making you go awe-inspiring in the very first visit. The menu at Pluck is decided by seasonal availability of produce. From Salads, appetisers and main course to cool, refreshing summer mocktails. I could never have imagined Mango being incorporated in such perfect manner in a 5 course meal. Mango Gazpacho Soup Served as a Gazpacho which is a cold soup had Avacado Cheddar Ice-cream and mint tagliatelle, soup was a complete delight. Tangy Mango Marinated Chicken Tikka Succulent and perfectly marinated chicken on spicy raw mango sauce topped up with some micro greens to give you a complete delightful experience on table. Quinoa and Mango Salad With lemon and ginger dressing this salad was a complete meal in itself. With the goodness of the superfood (Quinoa) and the distinctive sweetness of mangoes, this was balanced with the lemon and ginger dressing tossed well with other ingredients. Desserts  Cottage cheese Gnocchi over Kadhai Masala Risotto steps up to dress the main course to be followed by a sinful service of the Mango Parfait with coconut sorbet. With some spectacular ambience, the main entrance comes with a view of delight. And the pool of lip-smacking dishes, I am sure you won’t miss a chance to come back here again.

Apart from vast spread of buffet, they offer their signature dishes. Golgappa & Caviar served with tzatziki dip and dried ginger gastrique. Personal favourites are Lamb burger and Whiskey Prawns. Desserts – The list is endless! Love the Liqueur Chocolates, NY Cheesecake, desi ladoos, brownie, exotic fruit tarts. This is the best brunch I had till date! HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!

We had a lovely evening out Friday night at Pluck where they were serving an exotic Turkish buffet. Pluck was, to my delight, a well lit restaurant, so we weren't peering over menu cards in the dark. It was spacious and other diners were out of hearing range, so even sitting in the centre of the room, you didn't feel like you had to yell to have yourself heard over the din. The angled wall sized mirror at one end of the wall amplified the space even more, and gave us an interesting perspective of the dining hall. The food was a buffet spread, and we had a range of dishes to choose from. For starters there were these thin, crisped breads and pita which were to be had with at least twenty different dips, several salads and soups. For the main course, herb rice and chicken stuffed with rice reached the top of my favourites list; they were fantastically flavoursome. Besides there was spinach kebabs, seekh kebabs, meats of every kind and flavour - roasted and curried, mutton stew, steamed fish and so on. There was also an Indian food counter, complete with a choice of multiple papads (that my son tried one each of), and many, many pickles. The dessert counter was delightful, with pineapple cakes, berry cakes, chocolate truffles, papaya with clotted cream, malpuras, and of course the Baklava. The Baklava was to die for. The most fun thing was the servers, dressed in their red pyjamas, blue embroidered jackets over white tops, and blue topis with tassels. They were a jolly lot, smiling, laughing and serving with an obvious delight. A special call out to Binay, who served us so intuitively and with so much fervour, and made our evening extra special. On the way out we laughed as we watched the ice cream vendor have fun with the kids, tossing their icecream cones just out of reach and teasing them before finally letting them have their treats. All in all, a really great place to go. I shall look forward to more such food festivals at Pluck and would definitely like to treat more family and friends to the place. Thank you Pluck!

Located in Pullman Aerocity, Pluck is a modern day eatery that serves a plethora of contemporary Indian and Modern European cuisines. It is an avant-grade restaurant with an in-house farm that is commited to offer the freshest ingredients in a seasonal menu that is locally focused and globally artistic. The ambience is also really beautiful which is peacock themed and the seating here is very spacious and comfortable. I have been to a couple of Sunday brunches here and had a wonderful experience.  Recently i went here last week and got to know that they have launched their new summer meni so i tried dishes from that value only. This menu is mainly focused on using healthier grains like quinoa, barley, kodo millet, ragi, amaranth, in all the courses throuout the menu. Being a contemporary Indian restaurant, they have tried to mould regional Indian dishes to modernised version but kept their simplicity and authentic taste intact.  I tried a lot of dishes from their menu and genuinely loved the taste of all of them but the following dishes i loved the most and hence recommending:  ▪︎Sweet n Sour Pumpkin Chaaru Soup  ▪︎Charcoal Fumed Peri-Peri Chilli Paneer Tikka  ▪︎Nadru Kofta, Ground Mustard Yakhni  ▪︎Motichoor, Thandai Parfait

I would not come here again for breakfast ever, I came here in the first place because of the Thai restaurant which my family and I love thought to wait for my client and enjoy a nice breakfast but everything was a mess.

My first time in India Delhi to meet my fiancee and her sister from Bhutan for 6 days. But also time to see Delhi city and its surroundings. The hotel and its eating and lounging and bar establishment is conveniant located between airport and the busy city centre but both can be quickly reached without issue. The Hangar lounge and bar has an aeronautical theme showing pictures and items from flight history. You can have nice snacks and light meals and enjoy a nice cocktail or drink. Lalit serves you there very well and he is a relaxed person and anything you want he will help you and provide. It's a nice place to retreat too after a busy day at the Palika Bazar and Delhi zoo during the day. There is also an all day dining for lunch breakfast and dinner buffet and a la carte. Akash will help you make a good choice and will also help you when you want something different. All in all a very nice experience surrounding holiday Inn. I also enjoyed the fact that for a very reasonable price you can get a chauffeur for the whole day who shows you the city and its surroundings or anything else on request and helps you navigate the busy streets of Delhi. We choose Ramdev. A really nice chaufeur and city guide who also helps us from start to end at visiting monuments museums and good local shopping facilities and makes sure we are in time for our appointments at Embassy and visa office or airport. I now feel comfortable in Delhi while i was a little anxious in the beginning but not anymore. I will return next time. Thats for sure. Warm Greetings from Mr Franklin and Paro.

Visited this place with my friends and family and we all loved it.  Our servers Binay, vashy and pushpa all of them were very polite and helpful.  Chef came to meet us and he told us their menu and we thoroughly enjoyed the food he served to us. Starting with dahi bhalla which was like a sponge rasgulla was a delicious dish. Then we had caviar Golgappas which were so good.  Golgappas stuffed with yogurt, served with caviar and meetha panni was a treat to eat. We also had soups which were absolutely perfect.  Also for main we had chicken leg with mashed Potatoes, lamb curry with sour dough bread. The chicken leg was cooked to perfection, it was so soft and good. I loved it. Also the lamb curry was so rich in flavour and good in taste.  For desserts we had ghevar with rabri and dark chocolate mousse. Both the desserts were amazing.  Overall  I loved the place and food was great.  Definitely worth trying.  I would recommend this place.

Such a classy place it is! I was there for dinner with my family and I really enjoyed the food, ambience and service.  The service is top notch. Mr. Binay was the best of all, he knows everything and helped us alot in selecting food items and he was polite and friendly.  Food  Signature Dahi Bhalla - dahi bhalla with a texture of sponge rasgulla served with deep fried paan patta and yogurt. Highly recommended dish.  Caviar Golgappa- if you want to experience once in your lifetime then do try them out! Bhatti Murgh - oh man what a delicious dish it is! Do try it out. Perfectly cooked chicken and topped with coriander seeds. Perfectly spiced and cooked. Also we tried new Zealand fish with mashed Potatoes for our main course and it was absolutely amazing.  For desserts my top pick would be the dark chocolate mousse.  Also they have in house wine which is fantastic.  I had sparkling wine and it was good.  Overall  We had a wonderful experience over here and I would recommend it to all.  Cheers!

It was my 1st visit in Pluck for Brunch. it was Fathers day and there was offer for free brunch for fathers accompanied by children So we availed that. The cafe is spread out in large area and the buffet spread has more than 50 dishes and was spread section wise. As usual its tiring to get up every time for picking the food from counters. Generally in such Hotels starters are served on the table. Here also when I was waiting in the counter, I was told that it will be served on table which took considerable time and it causes break in the flow. more over the tables re too small for four. If you are eating something and your next order arrives there is hardly any space to keep the plate. They have done it for making space for more people. They have live grill section and which is best among all. The chef was grilling everything so nicely. But hardly any exotic fish, all very common one which is not expected. Sushis were extremely well done both veg non veg but very limited variety. Dimsum very good. Tawa mutton and Tawa paneer were good. They have pani puris, bhalla papri ok ok kind. Chole bhature good. Indian section they had common mains like dal makni, paneer, chicken in white curry. I did not try any of them. In Chinese noodles, rice, veggies were good nothing extra ordinary. But Kudos to the Continental section. From Brinjal to Pork Belly, each dishes were very well done. Jerk chicken, was too good. Live pasta section was very interesting. Ravioli pastas and canolini, both were cooked so well. They had beautifully decorated continental bread and dessert section which had varieties of interesting breads and pastries but frankly they were treat to the eyes not to your tongue. But thumps up to Indian sweets spread. All were too good. In fruit section they were making fresh fruit juices which were really good. Cut fruit bowl was disappointing. Had expected all the fruits in small quantities and I had specified for that also but there were only lots of safeda mangoes, peaches and pear. No pineapple, lichies, plum not even the melons. It was already 3 pm. But wanted to have hot beverages. However, the attendants were nice and served us tea. However, they did not have jasmine tea in store.  Any ways because it was a celebration day we enjoyed ourselves. But definitely, I found it overpriced as per the food they serve.

I am no one special, yet he and the NYC Team made me feel like a VIP. Dhanyavaad and as we say in Hawaii Mahalo Nui Loa.

Quoin- Literally means "Corner" in French. Well the management have maintained their cliche given the location of this place. Very rarely would you expect a bar poised in an airport/business hotel buzzing with customers. Already been here twice with two polar different experiences. During the day it portrays a serene (see their outside seating) yet contemporary bar. Surprisingly, come evening and its good enough to give a sports bar a run for its money. They have collaborations with football communities in New Delhi- different yet a breezy trendsetting concept for other bars in the vicinity. The food and service matches up to the venue. Their Thin Crust Pepperoni Pizzas and Lamb Chops are highly recommended. The staff are warm, hospitable and efficient. Pricing doesn't make you think twice before helping yourself again at the bar.  In all, it's a given that I shall be visiting Quoin in the coming month. Props to all in the management.

Me and my husband were in Delhi for a day and we reached really early in the morning so we decided to have some good breakfast, and while looking for places we came across Cafe Pluck.Upon reaching the cafe the staff was kind of confused because we were a little early to arrive and their breakfast wasn’t laid fully. After 20mins or so the spread was laid, and so we had a look and were convinced to go for the buffet. The restaurant is super pretty with a lovely view of the garden. They had some good cold pressed juices in variety of flavours and wheatgrass shots in test tubes. The show stealer for me was the milkshake section, the peanut butter milkshake was simply amazing. The almond croissant was delicious. The cheery compote to go with the pancakes was great. There was a super variety in continental and Indian spread, almost everything was on point. There was an impressive variety of honey infused with flowers and spices and some fruit compotes. Also this is one of the very few restaurants which had almond milk along with the other variety of milks, I was really happy and impressed as I consume almond milk and I don’t easily get it in other restaurants. In the confectionery section they can put better muffins in terms of taste and presentation, the tea cake and almond cake were tasting really stale tasting, I wish they hadn’t put something which wasn’t upto the mark with the restaurant food standards. Mr. kamal and the other restaurant staff who were serving us were very polite and friendly, we received good service from them all. The used plates and cutlery were replaced without reminders.The person at the entry of the restaurant to welcome the guests was smiling and welcoming the whole time. We are glad that we had breakfast here.

Visited pluck during the sunday brunch today and it was a very wonderful experience. The buffet had very lavish spread and the staffs were very polite and professional, really like the selection of wines and cocktails on the menu. Will cone back for another brunch... Cheers guys
Excellent staff .our special thanks to Nisha Mohit and vaghy who went extra mile to make us very comfortable. They were very very courteous and helping

Thinking of how to utilise a lazy Sunday, my friends and me decided to spend our Sunday afternoon at the highly spoken brunch of the city at restaurant Pluck - Pullman Aerocity. Warm welcome followed by a huge variety of buffet spread. Prefect combination of sweet and savouries to fit various taste buds. Specifically enjoyed the deliciously aromatic crepes and the amazing variations of dimsums and sushi stations, lamb stations, summer coolers and juice stations, much more to mention from the diverse options of the global cuisine buffet, Pluck offers. Not to forget the beverages, being a white rum lover I specially requested for a rum based cocktail and the manager of the restaurant- ARPIT DHAWAN did a phenomenal job at getting me a perfect cocktail to complete my meal. Individual attention to each guest and recognition is what makes Pluck a unique place to dine at. Spending our Sunday afternoon at Pluck was throuougly worth it. The overall experience made my Sunday fantastically awesome. Just perfect! Great service and super staff! Keep up the great work!
I visited Pluck restaurant at Pullman Aerocity after hearing highly of ambience and the food. Great service and prompt staff. Really enjoyed being here. Thanks Arpit Dhawan :)

I went to Pluck (Hotel Pullman) on a Friday night. We were a group of 7 adults and 1 infant. There weren’t many people in the restaurant when we reached around 10:20PM and very soon the ones that were there also left. So eventually it was just all of us on one table and after that no one else came. Some person came to our table and introduced himself as Ashish and told us that he would take care of our table. The menus were in a tablet which seemed impressive. We were coming straight from the airport after our journey and were hungry and tired too. We quickly ordered 3 set menus, some food items and some drinks. The service from the very beginning was very slow. Apart from Ashish, we could see about 4-5 more waiters inside the restaurant; but when it came to servicing our table, it was only Ashish who was doing it and couldn’t do it effectively. We kept waiting for our order; we had to call him so many times to remind him to bring our order, particularly the next dish of our set menu. Looked like the chef also couldn’t manage. The tomato and coriander consommé (more commonly known as tomato dhaniya shorba in India) was a big disappointment! There were few pieces of tomato and coriander (or some greens) and he gave some sort of liquid to pour over it which was bland, watery and didn’t give the soup any taste. The organic quinoa broccoli cake that I got was burnt/over cooked and I left it. The pan roasted lamb Loin that was ordered for the second time was undercooked. Some of the food items that we got were decent in taste but the service level was so poor that the overall experience was very disappointing. We were in the restaurant for almost 2 hours, only waiting for our food to come, with only one person looking after the needs of 8 people! And in the end came the bill which was a bomb (Price for one person came out to approx INR3,000). It seemed an absurd figure, considering the extremely poor service that we received on a Friday night at a Fine Dining restaurant of a renowned hotel near the airport. I would not recommend this place to a family, especially if you are coming back from or going to the airport; you might just end up with a long wait!

The restaurant itself is beautiful with marble everywhere and giant bowls of spices as you enter, and a view of the kitchen which is always good. They had a really interesting band playing a mixture of different styles of Indian music, unlike anything else (and the staff who were Indian agreed!) which added a lot to the atmosphere, especially when it was quiet in the restaurant (20:00). The service was extremely attentive, bordering on too attentive, but that's what you expect from such a 5* hotel restaurant. The waiters were chatty and helpful in their recommendations. They unfortunately didn't have the drink I requested, despite it being on the menu, and offered me a cosmopolitan instead, which I didn't actually like. There was nothing they wouldn't arrange for us. The only flaw was that they specifically asked for us to complete this review *while* we were at the restaurant waiting for the puddings! We naturally didn't want to disrupt our dinner to go on our phones and write reviews. Another waiter also asked us to at the end after we'd paid, which by itself would have been ok. The tasting menu was around 1400 rupees (excluding taxes), and we took the meat and veg versions, and so it included at least 6 starters, and 8 mains and two puddings and offered refills. It was unfortunately more than we could eat, which was a shame as it was delicious, if not really spicy enough for my tastes. I wouldn't have been surprised if they'd done the standard thing of seeing Europeans and assuming they want it less hot. If that was the case, I'd recommend they ask the customer in future! Generally it was an excellent experience.

Chef Ajay Anand has curated a classic yet modern menu which captured the true essence of French cuisine.  It was divided into 4 courses and was paired with choicest wines which complimented the food well.  The meal started with amouse bouche which was pickled cod served with jalepeno cream, purple potato chips, blue cheese foam and honey caviar. It set the tone of what followed.  Starters included wild mushrooms, truffle parmesan choux and chicken liver, saw belly terrine.  The chicken liver was my favorite out of the two as it had melt in the mouth texture and came along with a celery marmalade, sour dough strings and berry glaze.  This course was paired with Maison Louis Latour (Chardonnay)  For mains they had choice between Baked 3 cheese, Artichoke, Sea Bass and Grenobloise.  The baked cheese dish blew our minds and was super delicious. It was perfectly balanced and had tangy cheese with mustard dill emulsion along. Yumm!  This course was served with Camas Pinot Noir.  Then came in the much awaited cheese platter. Now how can one do a French dinner without this!  It included Camembert and Gruyere which were accompanied by Kumquat Compote, white wine poach apricot,Chive cracker, Walnuts and pickled celery. We were amazed by the hospitality, quality and flavors. Kudos to the team for putting up a brilliant show.
