The restaurant curated by celebrity chef Rakesh Sethi is located on the first floor of the Radisson Blue,Dwarka. This and Rice- the Asian restaurant are a welcome break from the crowds of the multi- cuisine Spring. The food caters to traditional food from the various regions of India. They often have festivals celebrating schools of regional cuisine, which are not readily available. The live music adds to the ambience of the place.

Have been in Delhi for around 10 years and yet havent heard about this place. Found it through Tripadvisor App and located it in a back lane of SP Marg. Nice green surroundings, wooden motifs on the walls to represent the Malabar coast. Menu had a clear emphasis on Mangolorean/Karnataka coast cuisine. Clear indications of what is Gluten-Free, spicy etc all, which I always appreciate.  Food was served in metal plates with plantain leaves inlaid , which is a good touch anytime. The seafood Thali had the usual fried fish , fish in coconut gravy , prawn curry as bonus and one more fried fish ! Now that was a fish too many for me. The preparation was authentic , the rice pancakes or appams were done to perfection , vegetable stew in coconut milk was superb. Mind you, when the menu indicates 'spicy' , you got to take it seriously.  Need to come back here to try the rest of the dishes.

Sana-Di-Ge, truly, a hidden gem in Chanakyapuri. Sana-Di-Ge means a lamp, one which is used during prayer and is used sometimes during inauguration ceremonies. This place Sana-Di-Ge is really famous in Delhi NCR to serve best of the Manglorean food. I got to know about this place from my friend, so thought to visit it. The place itself is located in a very posh area of Delhi where most of the embassies are located. There is a good parking area and you will definitely find peaceful environment even outside the place. I came with my friends here, and we were welcomed gracefully here. The manager, Gaurav Pandey, himself showed us the way for table. All the staffs welcomed us with namaste. It was really a beautiful gesture that one can think of from such place. Now coming to the ambience, the place is decorated really well with the beautiful lantern lamps, antique frames and what not. Even the platings are done in a very royal way. All the plates that were placed in the table were made of brass alloy. There is a good seating area here, it's divided into 3 floors though. There is even a outside seating space (terrace) which again adds beauty to this place. I have never been a fan of South Indian food before. But after trying this place I had to change my mind. There are so many things to eat, that too a health wise perspective. Manglorean Sea food is what one can think of in this weather of Delhi. The food here is fresh, tasty and healthy. What else one can expect? In the starters I totally loved their Alambe Sukka,  Lotus stem Kali mirchi, Paneer Varuval, Kaju Sanadige, Koshambari salad, Pomfret Masala Fry, Anjal wrapped in banana leaf, prawn salad and kokum flavoured chicken. In the main course do try their Mangalore mutton curry, Kori (chicken) kundapuri, Kane (lady fish) kudla curry along with Kerala Appam, Mangalore Appam, Neer Dosa, Pundi and Kerala Paratha. In the dessert side go for their Elaneer Payasum. Each and every dish, here, is made using the ingredients from the South that they get imported from Mangalore. You will get to experience authentic flavour of each and every dish because of the spices. Also, do try their King Fish, its really tasty and is full of omega 3. This is the first ever place which I would say have focused both on taste side and health side of customers.  Not to forget, the white wine is something which I would recommend everyone to try from this place. I never had such flavour of wine before. Totally loved it. Also, in the drinks side, have their Elaneer Shunti Nimbehanu (coconut water flavoured with ginger, lemon n mint), Yedde Munchi Panaka (black pepper, fennel, tamarind n jaggery flavoured drink), and Sol Kadi (fresh kokum juice, coconut milk and tempered with spices). Further, in beverage side, filter coffee is a must here.  Overall, I had a good time here. With such service, food, music, I definitely felt that India is full of culture and diversity. Totally an amazing experience. Highly highly highly recommended.

Sana-di-ge is not merely a restaurant. It's a unique culture to experience. It's, in fact, a soul of Indian traditions in food. I find this place elite and exclusive. Especially, in coastal food, there is nothing like dining at Sana-di-ge. Every dish has a unique set of masalas and other ingredients. Where every masala and ingredients are sourced from the best of their origins keeping adherence to processes and procedures intact for sure. In case, a particular masala doesn't reach in time and is not available in the kitchen, the particular dish or dishes needing that masala will not be prepared with any substitutes procured from a local resource. That is the level of discipline to ensure extreme quality, taste, and genuinty in the dishes and food. That is a great level of commitment towards its customers and guests. Not only the food, but it's also the unique culture and experience that is more than delightful. It's a combination of luxury and rich experience. Let me highlight the key features of some classic and traditional drinks here that are prepared with utmost care and perfection. The first one is Sambharam. It is entirely different. Basically, it is all time Kerala's spicy buttermilk that is tampered with fresh coriander, turmeric, ginger, and curry leaves. Yedded Punchi Panaka is one of the best drinks. It is a perfect blend of black pepper, jaggery, water, and tamarind. So, actually, it is a sweet, spicy, and tangy drink. Punar Puli is made of kokum, water, and sugar. The word itself means kokum in the local language. Kokum is used traditionally in many south Indian curries as an alternative to tamarind. Kokum hails from the family of mango stream.  Sol Kadi is a fabulous drink. It is made with coconut milk, dried kokum fruit, spices, finally tempered with roasted garlic. Elaneeru Shunti Nimbehannu is another classic drink that is a combination of fresh tender coconut water, pressed ginger, and lime. After these fabulous drinks, let us relish some fabulous starters. Kori Ghee Roast is a pan tossed chicken with byagdi chili, clarified butter, and original spices from Mangalore. Butter Pepper Garlic Prawn is something I never miss whenever I visit here. It is uniquely prepared that makes its taste fabulous. It is batter fried prawn cooked with butter, pepper, and garlic. Malvani Mutton Masala was so delicious that I finished it in no time. It is mutton cooked with ginger, onion, and a unique set of the spice mix. In veg, Kalimirchi Lotus Stem is one of the best dishes. Lotus stem is deep fried and then tossed with black pepper, onion, and garlic. Now, comes the best of all. It is Kaju Sanadige, one of the signature dishes here. Whole cashew nuts are fried along with coconut slices that are further tossed with crushed pepper with a finishing touch with curry leaves. In the main course section, Malabar Stew is fantastic. It is a fresh mix of vegetables with coconut milk and curry leaves. Allepey Meen Manga Curry is a real treat for fish lovers like me. It is fish cooked with raw mango slices further cooked in coconut gravy. Kori Kundapuri is one of the best chicken dishes that has chicken cooked in cocnut gravy. Pundi goes well with Kori Kundapuri. Pundi, actually, is a steamed rice ball mixed with semolina (Rawa) and grated coconut. Neer Dosa, as the name suggests, is a very soft and ultra thin pan cake made with rice. When I visit and dine at Sana-di-ge I don't forget to order Kerala Paratha. If I don't order that, it will be a big mistake that I can't afford to make. Kerala Paratha is nothing but Malabar Paratha, a multilayer pan fried paratha made with flour and ghee. The same goes true for Mangalorean Appam that is a flat and soft pan cake made with fermented rice batter. It is also quite soft and thin. Nei Choru is a memorable rice dish made with rice tossed in pure ghee, cashew nuts, onion, and curry leaves. Among desserts, I preferred Elaneer Payasam and Ragi Mani. Ragi Mani is a finger millet pudding that is a superb delicacy from the Kannadiga heartland. The presentation of Elaneer Payasam is excellent. So is its taste. It is another signature dish at Sana-di-ge. It is made with fresh coconut milk and Tender coconut pulp. Ended this fabulous journey with Dakshin filter kaapi.

This place is one of a kind with it’s own authenticity, sophistication and an overall serene vibe. The name Sana-di-ge is a Tulu interpretation of the large brass lamp at the entrance. This place majors in serving Konkan coastal food and delights from Dakshin Kannada, Maharashtra and Kerala. Their hospitality is undeniably welcoming right from the entrance where you’re greeted the Indian way, to showing you your bay and providing a personalised service at your table, till you bid your good byes. To start with food, we had a complimentary basket of Rice Papad served with an assortment of chutneys. The papad was crispy and fresh, not too greasy and a tad bit spicy. I enjoyed it along with the chutneys. Then we tried a tester of 4 drinks from their menu: Elaneer Shunti Nimbehannu, Punar Puli, Kokam Kadi and Sambharam. While all of them had a good taste, my personal favourite was Punar Puli. The Nimbehannu was very refreshing with the taste of coconut water and a dash of lime and mint. The Kokam Kadi was earthly. The Sambharam was good, but a little bland. We moved on to the soups next. The Nuggekai Saaru was basically a drumstick lentil soup. Quite simple yet nice to have for a cold weather. The Kurumilagu Rasam was the real delight though. A tad bit of tang and an appropriate amount of spiciness added to the taste. One of the things about this place is that, if you aren’t familiar with the names of the food or delicacies, you can always reach out to the staff or the chef to suggest you what to order, based on your taste preferences.  We tasted several starters, but among my favourites were Ananas Ragi & Bija Shavige, and Kali Mirchi. The former is basically a salad with pineapple and nuts with honey and chilli flakes served on vermicelli. I’ve to admit it was kinda addictive. And so was the later, lotus stem tossed in pepper and garlic with curry leaves. The Kempu Bezule was a very sober and least spicy baby corn dish, could be proffered for kids or people with sensitive tastebuds. The Alambe Sukka on the other hand was a little too spicy Mushroom dish. I personally loved the combination of Neer Dosa with Ghee Roast. Ghee roast being on of their specialities, you shouldn’t miss out on this one. For main course, we had a Sanadige Basket consisting of Manglorean Appam, Moode, Pundi and Neer dosa. They also served us a Kerala Appam, which is not included in the basket but can be ordered separately. To accompany with it, we had Malabar Stew, Ulli Theeyal and Alambu Arepu. And not to forget some of Thengina Kai Anna. Both the appams were good, but I would personally recommend the Kerala appam over the Mangalorean appam. Pundi and Moode are both basically semolina cooked in different ways, which makes Pundi seem little heavier in terms of appetite. And Need dosa is a very thin rice dosa which has a subtle taste. Based on the spiciness of these you gotta choose what to pair with it. The stew has a lighter plainer taste to be combined with something that is a bit more saltier. While the Shallot Curry(Ulli Theeyal) and Mushroom Curry (Alambu Arepu) is to be paired with something that is plainer. You could also eat these curry with Thengina Kayi Anna which is basically sumptuous rice with the coconut, mustard seeds and curry leaves. The best part about all the food that they serve is that, the ingredients are all locally sourced and then shipped to the restaurant via air multiple times in a week. So you can literally taste the authenticity in their delicacies. Adding to the vibe of genuinity is all the crockery, which are designed in the old Indian ways giving the real luxury feels. Last, but not the least, we had the dessert. And this was like an elixir - peculiarly refreshing, very earthy and… I’m kinda running out of words to describe it. The Elaneer Payasam is basically tender coconut cream with coconut pulp, so good! I would strongly recommend this place. Family dinners, business lunches, fancy dates - this is a place you have to try for the occasions.

Talking about Asian, Thai and Chinese cuisine, Mamagoto has always been my favourite as there’s no better place to pamper our taste buds with the best and an amazing taste of food.  Mamagoto can never disappoint you when it comes to their food, service, staff and what not. From the well trained staff to the drink shakers at the bar to the most delectable table presentation, it is indeed a mesmerising experience! So i recently made a reservation with my friends for a quick dinner and decided to try some items from the newly launched Thai cuisine menu. I must say, this recent menu addition is totally worth a try! Ambience- 5/5 i have been to many other outlets of Mamagoto and this one surely is my personal favourite. The walls covered with beautiful art and contemporary additions to part will make you go WOW!  There’s a huge bar right as you enter with stacked up drinks on the shelf behind. The dramatic lighting adds up to the beauty of the place. There’s also a partly segregated seating which can either be used for small gatherings or even for usual purpose. Food-5/5 What we ordered- 8 treasure bowl in chicken- Chicken with vegetables in garlic edamame and celery served with veggie fried rice. The dish is moderately spicy. aromatic rice meal fish- basa fish in homemade aromatic chilli sauce served with jasmine fried rice and rocket salad. The nutty taste and aroma of rice adds up to the flavours of the dish. The spiced fish is balanced with the plain rice accompaniment. Chicken Unbelievabao- not a bowl but a superBowl worthy. The baos are stuffed with spiced crispy chicken. A very handy and a quick starter to have. Properly stuffed bao with chicken and veggies for a great taste  Hot China Action- slow poached chicken with schezuan chilli oil, served with ginger and scallion sticky fried rice. Being cooked in schezuan sauce, the gravy is spiced up and is best suited with sticky rice  Drinks- 5/5  Kiwi and mint collins - freshly muddled kiwi with mint and lime topped with soda. The drink was sweet-sour with chunks of kiwi in every sip with a dash of mint. This drink is a must try! Watermelon beach - fresh watermelon mixed with mint and lime to provide for a classic summer special and is a perfect drink to beat the heat and is most refreshing  passion fruit and orange cooler - a tangy, refreshing yet a luscious drink to enjoy.  Service- 5/5 A quick service on every table and a friendly and courteous staff.  Must place to go! STRONGLY RECOMMENDED!

Mamagoto has nice chinese food and other cuisines.  The food is delicious and home delivery is timely. Nevertheless, a recommended food junction

It's been more than a month and I still find myself thinking of this place and dreaming of the day I can return... If only it wasn't a 14-hour flight away! Prices are reasonable and the place compared to most is clean, we never struggled for seating either. It seems to be a hot spot for tourists so was nice to come across other travellers too. We liked it so much we went again twice on our travels.
Food was little boring as there was no flavour in it.. Preperation is not that much good, and needs some improvement in the foods. The food quality was poor..
Simple but very tasty. Nice peace and quiet amongst the chaos. We each had a paneer - one spice and one more creamy. Delicious naan. Doesn’t serve alcohol, but not a problem for us.

The rating on the tripadvisor is not true. Came in 10 hours me have proposed only European Breakfast, basic it is impossible order. Wi-Fi secret it can not be used. The waiter has no respect for the customers

Spent a week in Delhi recently and dined twice at this great restaurant.  Fabulous service, great food, wonderful ambience and decor.  My tuk tuk driver alerted me to this restaurant and I recommend for anyone visiting Delhi.  Great experience.
Fresh food. Amazing aura with a good gathering. Variety of mocktails and cocktails. Bit of music missing..but overall a great experience ;)
An excellent fine dinning restaurant, excellent food, excellent service, awasome ambiance,value for money. We enjoyed our evening throuly.

Had come to the restaurant for a party and House music was being played by a DJ who was excellent. DJ Aqeel also played House music but he was not that great. Music was excellent. The place is huge and clean. The food was not that great, had the chicken pizza and the sauce was on the sweeter side. Food could have been better. Will be back for the music.

Visited here for lunch with my husband and local tour guide on her recommendation. The food was excellent as was the service. The surrounding were clean and extremely comfortable. While we were there though several very large tourist groups cane in and it became a bit ‘hands up who wants.....’ which changed the whole ambiance. I was pleased we were ready to leave .
Good ambiance, good food, good service but they close too soon. They should go at least 24.00 hours.
i love this place and food is very good and biryani is excellent service style is also good. i think people should go there and test a food.
If you are here, you cannot take a chance to miss it's super delicious burger bomb!! It is it's one of a kind and is a must have!

I dined in this beautiful Restaurant on 03.11.1983 with my would be wife. After 37 YEAR on 01.11.2020, I got the opportunity to dine along with my wife and son. We remembered those unforgettable moments spent together 37 YEAR ago and shared with our son. This RESTAURANT is the choicest decorated. Food was very tasty. FULL MARKS. Staff is courteous. Service is PROMPT. I recommend a glimpse of the restaurant and dine in a comfortable environment. WITH GOOD WISHES.

The 1911 Restaurant in the 5 Star Imperial hotel is a trip back in time. Service is excellent with a staff of mostly older waitstaff who have certainly been there for a number of years. The food was tasty (a bit expensive but worth it) and the atmosphere made the entire experience enjoyable/

We stayed at Le Meridien New Delhi from 26th October for a week. It was a lifetime experience to have stayed there. We had checked in with an intention to stay for 2 days but the hospitality of the entire staff made us extend our stay to one week. Duty Manager Ms Latika took personal care to meet our requirements, Mr Pushkar, Mr Rahul and Mr Himanshu at The One, their only operating Restaurant made us feel special by serving us their best culinary specialties. Overall it was a wonderful experience staying there.

My husband and I used to visit Mamagoto very frequently till some years back; then we stopped (because we could never find space here at lunchtime on weekends). But the Sunday right after Republic Day, Select CityWalk mall seemed fairly empty and we could actually get a table (with a high chair for our little girl) in Mamagoto.  The décor here is deliberately kitschy East Asian, what with its neon signage, its larger-than-life mural of a woman’s face, its crisscross of multicoloured plastic drinking straws forming patterns on the wall. The menu, however, does show that the food holds centrestage: it covers a wide range of cuisines, all the way from stuff cooked on a robata grill, to Thai and Malaysian curries, stir-fries, a range of dimsums and appetisers, one-bowl dishes (these are among the most appealing, especially if you happen to be just two or two-and-a-quarter people, like us!). There are some mouthwatering desserts, and some refreshing mocktails, besides a fairly extensive range of alcoholic drinks.  We ordered a rock shrimp tempura to start with, followed by a smoked barbecue pork belly bowl (for my husband and I), and a stir-fried udon noodles (with chicken, though it’s also available with prawn or with vegetables) for our daughter, who wanted noodles. While we waited for our food to arrive, the waiter brought up a large bowlful of fried rice sticks, all crunchy and crisp, with two dipping sauces, one a fiery red chilli-based sauce and the other a sweet, slightly spicy one. This was a big hit with our daughter, who would gladly have made a meal out of them. (One plus point if you’re dining with children: Mamagoto is quite child-friendly. They have high chairs, they have trainer chopsticks, which our daughter loved, and our waiter was perceptive enough to let us know that the udon noodles would have a little spice in them, and to check whether that would be okay with our child).  Here’s the verdict: Rock shrimp tempura: Not exactly rock shrimp, but fairly large shrimp cut into smaller pieces. The tempura batter could have been fluffier, but it was still pretty light. Served with a lightly spiced mayonnaise dipping sauce. Delicious.  Stir-fried udon noodles with chicken: Included in the stir-fry were onions, spring onions, and pieces of bell pepper. Full of flavour, and only mildly spicy.  Smoked barbecue pork belly: Three skewers with four pieces each of barbecued pork belly. Served on a platter with a large helping of stir-fried rice, vegetables (broccoli, bak choy, snow peas, etc) on the side, a fried egg on top of the rice, and a little bowlful of extra barbecue sauce. I thought the pork wasn’t fatty enough, which was why it tended to be too dry and fibrous in places. But the flavour was perfect, the vegetables were beautifully cooked, and the portion size was good. We paid a total of Rs 2449 for our meal. Expensive, yes; but for the quantity we got, and the quality, I’d pay that.
love their fried rice bowls and thai curries. The waiter and managers are always extremely efficient, friendly, and polite. my favourite chain for getting casual asian anyday.

We were exploring the shopping centre next to our hotel and needed to eat dinner. We made a good choice as the share plates we ordered we very tasty. We were introduced to Sula wines, an Indian brand, as we always like to drink the local product and we were pleased with our Sauvignon Blanc. Give it a go and if you are in the area and hungry go to Mamagoto.

The food at khan market is much better and the ambience is much better than what we have in the mall ,service also is not that great ,the khan market service is definitely better.Might not want to go there again

We had excellent small plates of pork tacos and veg spring rolls , sauces were special, main course of kungpao chicken and Hakka noodles was great , good big portions lovely food better than few best!! Go from it
Simple menu with a lot of options. Tasty and with good service. I ordered vegi burger and it was very good.
Amongst the chaos of the main Bazar this place offers a decent Veggie Curry for an price that won't hurt your pocket.

What a great find!! I read some reviews & tried it for myself.  Ended up eating here 3days in a row it was so good! The staff at my hotel even eat there & they said it's the best local food around Paharganj. I loved the veg thali, Dahl tadka & garlic naan (which was the freshest/lightest naan I've ever had!

Took the advice of reviews on here and went for a wander. Was not disappointed, great little meal for a decent price and I had enough left over for an early morning breakfast (up at 5 to fly). Took the mixed veg curry with a garlic naan and butter roti. Not at all spicy (shame, but live and learn). And as someone else said, very good on the western gut. Clean little restaurant. 50/50 mix of westerners and Indians, always a good sign. Manager or owner (?) seemed attentive to ensure patrons were happy. I'd recommend a visit.

Fabulous experience, very tasty and yummy snacks, state of the art drinks, comfortable seatings, very huge space for partying and clubbing, very well designed. Would recommend all the corporates, party people, solo, family, friends to must visit there.

Connaught club is amonst the most new happening places in cp with live music and great space. They serve fresh brewed beer which is their usp, so for all the beer lovers this is the place to be. Large spacious beautiful ambience, perfect to do a party for good number of ppl. Staff is courteous and well mannered. Food was really really good in taste in terms of quality and quantity. Since it was a new place so we jumped straight to the main course and ordered Dal Makhni, Biryani Raita, Malai koft with breads. The quanity was sufficient for about 2-3 people and super fresh food. Defintly a must try place.

Clubhouse is a new entrant on the janpath road serving different yummy cuisines under one roof. They have indian, mexican, asian, korean, chinese, chaat all together. Ambience is bright with their live music adding on the charm. Soft music and really good singer. Huge space available. Staff is attentive and polite, helpful and well mannered They are one of the few places in cp serving fresh brewed beer, so incase u really want to have some fresh beer this is the place to be. We oredered Avocado Chaat must must try Tandoori Veg Platter everything was very yummy Dal Makhni, Palak Paneer Kofta, Garlic rotis for the main course. The food was lipsmacking nd really really tasty. Quality and the quantity was oustanding. Caramel custard for the dessert was to top it all. Had a great experience dining in here with good quality food music and ambience.

i visited spice market in saket select city. Completely cherished the feeling of the spot... completely cherished their introduction of nourishment styling.. with affection with there inviting beverages..  We had mint shikanji this mocktails was damn stunning and invigorating.. alongside this we ordered small vada pao all of a sudden after this I began missing mumbai vibes.. what's more,popcorn Panner was damn appealing and flavorful..  introduction was so adorable.. in desert we requested moong dal stogie which is extreme in taste..  Staff was so courteous and humble..

I visit spice market , Saket today with my friends . There desserts are to die for , totally in love with there desserts must try there moong Dal cigar  . Things I order -  Moong Dal cigar - 10/10 Parsi dairy kulfi - 8/10 Crunchy asparagus - 9/10  Overall my experience was good

I visit this amazing place in Saket today with my friends and trust me if you have never been there you have missed a amazing place in Delhi. I m totally in love with tere paneer popcorn and fish it's the best fish I have ever had . Must visit this place once . Amazing classy interior good food what else you need , this place is best option ether you want to go out with family or friends .

Spice market , Saket is just amazing place to go out for lunch and dinner or if you are looking for a place for fine dine this cute little place is just beautiful amazing classy interior with delicious food . Overall it's kind of perfect . Love there food amazing amritsari kulcha and Chole are to die for .

I have visited this place with some of my friends yesterday. They have lots of different and unique options. Ambience was okay but food was really good. The presentation was good. Service was also good. Staff was quick and friendly. Now coming to the food. I am going to give a brief description of everything i have tried there. 1) gondhoraj Gin based cocktail - was so refreshing and great in taste. 2) mini vada pav - this was really good. I personally love vada pav so this was a great option for me..If u are a vada pav lover then go for it 3) kolkata sour - were good. 4) lime soda - was super amazing. One of my favorite there. 5) paneer popcorn - was my favorites there, do try this one as you will not gonna disappoint. 6) kolkata fish kebabs - 5/5 this was the best thing i have ever tried. A must try option there. 7) moong daal cigar rolls - a must try option 8) parsi kulfi - was good. 9) veg biryani - not so good so I wouldn't recommend this one.  So overall I had a great experience there. I would recommend vada pav , kolkata sour and gondhoraj Gin for alcohol lovers, parsi kulfi and popcorn paneer in snacks also pindi chhole with amritsar naan for main course. Looking forward to visit again.

So i visited this place with my friends for a quick lunch, place is located near Select City Walk Mall Saket, place can accommodate around 20 people at a time, they have really pretty nice ambience. We started our lunch with Mini Vada Pav, pav was fresh and soft while the vada tasted good too, Kolkata Fish Kebabs was amazingly delicious so tender melted once put in mouth, For The Main course we ordered Pindi Chole With Amritsari Kulcha, and Gosht Dum Biryani Chole and Kulchas were star of the day, but Biryani left us dissatisfied really neend to work on Biryani, it tasted like simple pulav, During lunch we Enjoyed Our Gin and Old Monk based Cocktails, Gin Shots and Lime Soda all were really refreshing, After a meal who doesn't want to have desserts ? We had Parsi Dairy Kulfi this creamy kulfi is a must Try, and Moong Dal Cigar With Rabri Was okayish. I had great time here giving 5 star coz of their very quick service, ambience and few dishes which we loved, hoping as o mentioned they will improve where they are lacking

I have been to this place for the first time. Its the best in terms of taste, ambience, price to hangout with friends and family. Th songs ,dim lights everything is very good. I would love to recommend this place to all.

Big yellow door in satyaniketan is probably the best place in the locality for some thick shakes. I recently visited this place and had a great time here! I ordered - Butter chicken pizza - it came with a butter chicken dip and was nice in taste Chicken tikka pizza - loaded with chicken and cheese, i found this one better than the previous one! Butter chicken pasta - this was a nice dish. It was Cooked in butter chicken gravy and had minced chicken pieces! Must try! Smoked chicken pasta with mushrooms - this was a nice white sauce pasta and had amazing taste! The cream was thick and it was nicely cooked. Rocky road Shake - this was a nice thick shake with loads of chocolate! Must try this for some awesome experience. Chocolate brownie Shake - This was a nice shake too! It had pieces of brownie and was topped with chocolate sauce! *Recommended* The ambience of this place is great! The staff is very polite! Do try this place for some droolworthy food and you won't regret it! Food : 5/5 Ambience : 5/5 Service : 5/5

Big yellow door is one of the oldest restaurant we have in gtb nagar.And thier is no competeter of this restaurant.It has own space in everyone's heart.Burgers, pizzas, shakes, pasta etc everything is just awesome here.
I was disappointed with the nachos dip rest the food was great. I would seriously recommend the restaurant to all
I know this was the first cafe launched in GTB Nagar.A beautiful small cafe to have your amazing food.They serve the best food burgers are really nice.You will surely love their food.

I visited with my friends. I heard a lot about it but now got time to visit. It was worth waiting. We waited for around 20 mins but the delivery was fast.. food was also nice.. & ambience really nice..
Went their to celebrate my friend's birthday. We'd thier pizza and potato wedges . Pizza was very good the potato wedges could be better .🤗 Overall good experience 👍

I visited this place to try their famous chana bhautura. But this place amazed me. This place is very old from the time of Britishers and have such a royal interior, sophisticated crowd, vintage decor which will give an amazing vibe. I ordered Chana bhatura and like wise the place the serving was royal too. The place is very expensive, you can try the place to enjoy it's authenticity. #royalfood #chanabhatuta #foodieforlife

Had cocktails here one night during our Mercury escorted tour of North India. Had already eaten at our hotel but wished we had tried the food here instead. The history of the place is amazing and the atmosphere takes you right back in time

Today we visited Kwality restaurant at regal building it was a family dinner ,,we had booked the table for 8 o'clock and we were there by 8:30 to our surprise the table was ready for us and they waited for half an hour without releasing the table , we were welcome with folded hand by the staff ,what an excellent hospitality, order was taken in no time for the drinks and was served within few minutes although they were few members late who had joined us ,but none of the staff were making faces which I like the most the restaurant was full there was a waiting ,,we could feel that the service was coming through the heart excellent food good choice was offered by the boys I don't remember the name but each and everyone was keeping a good eye contact the gentleman GM Mr Ajit excellent host for us, on the whole excellent service and food one must go there value for money.

Was told by my mother that the kwality of my childhood was back! Freshly renovated and classic.  Took my daughter there for their famous chana bhaturas. What an amazing experience. Told my daughter about the old days and she was fascinated about the stories of our times spent here in our childhood.  Needless to say the food was fantastic. A trip to Delhi would go waste if you do not visit kwality.

This grand restaurant, located in the heart of Delhi in the Cannaught Place, on Parliament Street in the Regal Building, is a remarkable landmark for the famous of Delhi from time immemorial. You will find a sense of history everywhere in the restaurant. The food is good. Service is slow. Live Piano music soothes the mood.

We had a cracking meal at this Delhi stalwart. The wonderful staff advised us on what was good and we also selected some of the house specialities from the extensive menu. This is a traditional and atmospheric restaurant. It’s excellent value and we’ll definitely be back the next time we visit Delhi.

I wrote a review in March 2019.  The restaurant is still excellent.   I took my doctor and his lovely wife for dinner.  The numerous dishes we ordered were full of flavor and prepared wonderfully.   The chicken took a appetizer was excellent.
Breakfast here is first class, lunch good and dinner if you stray from Indian dishes a little wayward. Great service and not too expensive except liquor which is harmed by excessive tax.

Though costly, this is such a wonderful treat for any weary traveller looking to step into a different culture and speed of Delhi. The staff are professional, warm and provide top service. Likewise the food is extremely well prepared and there is a cornucopia of items to select from regardless of diet. Highly recommend that you save plenty of room for desert! And enjoy a stroll in the main floor after to enjoy the ambiance of the hotel.  Beautiful and unique taste of India!
Ate here during our stay at the Imperial. Lots of good choices, both Western and Indian. Very attentive service for a buffet.
We stayed at the Imperial and the brekfast in the 1911 was included. My review is only about the Decoration, surrounding and the vibe you get when you are in this historical location. Wonderful!
A good place to meet with friends. The food was good and the service even better. The place is expensive but if you have money to spend then this is an excellent place for lunch.

Having stayed here with my family 5 years ago, and having been back on annual trips to Delhi ever since, I once again took people to this restaurant for lunch today. It was a disaster. There was no view of the beautiful grounds as tents up against the verandah windows completely obscured the. Once again, one had to walk through a package holiday buffet but...that would all have been fine if the service had been as impeccable (or even OK) as one might have suspected. It was DIRE. Embarrassing. Terrible. The food was OK but certainly not outstanding but we had to wait so long to order, then check the order, then get it in staggered bits as clearly not all of it had gone through that we couldn’t possibly have enjoyed it.  As the host I was mortified. I strongly advise against taking any business meeting to this hotel. I was so disappointed on the hotel’s behalf as well as my own.
With the epitome of world-class architecture and international amenities, Le Meridien is one of the best staycation five-star hotels for you and your loved ones. So when are you visiting?

Celebrated my Birthday here alone... But I thought I will be alone but Thanks to these great men who assisted me with a great service - Nishakar, Rahul & Vikram... If you want to try delicious food buffet with different varieties... Highly recommended Latest Recipe

It is le meridan is a great choice for travelers in delhi, location is great all around you and peace. Most impressive is the interior of the hotel classic touch decor and rooms were fine too spacious and good facilities.  Hotel serve the food which was awesome in taste wonderful by staying and eating in le meridan.

Recently stayed for 2 nights, excellent room, very nice and colourful interior. Food is excellent and in-room dinning is so wonderful. Service is good. I do not know the reason. Overall all is good. Very near to city centre / shopping mall / Gate of India. Early morning walk out side is very convenient. One things... morning breakfast... superb.
